segments.p2-1-g22: This segment is an isolated abbreviation 'д.' presented as a single orthographic unit. In Russian editorial or list contexts 'д.' can be an abbreviated label; the exact expansion is not given here, so treat it as an unresolved abbreviation standing alone rather than a full word with inflection or tense. It functions as a nominal label in the line.
segments.p2-1-g23: This segment is a comma punctuation mark that separates items; it does not carry lexical content but shows continuation or a pause in the list.
segments.p2-1-g24: Here 'и' is the coordinating conjunction meaning 'and' linking elements; 'т.' is an abbreviation following it. Together (in similar contexts) this sequence typically signals a formula like 'и т.д.' ('and so on'), so the clause reads as an abbreviated continuation marker. Because 'т.' is an abbreviation its exact expansion is unclear from the line alone.
segments.p2-1-g25: As in p2-1-s7, this is a single abbreviated token 'д.' used by itself. It behaves as a clipped label or shorthand; without surrounding expansion its grammatical role is nominal/lexical and unspecified here.
segments.p2-1-g26: 'Из' is the preposition meaning 'from' or 'out of' and governs the following noun in the genitive; here it marks source or selection. 'поэзии' is the genitive singular of 'поэзия' ('poetry'), showing what is being taken 'from'. 'Н.' is an initial/abbreviated proper name (a single-letter abbreviation), functioning as the genitive of a name and referring to the owner/author of that poetry.
segments.p2-1-g1: "Когда" begins a subordinate temporal clause ('when'). "из мрака заблужденья" is a genitive phrase literally 'from the darkness of delusion' (заблужденья is an older spelling with the soft sign). "Горячим словом убеждения" is an instrumental phrase ('by/with a fervent word of persuasion') and functions adverbially to mark means. The main clause follows: "Я душу падшую извлек" — "Я" is the nominative subject, "душу падшую" is the accusative object (падшую agrees with душу; here 'fallen' in a moral sense), and "извлек" is the past-tense perfective verb 'drew out/took out', giving a completed action in the past.
segments.p2-1-g2: The single conjunction "И" ('And') links the preceding clause to what follows; the comma shows the sentence continues. It functions as a simple coordinator and carries no tense on its own.
segments.p2-1-g3: "вся полна глубокой муки" is a predicative phrase: "вся" (intensive) + short-form predicate "полна" meaning 'full' (here 'utterly/full of'), followed by the genitive "глубокой муки" ('of deep torment'). Word order places the intensifier before the predicate for emphasis; the whole phrase describes the subject (you) as being filled with torment.
segments.p2-1-g4: "Ты прокляла," is a finite past tense clause: "Ты" (you, nominative) + "прокляла" (feminine past, perfective) 'cursed' or 'condemned.' The comma indicates the object or modifying phrase follows; the verb is perfective, so it describes a completed action.
segments.p2-1-g5: "ломая руки" is a деепричастный оборот (adverbial participle/gerund phrase) meaning 'wringing/tearing your hands' and denotes an action simultaneous with "прокляла." The verb form "ломая" is present adverbial participle from "ломать" and governs the direct object "руки" (accusative plural).
segments.p2-1-g6: "Тебя опутавший порок;" is a noun phrase with a past active participle: "опутавший" (having entangled/ensnared) modifies "порок" (vice). Grammatically, "Тебя" (you, accusative) is the object of the participle, placed before it for emphasis; syntactically the whole phrase is the direct object of "прокляла" ('you cursed the vice that had ensnared you'). The semicolon closes the first periodic sentence unit.
segments.p2-1-g7: Another temporal clause introduced by "Когда" ('When'). "забывчивую совесть" is accusative ('the forgetful conscience') and is the object of the following verbal noun/participial construction. "Воспоминанием" is instrumental ('with recollection'), and "казня" here functions as an adverbial/participial verbal noun ('punishing' or 'inflicting punishment'), so the whole phrase reads 'when, punishing the forgetful conscience with recollection...'.
segments.p2-1-g8: "Ты мне передавала повесть Всего," is a past imperfective clause: "Ты" (you) subject, "мне" dative indirect object ('to me'), "передавала" imperfective past 'was telling/related' (stresses the ongoing or repeated character of telling), "повесть" here means 'account' or 'tale', and "Всего" is genitive ('of everything/the whole'). Word order is normal: verb after subject but before object for emphasis on the action.
segments.p2-1-g9: "что было до меня," is a relative clause modifying "повесть": "что" = 'what/that which', "было" past tense 'was', "до меня" ('before me' / 'prior to me') with до + genitive. Together: 'of what had been before me' or 'of what happened before I came.'
segments.p2-1-g10: "И вдруг," is a conjunction + adverb ('and suddenly,') signaling an abrupt change of action; the comma prepares for the following participial clause. It connects the remembered-telling episode to the sudden emotional reaction that follows.
segments.p2-1-g11: "закрыв лицо руками," is an adverbial participle phrase ('having covered [your] face with [your] hands'): "закрыв" is the perfective adverbial participle of "закрыть" indicating a completed action accompanying the main clause, "лицо" is the accusative object 'face', and "руками" is instrumental 'with hands.'
segments.p2-1-g12: "Стыдом и ужасом полна," is again a predicate construction without an explicit finite verb: the short-form adjective "полна" ('full') takes two instruments "стыдом и ужасом" ('with shame and horror'). It describes the subject's state (you were full of shame and horror).
segments.p2-1-g13: "Ты разрешилася слезами," contains the reflexive past verb "разрешилася" (archaic spelling with -ся) meaning 'you gave way' or 'allowed yourself' — here 'allowed yourself to be carried away by tears.' "слезами" is instrumental indicating means ('with tears'). The reflexive suffix marks the subject undergoing/allowing the action on herself.
segments.p2-1-g14: "Возмущена," is a short-form adjective (past participle used predicatively) meaning 'indignant' or 'outraged.' As a short form it expresses the instantaneous or resulting state of the subject (you were indignant). The comma separates this descriptive predicate from the following one.
segments.p2-1-g15: "потрясена." is another short-form participial adjective meaning 'shaken' or 'astonished.' Placed at the end, it gives a final, emphatic state: 'shaken.' Both short-form adjectives agree in gender/number with the implicit feminine subject "ты."
segments.p2-1-g18: "И" is the coordinating conjunction "and" linking this fragment to what precedes; "т." is an abbreviated, dotted form standing for the first word of the set phrase "так далее" (so "и т. д.") — the dot marks the truncation and the pair forms the fixed expression meaning "and so on/et cetera." Here the printer has split the usual abbreviation across segments.
segments.p2-1-g19: "д." is a dotted abbreviation for the second word of the phrase "и так далее" ("далее") and completes the conventional abbreviation "и т. д." meaning "and so on/et cetera." The dot indicates truncation; used alone here because the typesetting split the abbreviation.
segments.p2-1-g20: A comma serving as punctuation; it separates what comes before from the following coordinating phrase and indicates a brief pause or continuation.
segments.p2-1-g21: "и" is the coordinating conjunction "and"; "т." again is the dotted abbreviation for "так" as part of the fixed formula "и т. д." ("и так далее"). Together (with the comma) this reads as a conventional trailing phrase ", and so on."
segments.p2-1-g27: This is a single-letter initial with a period, functioning as an abbreviation (not the conjunction 'а' meaning 'but'). It stands alone as a heading/initial and carries no grammatical relation to a verb — it's typographical, indicating an author's initial or similar.
segments.p2-1-g28: The first item is a name/heading (Некрасова) presented as a standalone label. The time-clause uses the fixed phrase 'В то время' ('at that time'); 'мне было' is an impersonal past construction expressing age (dative pronoun + past tense of 'быть' + nominative age expression). 'Всего' here is an adverb meaning 'only/merely', and 'двадцать четыре года' is the numeral phrase giving the speaker's age.
segments.p2-1-g29: 'Жизнь моя' is the subject ('my life') with the possessive after the noun for emphasis; 'была' is the past tense copula linking to predicate adjectives. 'Уж и тогда' is an idiomatic intensifier meaning 'already even then' (уж = already/indeed; и = even), and 'угрюмая' is a predicative adjective agreeing in gender and number with 'жизнь' (feminine singular).
segments.p2-1-g30: This continues the predicate with coordinate adjectives. 'Беспорядочная' is another predicative adjective ('disorderly'); 'и' links adjectives; 'до одичалости' is a prepositional phrase where 'до' governs the genitive to indicate extent ('to the point of'), and 'одичалости' (genitive) means 'wildness' or 'savagery'; 'одинокая' is a final predicative adjective agreeing with the subject.
segments.p2-1-g31: The clause begins with a negative habitual past: 'Я ни с кем не водился' ('I associated with no one'), where 'ни с кем' is the emphatic negative pronoun phrase and 'водился' (imperfective past reflexive) indicates habitual association. 'И даже избегал говорить' uses 'избегал' (imperfective past) + infinitive 'говорить' to mean 'even avoided speaking'. The final phrase 'и все более и более забивался в свой угол' uses the repetitive intensifier 'всё более и более' ('more and more') with the reflexive verb 'забивался' ('shut/crept myself away') plus 'в свой угол' ('into my own corner'), where 'свой' refers back to the subject.
segments.p2-1-g90: А is a contrastive particle introducing the speaker's personal stance (here closer to 'as for' / 'but'). Я is the nominative subject 'I'. Бы is the conditional particle that combines with the past-tense verb to form the counterfactual/conditional mood (Я бы помирился = 'I would have reconciled myself'). Вполне is an adverb modifying the verb 'помирился' meaning 'completely/perfectly'. Помирился is the past masculine singular reflexive perfective verb 'to reconcile oneself' (here: 'be reconciled'). На уме is an idiomatic prepositional phrase ('on/in the mind') indicating a mental or internal reconciliation rather than an outward action.
segments.p2-1-g91: Даже так is an adverbial phrase meaning 'even so' / 'to such an extent' (даже = 'even', так = 'so/thus'); the comma signals that what follows is the result or consequence clause introduced by что.
segments.p2-1-g92: Что introduces the result/content clause ('that...'). Согласился бы is the past-form + бы conditional meaning 'would have agreed/consented' (subject 'I' is omitted but understood). Даже и is a doubled particle (даже + и) adding extra emphasis = 'even (indeed)'. На + accusative (на подлое выражение) marks what he would agree 'to' — 'на подлое выражение' = 'to a mean/base expression' (подлое modifies выражение).
segments.p2-1-g93: С тем только is an idiomatic concessive phrase meaning 'with the sole proviso' or 'only on the condition' (с = 'with', тем = 'that/this', только = 'only/merely'), preparing the restriction expressed by the following clause.
segments.p2-1-g94: Чтоб introduces a purposive/conditional subordinate clause ('so that' / 'provided that'). Лицо мое is the direct object ('my face'). Находили is 3rd-person plural past/imperfect form used here in an optative/impersonal sense ('people would find/regard'), so the structure is 'they would find my face ...' Note that Russian uses the verb находить + complement in the instrumental to mean 'to find/regard someone as something'. В то же время is an adverbial phrase 'at the same time' (то + же + время), ужасно is an intensifying adverb 'terribly/very', and умным is instrumental singular of умный ('clever/intelligent'), the predicative complement ('regard [me] as terribly clever').
segments.p2-1-g95: Всех наших канцелярских is a fronted object phrase meaning 'all our clerks/staff.' Канцелярских is an adjective used substantively (short for канцелярские служащие), and because the phrase refers to animate people it appears in the accusative plural form (which for animate masculine equals the genitive form). The pronoun я follows for emphasis (‘I’).
segments.p2-1-g96: Разумеется is a parenthetical/modal adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally,' inserted between the object and the verb to mark the remark as obvious or expected.
segments.p2-1-g97: Ненавидел is the past tense, imperfective indicative of ненавидеть ('to hate' / 'to detest'), masculine singular agreeing with the understood subject я; it conveys habitual or continuous hatred in the past.
segments.p2-1-g98: С первого до последнего is an idiomatic adverbial phrase 'from the first to the last (one),' using с ... до with ordinal forms in the genitive to indicate full extent or scope (i.e., every one of them).
segments.p2-1-g99: И всех презирал repeats the action with a coordinating и ('and'), where всех ('all/everyone') is again the animate object and презирал is the past imperfective of презирать ('to despise'), paralleling ненавидел.
segments.p2-1-g100: А вместе с тем как будто их и боялся is a contrasting clause introduced by а ('but/yet'); вместе с тем ('at the same time') is a set adverbial phrase, как будто ('as if') introduces the comparative/conditional sense, их is the object pronoun 'them,' and the particle и before боялся adds emphasis ('even'). Боялся is past imperfective of бояться ('to be afraid'), with the subject я omitted but understood.
segments.p2-1-g101: Случалось is an impersonal past/imperfect form of случаться meaning 'it happened' or 'there were times (when)...' and signals that what follows occurred occasionally rather than constantly.
segments.p2-1-g102: Что introduces the content clause ('that/when'), я is the subject, вдруг even emphasizes suddenness, даже ('even') adds surprise, ставил is the past imperfective of ставить ('to put/place') used idiomatically to mean 'rank/consider,' их is the object 'them,' выше is the comparative adverb 'higher/above,' and себя is the reflexive 'myself' — together 'I would suddenly even place them above myself.'
segments.p2-1-g131: The noun phrase 'Всякий порядочный человек нашего времени' is the subject: 'всякий' (every) + adjective 'порядочный' (decent) + noun 'человек' (person) with the genitive modifier 'нашего времени' (of our time). The copula 'есть' is the present-form verb 'to be' used for emphasis (Russian often omits it), followed by 'и должен быть' where 'должен' is a modal adjective meaning 'ought to / must' and takes the infinitive 'быть'. The two predicate nouns 'трус и раб' are in the nominative and form the compound nominal predicate: 'is and ought to be a coward and a slave.'
segments.p2-1-g132: 'Это' is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/it' serving as the subject. The nominal predicate is 'нормальное его состояние': 'нормальное' is an adjective in nominative neuter singular agreeing with 'состояние' (state/condition); 'его' is the possessive 'his' placed after the adjective (a permissible word order that slightly stresses 'нормальное'). Together they mean 'his normal condition.'
segments.p2-1-g133: 'В этом' is a prepositional phrase 'in this / about this' and functions as the complement of 'убеждён'. 'Я' is the subject and 'убеждён' is a short-form past participle used predicatively meaning 'convinced' (no auxiliary). 'Глубоко' is an adverb modifying 'убеждён', placed after it for emphasis: 'I am deeply convinced of this.'
segments.p2-1-g134: 'Он' is the subject. 'Так сделан' is a short passive construction ('сделан' from сделать) meaning 'made so / made that way.' The conjunction 'и' links the two predicative phrases. 'На то устроен' uses 'устроен' (short passive/participial form of устроить) with the prepositional phrase 'на то' ('for that / for that purpose'), so the second part means 'is constituted/arranged for that.' Both predicates describe his fixed constitution or disposition.
segments.p2-1-g68: The single pronoun subject in the nominative: a plain, first-person singular 'I' introducing the speaker as the agent of the following past actions.
segments.p2-1-g69: A parenthetical adverb meaning 'for example' that qualifies the whole statement; it does not change the sentence's case or tense but marks the remark as an instance.
segments.p2-1-g70: 'ненавидел' is the imperfective past tense, 1st person singular, meaning 'hated' or 'detested' (a continuing/habitual feeling); 'свое' is the reflexive possessive agreeing with the subject (my own); 'лицо' is the direct object 'face'.
segments.p2-1-g71: 'находил' is past tense used here in the sense 'I found/considered' (i.e., I judged); it links as a reporting verb introducing the following subordinate clause.
segments.p2-1-g72: 'что' introduces the subordinate clause; 'оно' is a neuter pronoun referring back to 'лицо' (face); 'гнусно' is a predicative adjective/adverb meaning 'vile' — used here as the complement 'it was vile'.
segments.p2-1-g73: Coordinating connector with emphasis: 'и' + 'даже' = 'and even'; 'подозревал' is past tense 'I suspected' (imperfective), continuing the list of his negative judgments about his face.
segments.p2-1-g74: 'что' again introduces the subordinate clause; 'в нем' (= 'in it/in him') uses the prepositional pronoun to refer to the face; 'есть' is the existential/present 'there is'; 'какое-то' (какое + то) is an indefinite 'some kind of'; 'подлое выражение' = 'a base/mean expression' (подлое modifying выражение).
segments.p2-1-g75: 'и потому' = 'and therefore/for that reason'; 'каждый раз' = 'each time/every time' — an adverbial phrase signaling repeated occasions on which the following action took place.
segments.p2-1-g76: 'являясь' is a gerund (деепричастие) meaning 'appearing/coming' or 'being present' and expresses an action simultaneous with the main verb; 'в должность' means 'to the post' / 'on duty' (here 'в' has the sense 'into/toward' with должность = position/post').
segments.p2-1-g77: 'мучительно' is an adverb 'agonizingly/painfully' modifying the verb; 'старался' is past imperfective 'I tried/endeavoured'; 'держать себя' = 'to keep/hold oneself'; 'как можно независимее' is a comparative construction meaning 'as independent as possible' (как можно + comparative form).
segments.p2-1-g78: 'чтоб' introduces purpose ('so that'); the clause uses past perfective plural 'заподозрили' impersonally to mean 'they would suspect' (a typical colloquial/use of past to express a potential action by unspecified people); 'меня' is the object 'me'; 'в подлости' is the government of 'подозревать/заподозрить' with 'в' + noun meaning 'suspect (someone) of meanness'.
segments.p2-1-g79: 'а' is a contrastive conjunction ('and/while/but'); 'лицом' is instrumental singular 'with (my) face' — indicating the means by which he sought to communicate; 'выражать' is the infinitive 'to express'; 'как можно более' is the intensifier 'as ... as possible'; 'благородства' is genitive 'nobility' (amount/quality to be expressed).
segments.p2-1-g80: "Пусть ... будет" is an optative/imperative-like construction used to express a wish or concession — literally "let it be." Here "уж" is an intensifying particle (roughly "at least/indeed") and "и" functions as a concessive/augmentative particle ("even/also") modifying "некрасивое лицо"; the adjective precedes the noun in normal Russian word order.
segments.p2-1-g81: A brief parenthetical reporting clause. "Думал" is the past imperfective of "думать" ("I thought/was thinking") used here to frame the inner remark; the subject "я" follows the verb for stylistic emphasis.
segments.p2-1-g82: "Но зато" is a conjunctive pivot meaning "but/on the other hand/at least" introducing the contrast. Again the optative "пусть будет" asks that "it be" so; "оно" is the neuter pronoun referring back to "лицо," and the adjective "благородное" agrees with it in gender and number.
segments.p2-1-g83: Two coordinated adjectives describing the face. "Выразительное" (expressive) is in the same neuter singular form as the previous adjective; the particle "и" here is the simple coordinating "and."
segments.p2-1-g84: Parenthetical adverbial "главное" meaning "most important/above all" used to introduce the chief quality the speaker cares about.
segments.p2-1-g85: "Чрезвычайно" is an adverb intensifying the following adjective; "умное" is neuter singular to agree with "оно/лицо," meaning "intelligent/very clever." Together they form the superlative idea "extraordinarily intelligent."
segments.p2-1-g86: "Но" introduces contrast. "Наверно" is a modal adverb ("probably/it seems") that here qualifies the speaker's certainty; "и" links the adverbial phrase to the adverb "страдальчески" ("sorrowfully/painfully"), which modifies the verb "знал" (past imperfective "knew") to show the manner of his knowledge.
segments.p2-1-g87: A subordinate clause introduced by "что" ("that"). "Всех этих совершенств" is genitive plural ("of all these perfections") — the thing that cannot be expressed. The construction is impersonal with the infinitive "не выразить" (perfective infinitive in negative, "cannot express/it is impossible to express"); the dative "мне" marks the experiencer/agent for whom it is impossible, and "моим лицом" is instrumental meaning "by/with my face."
segments.p2-1-g88: "Но" signals contrast. The phrase "что всего ужаснее" is an exclamative/introductory turn meaning "what is most terrible/what is worst of all" ("что" = "what" here, "всего" = "of all/in total," "ужаснее" is the comparative/superlative sense of "terrible").
segments.p2-1-g89: "Я находил" uses the imperfective past of "находить" meaning "I judged/found (to be)" — a verb of assessment. "Его" refers back to the face (object pronoun), and "положительно" is an intensifier ("positively/definitely/certainly") modifying the adjective "глупым" (instrumental/adjective form used predicatively here: "stupid").
segments.p2-1-g135: И is a simple connective ('and'/'but') introducing the clause; не negates the following temporal phrase. В настоящее время is a fixed adverbial time expression meaning 'at the present time' (в + accusative here functions as a time expression). The comma marks a pause before the causal/contrast clause that follows.
segments.p2-1-g136: От governs the genitive and here means 'because of' / 'on account of' (not merely physical origin). Каких-нибудь is an indefinite genitive plural ('some/any'), with -нибудь adding indefiniteness; там is a colloquial filler meaning roughly 'there/so-called' and slightly dismissive. Случайных обстоятельств is genitive plural ('random/accidental circumstances') and is the object of от.
segments.p2-1-g137: А is contrastive ('but' / 'rather'), вводящее a generalizing statement. Вообще = 'in general'; во все времена ('во' is the allomorph of 'в' used before 'все') uses accusative plural to indicate duration/throughout ('at all times'). Порядочный человек is the nominative subject; должен быть uses the modal/obligation adjective должен + infinitive быть to mean 'must/ought to be.' Трус и раб are predicate nouns (nominative) linked by и and state the ironic condition required of a 'decent' person.
segments.p2-1-g138: Это functions as a demonstrative copula ('that is' / 'this is') introducing a nominal predicate. Закон is nominative and is the main noun ('law' or 'rule'); природы is genitive and goes with закон as an attributive genitive ('law of nature'); всех порядочных людей is an additional genitive phrase specifying whose nature or whose rule it is ('of all decent people'); на земле is a locative prepositional phrase ('on earth') that limits the scope. Word order is neutral Russian: demonstrative + noun phrase with nested genitives and a final locative modifier.
segments.p2-1-g139: Если и случится is a concessive conditional: если = 'if', and the particle и here strengthens it to 'even if'. случится is the perfective future 3rd-person singular of случиться ('happen'). кому из них is a dative phrase meaning 'to/for someone of them' (i.e. 'one of them'). похрабриться is a perfective reflexive infinitive meaning 'to become/braver' or 'to dare', and над чем-нибудь ('над' + instrumental with the indefinite particle -нибудь) means 'about/over something' (indefinite object).
segments.p2-1-g140: то is a result/so particle linking the conditional clause to the consequence ('then'). пусть is the jussive/imprecative particle used to give a third-person command/request ('let him'). этим (instrumental of это) means 'with/by this' and is the thing he would take comfort in. не утешается and не увлекается are negative forms of reflexive verbs (3rd-person singular form used after пусть to express 'let him not ...'), i.e. 'let him not console himself/take comfort' and 'let him not be carried away'.
segments.p2-1-g141: все равно is an adverbial phrase meaning 'all the same' or 'nevertheless'. перед другим uses перед + instrumental to mean 'in the presence of / before another person' (i.e. when faced with someone else). сбрендит is the perfective 3rd-person singular (colloquial) verb meaning 'will go mad' or 'will lose his mind'; here it states the inevitable result despite the earlier bravado.
segments.p2-1-g142: Таков is a short-form demonstrative meaning 'such is' (predicate placed before the noun phrase). единственный и вековечный are coordinated adjectives 'the only and age‑old/everlasting', and выход is the nominative noun 'way out/exit' governed by the predicate Таков; the whole is a concise copular statement 'Such is the only and eternal way out.'
segments.p2-1-g114: Это is a demonstrative pronoun/anticipatory subject ('this' or 'it') referring to what was just described; меня is the accusative object 'me'; мучило is the past neuter form of мучить used impersonally here to mean 'tormented' (imperfective past, describing an ongoing state); до + noun in genitive (до бешенства) expresses extent, literally 'up to/unto madness' (i.e. 'to the point of madness').
segments.p2-1-g115: До болезни ('to the point of illness') is an adverbial measure phrase modifying боялся; тоже here means 'even'/'also' emphasizing the degree; боялся я быть смешным uses past-tense бояться + infinitive 'to be ridiculous', with the subject pronoun я placed after the verb (a common Russian word order). Потому introduces the result clause; рабски is an adverb ('slavishly') modifying обожал; во всем is the preposition в + pronoun (prepositional) meaning 'in everything'.
segments.p2-1-g116: Что here is a relative pronoun 'that', introducing the subordinate clause; касалось is past tense of касаться and takes a genitive object, here meaning 'concerned' or 'touched'; наружного is a nominalized adjective in the genitive meaning 'the outward' or 'outward matters'.
segments.p2-1-g117: С любовью is an adverbial phrase 'with love' (i.e. 'lovingly'); вдавался (вдаваться) in past imperfective means 'would give oneself up to' / 'indulge in' and takes в + accusative (в общую колею 'into the common rut'); всей душою is a manner phrase 'with the whole soul' (instrumental) modifying пугался; пугался is a reflexive past verb 'was frightened' and governs genitive (всякой эксцентричности = 'of any eccentricity'); в себе means 'in myself' (internally).
segments.p2-1-g118: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but'; где here is a rhetorical interrogative 'how/where' (literally 'where'), used to ask how it was possible; мне было + infinitive (выдержать) is an impersonal past construction meaning 'it was for me to endure' — idiomatically 'could I endure' or 'how could I endure'.
segments.p2-1-g119: Я был is the simple past 'I was'; болезненно is an adverb 'morbidly' or 'pathologically' modifying развит; развит is a short-form adjective/past participle 'developed' used predicatively (short form adjective of result/state).
segments.p2-1-g120: Как introduces a comparative clause 'as'; и следует быть is an impersonal modal construction with следует ('one ought') plus infinitive быть 'to be' (следует governs the dative); развитым is the adjective in the instrumental expressing the state 'to be developed'; человеку нашего времени is dative ('for a person of our time' — человеку is governed by следует, нашего времени is genitive 'of our time').
segments.p2-1-g121: Subject (Они) + emphatic particle (же) + predicate made of past copula были and the short-form adjectival predicate тупы (plural). The clause один на другого похожи is a separate nominal predicate meaning “resemble one another” where похожи is the short-form plural adjective ‘similar’ and один на другого is a reciprocal expression literally ‘one on (to) the other.’ The final simile как бараны в стаде uses как + noun + locative phrase to compare them to rams in a flock.
segments.p2-1-g122: Может быть is a fixed modal phrase meaning “perhaps/it may be.” Grammatically it expresses possibility and functions as a sentence adverb (elliptical from ‘it may be that…’).
segments.p2-1-g123: только мне одному во всей канцелярии постоянно казалось is an impersonal construction where казалось (past tense of казаться) governs the experiencer in dative (мне) and the limiting phrase мне одному (“to me alone”) emphasizes that only the speaker had this impression; во всей канцелярии is a prepositional phrase ‘in the whole office,’ and постоянно is an adverb modifying казалось.
segments.p2-1-g124: что introduces the content clause ‘that I was a coward and a slave.’ был is past tense of быть, and трус и раб are nominative predicate nouns following the copula, naming the speaker’s supposed qualities.
segments.p2-1-g125: именно потому и казалось is a connective phrase meaning ‘precisely for that reason it seemed’; именно because emphasizes the cause (потому), and the particle и adds emphasis (‘and/even’), with казалось again impersonal past ‘it seemed.’
segments.p2-1-g126: что introduces the second content clause ‘that I was developed.’ разви́т is a short-form adjective (or passive participial form) meaning ‘developed/ cultivated/advanced’ and with был forms the past-state predicate ‘I was developed’ referring to the speaker’s intellectual development.
segments.p2-1-g127: Но introduces contrast; оно is a neuter dummy subject referring back to the previous notion; не только ... казалось is the phrase ‘not only did it seem,’ with не negating только to create ‘not only,’ and казалось is the impersonal past tense ‘it seemed.’
segments.p2-1-g128: а и действительно так было в самом деле combines particles/conjuncts for emphasis: а и = ‘but even/and even,’ действительно = ‘indeed/actually,’ так было = ‘it was so,’ and в самом деле is an idiom meaning ‘in fact/in reality.’ The clause restates the assertion as real, not merely apparent.
segments.p2-1-g129: я был трус и раб repeats the plain past assertion ‘I was a coward and a slave,’ with был as the copula and two nominative predicate nouns joined by и.
segments.p2-1-g130: Говорю is 1st-person singular present imperfective ‘I say’ used to state the speaker’s current assertion; это is the direct object ‘this,’ and без + genitive (без всякого конфуза) means ‘without any embarrassment,’ where всякого is the genitive of всякий used idiomatically as ‘any’ and конфуза is the genitive of конфуз (‘embarrassment’).
segments.p2-1-g173: Раз here is a discourse particle meaning 'once' or 'now that' introducing a circumstance; даже and совсем are stacked intensifiers ('even' + 'completely'). Подружился is a perfective past verb ('(I) befriended/made friends with') with an implicit first-person subject carried over from the narrator, and с ними uses the preposition с + instrumental (ними) to mark the people with whom the friendship was formed.
segments.p2-1-g174: Стал + infinitive (посещать) expresses 'began to' or 'took to' doing something; the imperfective infinitive посещать emphasizes the resulting habitual action. Их дома is a possessive phrase ('their homes'/'their houses') where дома is plural 'homes' and их marks possession; the subject 'I' remains omitted but understood from the previous verb.
segments.p2-1-g175: В + noun (в преферанс) names the activity/arena — 'at/playing preferans' — and играть is the infinitive coordinated with the previous infinitive посещать (the verb стал is not repeated; the infinitives form a list of things the narrator began to do).
segments.p2-1-g176: A terse coordinated phrase: водку (accusative) is the direct object 'vodka' and пить is the infinitive 'to drink'; again the auxiliary стал is omitted and understood, so this is another activity the narrator began to take up.
segments.p2-1-g177: О + noun (о производстве) marks the topic 'about production/industry' (производстве is in the prepositional), and толковать is an infinitive meaning 'to expound/discuss' — the final item in the coordinated list of activities begun after подружился.
segments.p2-1-g180: Но 'but' introduces a contrast. здесь means 'here' in the discourse ('at this point'). позвольте is the polite 2nd-person-plural imperative of позволить, used to request permission ('allow me'); мне is the dative 'to me'. сделать is the infinitive 'to make/do', and одно отступление (accusative) is the object 'one digression' — so the construction позвольте мне сделать = 'allow me to make'.
segments.p2-1-g181: У нас is a fixed prepositional/genitive phrase meaning roughly “among us” or “as for us/we have.” Here it introduces the topical group (we Russians). The preposition у with a genitive (нас) conveys the idea of 'among/for us' (possessive/locational use).
segments.p2-1-g182: русских is an adjectival form in the genitive plural used appositively: 'we, Russians.' Grammatically it modifies/identifies нас and functions as a noun-like description of the group.
segments.p2-1-g183: вообще говоря is a parenthetical adverbial phrase meaning 'generally speaking.' вообще (in general) is an adverb; говоря is the gerund (present participle) 'speaking,' forming the common idiom.
segments.p2-1-g184: никогда не было is an impersonal past-existential construction meaning 'there never were' or 'we have never had.' Оно takes the genitive plural (глупых ... романтиков). Nadзвездных, немецких, особенно французских are a stack of adjectival modifiers qualifying романтиков.
segments.p2-1-g185: на которых ничего не действует is a relative clause modifying романтиков: на которых = 'on whom' (lit. 'on whom'), ничего не действует = 'nothing acts/has effect' (ничего with negation). The verb действует is present indicative meaning 'has effect' or 'works on.'
segments.p2-1-g186: хоть земля под ними трещи uses хоть ... трещи as a concessive clause 'even if / though the earth crack(s) under them.' Трещи is an archaic/vivid jussive/optative form (imperative-like) of трещать, here expressing hypothetical concession rather than a literal command.
segments.p2-1-g187: хоть погибай вся Франция на баррикадах is a parallel concessive clause: хоть + verb in jussive/optative form (погибай) = 'even if all France perish on the barricades.' It’s dramatic/hypothetical rather than a literal imperative.
segments.p2-1-g188: — они все те же is the main-clause resumptive comment after the dash: они = 'they'; все = 'all/everyone'; те же = 'the same ones.' The particle же adds emphasis ('indeed/the very same').
segments.p2-1-g189: даже для приличия не изменятся: даже 'even'; для приличия = 'for the sake of decency/for appearances'; не изменятся is future reflexive (измениться) 'will not change' (3rd pl.). Together: 'they will not change even to keep up appearances.'
segments.p2-1-g190: и все будут петь свои надзвездные песни: и = 'and'; все = 'everyone/everybody'; будут петь is the compound future (быть + infinitive) 'will be singing/will sing'; свои = reflexive possessive 'their own'; надзвездные песни = 'transcendent/over-the-top songs.'
segments.p2-1-g191: так сказать is a parenthetical idiom 'so to speak.' так = 'so/thus'; сказать in infinitive forms the conventional phrase meaning 'one might say.'
segments.p2-1-g192: по гроб своей жизни is an idiomatic, slightly archaic expression meaning 'to the coffin of their life' → 'to their graves' / 'for the whole of their life.' По here is idiomatic (roughly 'throughout/until'), гроб = 'coffin,' своей жизни = 'of their life.'
segments.p2-1-g193: потому что они дураки is the causal clause 'because they are fools.' Потому что is the standard conjunction 'because'; дураки is the plural noun 'fools/idiots.'
segments.p2-1-g194: У нас is a fixed locative/possessive phrase literally 'at us' meaning 'among us' or 'here (in our country)'. The particle же adds contrastive or emphatic force ('as for us / whereas for us'), linking this clause to what came before.
segments.p2-1-g195: в русской земле is a prepositional phrase marking location: в + prepositional case (земле) with the adjective русской agreeing in gender, number and case; it means 'in the Russian land/country' and further specifies the 'у нас' locality.
segments.p2-1-g196: Нет + genitive expresses existential negation: 'there are no ...'. Дураков is the genitive plural of дурак ('fools'), so нет дураков means 'there are no fools'. This construction has no finite verb and is timeless.
segments.p2-1-g197: Это is a demonstrative/dummy subject ('this/it'), and известно is a neuter short-form adjective/predicative meaning 'is known' or 'well known' (impersonal predicate). Together they form the impersonal clause 'this is well known.'
segments.p2-1-g198: Тем-то is emphatic: тем is the instrumental/demonstrative element meaning 'by that' or 'thereby', with the particle то adding emphasis ('that is precisely/by that'). Мы is the nominative subject; и here adds emphasis/contrast ('indeed/also'); отличаемся is the present-tense reflexive verb 'to differ' (мы отличаемся — 'we differ'); от introduces the object of comparison, and прочих немецких земель is a genitive plural noun phrase ('the other German lands') serving as the thing we differ from.
segments.p2-1-g103: The phrase 'У меня ... делалось' is an impersonal construction meaning 'it happened to me' or 'I experienced it' (literally 'at me it was done'). 'Как-то' (here split as как + то) is an adverbial particle meaning 'somehow', and the stack 'вдруг тогда' ('suddenly, then' / 'at that time') are adverbials modifying 'делалось'. 'Делалось' is the past/imperfective passive/impersonal form of делаться, here conveying that the state or event used to occur to the speaker.
segments.p2-1-g104: The particle 'то' begins the correlative pair 'то ... то ...' meaning 'now/then ... now/then ...' (i.e. 'sometimes ... sometimes ...'). 'Презираю' is present tense, 1st person singular imperfective 'I despise' (habitual/ongoing action here).
segments.p2-1-g105: The second half of the correlative 'то' repeats to show alternation ('sometimes ...'). 'Ставлю' is 1st person singular present of ставить ('I put/place' or here 'I set/raise'), with the omitted direct object referring back to 'это'. 'Выше себя' is a comparative phrase meaning 'higher than myself' (выше governing the reflexive себя).
segments.p2-1-g106: Whole sentence: the adjectives 'Развитой и порядочный' agree with 'человек' (nominative). 'Не может быть тщеславен' uses the modal 'может' + infinitive 'быть' with the short-form adjective 'тщеславен' as predicate ('cannot be vain'). 'Без' governs the genitive 'неограниченной требовательности' ('without unlimited exactingness/demandingness'), and 'к себе самому' is a dative phrase 'toward himself' with 'самому' adding emphatic 'himself personally'. The participial/gerund phrase 'и не презирая себя в иные минуты до ненависти' ('and without despising himself in other moments to hatred') uses the present gerund 'презирая' adverbially to mean 'without even at times coming to despise himself to the point of hatred'; 'до' + genitive expresses the extent 'up to/into hatred.'
segments.p2-1-g107: 'Но' is the coordinating conjunction 'but/however' introducing contrast with what precedes.
segments.p2-1-g108: 'Презирая' is the present-gerund (деепричастие) of 'презирать', meaning 'despising' (adverbial, with an omitted subject understood as the speaker). The enclitic 'ли' turns the clause into a concessive/alternative 'whether despising...' or 'be it that I despised...'.
segments.p2-1-g109: 'Ставя' is the present-gerund of 'ставить' meaning 'placing' or 'setting' (here 'placing it higher'); 'ли' again marks the alternative 'whether placing it higher'; 'выше' is the comparative 'higher' modifying the gerund's implied object.
segments.p2-1-g110: 'Я' is the subject. 'Чуть не' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'almost' (indicating a near miss). 'Перед каждым встречным' ('before every oncoming person') uses 'перед' + instrumental to mean 'in the presence of/every person I met.' 'Опускал' is past imperfective 'lowered' (habitual action) and 'глаза' is the accusative plural 'eyes' — together 'I almost lowered my eyes before every passerby.'
segments.p2-1-g111: 'Я' is the subject. 'Даже' is an emphatic adverb 'even.' 'Опыты делал' literally 'did experiments' — past imperfective verb 'делал' indicates repeated/habitual attempts (I tried experiments/trials).
segments.p2-1-g112: 'Стерплю' is 1st person singular perfective (future) of 'стерпеть' — here used in a rhetorical question 'стерплю ли я' = 'would I endure / could I stand'. The enclitic 'ли' forms the yes/no question. 'Взгляд' is the direct object 'a look/gaze'; 'вот хоть такого-то' is a colloquial phrase meaning 'just even such a one' with 'такого-то' using the particle '-то' for indeterminate emphasis; 'на себе' ('on myself') indicates the look directed at the speaker.
segments.p2-1-g113: 'И' = 'and.' 'Всегда' = 'always.' 'Опускал' is past imperfective (habitual) 'lowered/averted' and the inversion 'опускал я первый' places emphasis on 'я' and 'первый' — 'I was the first to lower (my eyes).' The sentence states the result of his experiments: he always looked away first.
segments.p2-1-g48: У другого marks possession ('the other man had...') with у + genitive; вицмундир is the possessed item. Был is the past form of 'to be' describing the coat's state. До того is an idiomatic degree phrase ('to such an extent'); заношенный is a past participle/adjectival form meaning 'worn'. Together the clause literally says 'the other had a coat that was so worn.'
segments.p2-1-g49: Что introduces a result clause ('that/so that'). Близ него is a prepositional phrase 'near him/around him' (близ takes the genitive). Уже is a temporal/adverbial modifier 'already'. Дурно пахло is a fixed collocation 'smelled bad' (дурно modifies пахло, literal 'smelled badly').
segments.p2-1-g50: А между тем is a connective ('and yet' / 'meanwhile'). Ни один из этих господ is a negative distributive phrase 'not a single one of these gentlemen'. Не конфузился is the negative past 'did not feel embarrassed'. Ни ... по поводу платья begins a series of 'neither ... nor' phrases; по поводу means 'with regard to / on account of' and платья = 'garment/clothes' here.
segments.p2-1-g51: Continuation of the negated list: ни по поводу лица — 'nor because of his face'. Поводу is the noun 'reason/occasion' in the prepositional phrase with по; лица is 'face' (genitive after по поводу).
segments.p2-1-g52: Ни как-нибудь там нравственно is an informal final item in the negative list: ни ... нравственно = 'nor in any moral way.' Как-нибудь там is a colloquial indefinite modifier 'somehow/any which way', softening the sense; нравственно is an adverb 'morally / in a moral sense.'
segments.p2-1-g53: Ни тот is the first half of the correlative pair 'neither the one...'; this is a standard enumerative negative construction paired with ни другой. The short phrase simply marks the first item.
segments.p2-1-g54: Ни другой не воображали continues the correlative 'nor the other' and states the predicate: не воображали is the past negative 'did not imagine / did not fancy'. The subject ('they' or 'those men') is implied by the context.
segments.p2-1-g55: Что introduces the content clause 'that they are looked at with disgust'. Смотрят (present) is used for the perceived action 'look (at them)'; на них is the object 'at them'; с омерзением indicates manner 'with disgust.'
segments.p2-1-g56: Да если б и воображали is a colloquial concessive conditional: да here works like 'and/yet' or 'but', если б (conditional particle б attached) = 'if (they) had' and и adds the sense 'even if.' Воображали is past conditional meaning 'even if they had imagined it.'
segments.p2-1-g57: Так им было бы все равно is a conditional result: так = 'then/so', им было бы = 'it would have been to them', все равно is an idiom 'all the same / indifferent.' The sequence было бы uses the conditional particle бы to form the counterfactual 'would have been.'
segments.p2-1-g58: Только бы не начальство взирать изволило is a wish/condition: только бы = 'only (provided that)/if only', не начальство = 'that the superiors not...', взирать изволило is a polite/ironical verbal cluster meaning 'deigned to look' (взирать 'to gaze' + изволило, past form of изволить 'to deign/wish'). The construction expresses that the only concern was whether the authorities might look.
segments.p2-1-g59: Теперь мне совершенно ясно is a standard impersonal construction: мне (dative) + ясно (predicate adjective) = 'it is clear to me now'; совершенно is an adverb 'completely/utterly' modifying ясно.
segments.p2-1-g60: Что я сам вследствие неограниченного моего тщеславия sets up a causal clause. Я сам uses сам as an emphatic 'I myself'. Вследствие takes the genitive and means 'because of / owing to'; неограниченного моего тщеславия is the genitive noun phrase 'my unlimited vanity' (word order with моего after неограниченного is stylistically emphatic but grammatical).
segments.p2-1-g61: А стало быть is a frozen phrase meaning 'and so / therefore.' Стало быть here functions as a connector drawing the logical conclusion 'thus.'
segments.p2-1-g62: И требовательности к самому себе: и adds 'and' to the previous cause; требовательности is a noun in the genitive tied to the previous вследствие (i.e. 'and of my exactingness'); к самому себе is a dative phrase 'toward myself' with самому adding emphatic 'to myself in particular / to the very self.'
segments.p2-1-g63: Глядел на себя весьма часто с бешеным недовольством: глядел (imperfective past) indicates a habitual past action 'used to look' (not a single glance). На себя is the object 'at himself'; весьма часто = 'very often'; с бешеным недовольством is an instrumental phrase describing the manner 'with furious/violent dissatisfaction.'
segments.p2-1-g64: Доходившим до омерзения is a participial clause in the instrumental agreeing with недовольством: доходившим (past participle/verb in adjectival form) 'reaching up to / extending to', до + genitive expresses the limit 'as far as disgust', so the dissatisfaction 'reached the point of disgust.'
segments.p2-1-g65: А оттого is a short connective 'and therefore / for that reason', signaling the consequence that follows.
segments.p2-1-g66: Мысленно is an adverb 'mentally / inwardly,' marking that the next action took place in thought rather than outwardly.
segments.p2-1-g67: И приписывал мой взгляд каждому: приписывал (imperfective past) means 'attributed / ascribed'; the verb governs an accusative object (мой взгляд = 'my look/gaze') and a dative recipient (каждому = 'to each / to everyone'). The subject 'I' is omitted but understood from the earlier clause; the word order places emphasis on the attributed thing 'my look.'
segments.p2-1-g32: A short locative prepositional phrase: 'В' + noun in the prepositional (or stative) sense. Here 'в должности' means 'in the post/position' or 'while holding the post' and sets the location/role.
segments.p2-1-g33: Another locative prepositional phrase specifying where: 'в канцелярии' = 'in the office.' It narrows the setting introduced by the previous phrase.
segments.p2-1-g34: Main clause clause fragment with subject and a verb of effort: 'я даже старался' = 'I even tried / made an effort' (past imperfective → repeated or habitual effort in the past). It governs the infinitive 'не глядеть' ('not to look'), and 'ни на кого' is a strengthened negative object construction meaning 'not at anyone' (double-negation pattern common in Russian).
segments.p2-1-g35: 'и я очень хорошо замечал' is a linking clause: 'and I very well noticed' — 'замечал' is past imperfective indicating observation or repeated noticing; 'очень хорошо' here means 'very clearly/well' (intensifier of perception).
segments.p2-1-g36: Subordinate clause introduced by 'что' ('that'). Word order 'сослуживцы мои' places the possessive after the noun (stylistic), meaning 'my colleagues.' 'Не только ... считали меня чудаком' = 'not only did they consider me an eccentric' — note Russian uses the verb 'считать' with the object in accusative and the role in the instrumental ('чудаком').
segments.p2-1-g37: Contrastive clause begun by 'но' ('but'). 'все казалось мне и это' literally 'and this all seemed to me' — 'все' here = 'everyone/people generally' and 'казалось мне' = 'seemed to me'; the inserted 'и это' emphasizes that this impression was additionally notable to the narrator. After the dash, 'будто бы смотрели на меня с каким то омерзением' uses the modal/comparative 'будто бы' ('as if') + past plural 'смотрели' agreeing with 'все' → 'as if they looked at me with a kind of disgust.' 'каким то' (older orthography split) = 'каким-то' = 'some kind of.'
segments.p2-1-g38: 'Мне приходило в голову' is an impersonal idiom: dative 'мне' + neuter past 'приходило' + 'в голову' = 'it occurred to me' or 'it came into my head.' The past neuter form is typical for such impersonal constructions.
segments.p2-1-g39: 'отчего это никому' — 'отчего' means 'why/for what reason'; 'это' is a demonstrative/filler; 'никому' is dative negative 'to no one' and sets up the impersonal predicate that follows ('не кажется').
segments.p2-1-g40: 'кроме меня' = 'except me / besides me' (preposition 'кроме' + genitive 'меня'), restricting the group referenced by 'никому.'
segments.p2-1-g41: 'не кажется' is the impersonal present 'does not seem.' With the preceding dative 'никому' it yields 'it does not seem to anyone' (negative particle 'не' negates the verb).
segments.p2-1-g42: Subordinate interrogative clause introduced by 'что' ('that'/'that ...?') — 'смотрят' present plural 'they look', 'на него' = 'at him', and 'с омерзением' uses 'с' + instrumental to indicate manner: 'with disgust.' The whole clause expresses the content of what 'doesn't seem to anyone'.
segments.p2-1-g43: 'У одного из наших канцелярских было ... лицо' is a possession/existence construction: 'У + genitive (one of our clerks) + было' = 'one of our clerks had.' 'отвратительное и прерябое лицо' are adjectives modifying 'лицо' ('a revolting/disgusting and mottled face'). 'прерябое' is an older/colloquial adjective meaning 'mottled/speckled.'
segments.p2-1-g44: 'и даже как будто разбойничье.' — 'и даже' = 'and even'; 'как будто' = 'as if / as though'; 'разбойничье' is an adjective standing alone (neuter) and refers back to 'лицо' (omitted noun): 'and it even seemed as if [the face were] brigand-like / bandit-like.'
segments.p2-1-g45: Subject pronoun plus conditional particle: 'Я бы' — 'I would' (the particle 'бы' marks the counterfactual/conditional).
segments.p2-1-g46: Parenthetical 'кажется' = 'it seems' / 'I suppose' used to soften or hedge the speaker's assertion; impersonal present form.
segments.p2-1-g47: Main conditional clause: 'и взглянуть ни на кого не посмел' = 'and wouldn't even have dared to look at anyone.' 'Взглянуть' is the perfective infinitive 'to glance'; 'ни на кого' = 'at no one / not at anyone'; 'не посмел' (past of 'посметь') combined with 'бы' expresses the conditional 'would not have dared.' 'с таким неприличным лицом' is 'with such an indecent face' (instrumental), describing manner/possession — literally 'having such an indecent face.'
segments.p2-1-g143: Храбрятся is a reflexive present‑tense verb in the 3rd‑person plural (they 'act/pretend brave' or 'brag'); the grammatical subject follows it (ослы и их ублюдки). Только restricts that subject ('only'); их is the possessive pronoun 'their' modifying ублюдки, an insulting plural noun 'bastards/scoundrels'. Word order is verb-first for emphasis (verb agrees with the plural subject).
segments.p2-1-g144: Но ведь и те до известной стены is a concessive clause: но 'but/however', ведь as a parenthetical 'after all' or 'you know', and и here functions as 'even' (emphatic). Те = 'those (ones)', referring back to 'ослы и их ублюдки'. До governs the genitive стена and here means 'up to/until' (literal 'up to a certain wall' used figuratively 'only to a certain point'); известной is an adjectival modifier 'certain/well‑known' in genitive to agree with стены.
segments.p2-1-g145: На них is a prepositional phrase 'at/to them' (object of обратить внимание на кого). И here is emphatic, best translated 'not even' or 'indeed' — it intensifies the negation. Внимания is genitive singular (partitive/genitive after отрицание‑style construction), обращать is the infinitive 'to direct' (as in 'to pay'), and не стоит is an impersonal phrase 'it is not worth / one should not'. The whole phrase literally: 'At them, even paying attention is not worth it.'
segments.p2-1-g146: Потому что introduces the causal clause 'because'. Они is the subject 'they'; ровно is an adverb here intensifying the negation — 'exactly/quite/precisely' and colloquially 'utterly'; ничего is the negative pronoun 'nothing' (object), and не означают is the present tense plural negative 'do not mean/signify'. Word order places ровно before ничего for emphasis: 'they quite mean nothing.'
segments.p2-1-g147: Мучило is the past tense verb 'tormented/troubled' agreeing with a neuter subject (обстоятельство) but placed before that subject for stylistic order; меня is the accusative object 'me'. Тогда ещё ('then still' / 'at that time, also') are temporal/adverbial modifiers. The construction is typical Russian verb–object–subject ordering for emphasis: 'Tormented me then still one circumstance.'
segments.p2-1-g148: Именно is an emphatic adverb 'exactly/namely' marking that what follows will specify the circumstance; то is the demonstrative 'that' (here functioning as a resumptive/demonstrative element introducing the explanatory clause). The comma/colon signals a definition or elaboration to follow.
segments.p2-1-g149: Что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the content clause. На меня никто не похож literally follows the pattern 'on me no one is like' — на introduces the target of resemblance (here used as 'to'/'on' in the idiom похож на), никто ... не = 'no one ... not' (negative subject + negative predicate), and похож is the short-form adjective 'similar/resembling' used predicatively. The second half reverses perspective: я ни на кого не похож = 'I resemble no one', with ни adding extra negation/emphasis ('not a soul').
segments.p2-1-g150: Я-то один uses the particle то immediately after the pronoun as an emphatic marker ('I, for my part' / 'I — the one'), and один here means 'alone' or 'the only one' in contrast to the others. The particle placement (pronoun + то) is a common colloquial way to stress contrast.
segments.p2-1-g151: А introduces a contrast ('while/and' or 'but'), они-то repeats the emphatic particle construction on 'they' ('they, for their part'), and все means 'all/everyone' — together giving the sense 'and they, all of them.' The comma and closing quote mark a pause before the reporting clause.
segments.p2-1-g152: Думал is the past tense 'thought' (masculine singular matching я) and here begins the reporting clause; я repeats the subject after the verb (inverted word order). И is the coordinating 'and', and задумывался is the past reflexive verb 'began to ponder / became lost in thought' — the dash marks a pause or hesitancy between the two mental actions. Both verbs are imperfective past, indicating ongoing or internal mental activity.
segments.p2-1-g153: Из этого видно is an impersonal clause: из (preposition + genitive) 'from/through this' + видно (impersonal predicate) 'it is visible/clear'. Word order puts the source first for emphasis: 'from this, it is clear...'.
segments.p2-1-g154: This is a subordinate clause introduced by что 'that'. был is the past-tense masculine singular of быть, marking a past state; ещё совсем are adverbial intensifiers (ещё = still/yet, совсем = completely/quite). мальчишка is a colloquial noun 'boy/lad', showing childishness.
segments.p2-1-g155: Случались is past imperfective plural used impersonally ('occurred' / 'there happened'), with no explicit subject. И here functions as 'also/even', linking back to the previous thought; противоположности (nominative plural) names the things that occurred.
segments.p2-1-g156: Ведь уж как иногда is a colloquial/intensifying fronted phrase (ведь 'after all', уж 'indeed', как 'how' used exclamatorily) + иногда 'sometimes'. становилось is an impersonal past 'it became', followed by the infinitive ходить 'to go' indicating what became unpleasant; канцелярию is accusative singular 'the office'.
segments.p2-1-g157: доходило до того is an idiomatic perfective/imperfective use meaning 'it reached the point (that)...' — доходило (imperfective past) + до + того (genitive) marks the extent reached before the following clause.
segments.p2-1-g158: This subordinate clause begins with что 'that'. много раз is a frequency phrase 'many times'; со службы uses the preposition со (variant of с used before certain consonant clusters) + genitive to mean 'from duty/from work'. возвращался is imperfective past 'I used to return / returned (repeatedly)', and больной is a predicate adjective 'sick'.
segments.p2-1-g159: ни с того ни с сего is a fixed idiom meaning 'out of the blue / for no reason' (ni ... ni negation particles + с + demonstratives). наступает is present-tense 'begins/comes on' used to describe a recurring mental state; полоса here means 'a stretch/period' and скептицизма и равнодушия are genitive nouns 'of skepticism and indifference'. у меня introduces a possessive/experiential construction 'with me / for me' and все было полосами means 'everything was in streaks' with полосами in the instrumental plural.
segments.p2-1-g160: и вот я же сам смеюсь над моею нетерпимостью и брезгливостью links the sudden mood shift to the narrator's reaction: вот 'then/indeed', я же сам 'I myself' (же + сам for emphasis), смеюсь 'I laugh' (present), над governs the instrumental моею нетерпимостью and брезгливостью 'at my intolerance and squeamishness'; моею is an older/poetic instrumental form of 'my'.
segments.p2-1-g161: сам себя в романтизме упрекаю is a concise present-tense clause: сам себя (emphatic 'I reproach myself') with себя as the reflexive accusative, в романтизме 'in (my) romanticism' functioning as the charge/ground, and упрекаю 'I reproach' (1st person present).
segments.p2-1-g199: Следственно is a sentence‑initial discourse adverb meaning 'consequently' or 'therefore'; it links this sentence to the preceding argument and sets a logical conclusion. It is bookish/formal rather than colloquial.
segments.p2-1-g200: This is an impersonal negative clause: 'и' here functions as an adding/emphatic particle ('even/also'), 'надзвездных натур' is an attributive phrase (adjective in genitive plural + noun in genitive plural) referring to 'transcendent natures/types of persons', and 'не водится' is the impersonal present construction 'is not found/occurs' (водиться). 'У нас' means 'among us' (literally 'at us'), and the prepositional phrase 'в чистом их состоянии' ('in their pure state') places the condition in which such natures do not occur; the order 'чистом их состоянии' (adjective before the pronoun) is stylistically marked but grammatical.
segments.p2-1-g201: This noun phrase is the sentence's subject: 'Это все' ('all these/these all') is a demonstrative + quantifier; 'наши' is the possessive 'our'; '"положительные"' (in quotes) is an ironic or quoted adjective modifying the following nouns; 'тогдашние' ('of that time') is a temporal adjective; 'публицисты и критики' are the coordinating nouns in the nominative plural. The whole phrase names who performed the actions in the main clause.
segments.p2-1-g202: 'Охотясь' is a gerund (деепричастие) from охотиться and functions adverbially: 'being eager/pursuing' or 'in hunting after'. The preposition 'за' with instrumental marks the objects of pursuit (here the proper names in instrumental). 'Да' is an archaic coordinating particle here meaning 'and' (joining the two 'за' phrases). 'Сдуру' is an adverb meaning 'foolishly' and modifies the following gerund 'приняв' ('having accepted/taken'), which is a perfective gerund indicating a secondary action ('having accepted them as our ideal'). Note: 'принять ... за' = 'take/accept ... for/as' is a fixed verbal pattern (the sense of 'за' = 'as' is idiomatic and not listed in the provided senses).
segments.p2-1-g203: 'Навыдумали' is a past plural perfective verb meaning 'they made up / invented' (of fictions or lies). The preposition 'на' with accusative here marks the target/person about whom things were invented (на + accusative in colloquial Russian can mean 'about/against somebody'); 'наших романтиков' is the accusative plural object. The clause is the main action performed by the subject named in the previous segment.
segments.p2-1-g204: 'Сочтя' is another perfective gerund from считать (having considered/ deeming). It governs the object 'их' ('them'), then the preposition 'за' with the following phrase yields the meaning 'considering them as ...'. 'Таких же' is 'such (same) + emphatic particle' (же intensifies/identifies), and 'надзвездных' is the adjective describing the supposed quality. Grammatically, this gerundial clause gives the evaluative stance that motivated the invention.
segments.p2-1-g205: A short comparative clause introduced by 'как' ('as/like') with locative phrases 'в Германии или во Франции' – both prepositions 'в'/'во' mean 'in' (во before Франции for euphony). The clause supplies the standard of comparison: 'as (is the case) in Germany or in France.'
segments.p2-1-g206: Напротив is an adverbial discourse marker meaning 'on the contrary' used to signal contrast; it stands at the start of the sentence and is followed by a comma. Here it functions as a connective, not as the preposition 'напротив + Gen.' ('opposite').
segments.p2-1-g207: This chunk has subject and predicate: 'свойства' (nominative plural) is the subject modified by the genitive attribute 'нашего романтика' ('the qualities of our romantic'). The predicate 'противоположны' is a short-form adjective in the plural and requires a dative complement (hence the following dative 'надзвездно-европейскому'); the adverbs 'совершенно' and 'прямо' modify and intensify that predicate, and the coordinating 'и' simply links the two adverbial modifiers.
segments.p2-1-g208: The conjunction 'и' connects this clause to the previous one. 'Ни одна европейская мерочка' is an emphatic negative noun phrase using 'ни ... не' ('not a single ...'), with 'европейская' agreeing in nominative feminine singular with 'мерочка'. 'Сюда' is an adverbial meaning 'in this case / here', and the verb 'подходит' is 3rd person singular present and is negated by 'не', so the whole clause means that no European standard/measure is appropriate here.
segments.p2-1-g209: Уж is an emphatic particle here (roughly 'do'/'indeed') strengthening the imperative позвольте; позвольте is the polite/plural imperative of позволить 'allow' and takes a dative indirect object мне ('to me'). The infinitive употреблять is the complement of позвольте (allow me to do X), and это слово is the direct object in the accusative (this word).
segments.p2-1-g210: The quoted ROMANTIK is presented as the lexical item itself; the dash links it to an appositive description. Словечко is a diminutive noun 'a little word/wordlet' and старинное is an adjective agreeing with it (neuter singular), here meaning 'old‑fashioned' or 'antique.'
segments.p2-1-g211: Почтенное is an adjective/past participle ('respected'/venerable) in neuter singular to agree with the head словечко; it continues a coordinated list of attributes and thus has no separate verb.
segments.p2-1-g212: Заслуженное is another past participle used adjectivally ('well‑deserved'); и simply connects the final attribute. Всем is dative ('to everyone') and знакомое is an adjective (neuter singular) agreeing with словечко — together they mean 'known/familiar to everyone.'
segments.p2-1-g162: То... ни с кем не хочу is a colloquial pair meaning 'at times I don't want to...'. То here is an adverbial particle ('at that time/then'), and the verb хочу is first-person present indicative. The negative is expressed with ни + prepositional phrase (ни с кем = 'with no one') together with the verb negation не before хочу (double negative is normal in Russian idiom).
segments.p2-1-g163: а то introduces the contrasting alternative 'and/or at other times', with то again as a temporal particle. до того is a prepositional phrase meaning 'to that (extent/point)', and дойду is the perfective future (дойти) first-person singular 'I will reach/come to (that point)'.
segments.p2-1-g164: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the result clause. не только is the fixed negating/contrastive pair 'not only', placed before the verb; разговорюсь is the perfective/inchoative first-person future ('I will begin to talk' / 'I'll get into conversation').
segments.p2-1-g165: но еще sets up the additional result 'but even/moreover'. вздумаю is future first-person of вздумать, colloquial 'I'll take it into my head / I'll think of (doing something)'. с ними is the instrumental 'with them' marking the people involved, and сойтись is the infinitive 'to come together / to get together / to join with'. по-приятельски is an adverb (по- + adjective) meaning 'in a friendly way' — the whole phrase means 'I'll even think of getting together with them as friends.'
segments.p2-1-g166: Вся брезгливость is the nominative subject ('all my squeamishness' / 'all the fastidiousness'). вдруг разом ни с того ни с сего is an adverbial cluster meaning 'suddenly / all at once / out of the blue' (ни с того ни с сего is an idiom 'from no cause'), and исчезала is the imperfect past feminine singular (matching брезгливость) 'would disappear' / 'disappeared' used to describe a recurring or characteristic action.
segments.p2-1-g167: Кто знает is an impersonal rhetorical question 'Who knows...' used to introduce speculation; знает is present third-person but the phrase functions idiomatically as 'perhaps/one cannot say for sure'.
segments.p2-1-g168: может быть is the common modal phrase meaning 'perhaps / maybe' — может is modal 'may/it is possible' and быть is the infinitive used in the fixed expression.
segments.p2-1-g169: ее refers back to the feminine noun (брезгливость) as an object pronoun 'it/her'. у меня in this existential/possessive construction means 'at me' → 'I had' (or rather 'I possessed'), никогда 'never' with the particle и adding emphasis ('never at all'), and не было is the impersonal past 'there was not' / 'I did not have'. The sequence emphasizes that it perhaps never existed in the speaker.
segments.p2-1-g170: а была она напускная contrasts with the previous negative: а была = 'but it was', with была past tense and она the pronoun for emphasis; напускная is an adjective used predicatively meaning 'affected / put-on / feigned'. The word order (была она напускная) places focus on the judgment that it was merely put-on.
segments.p2-1-g171: из книжек? is a short question 'from books?' — из + genitive indicates origin/source, and книжек is the genitive plural of книжка ('books' here). The speaker wonders whether the affectation was adopted from reading.
segments.p2-1-g172: Я is the subject 'I'. до сих пор is the fixed temporal phrase 'until now / up to this time' (сих = 'this', пор = 'time'), этого вопроса is the genitive object 'of this question' (literally 'this question' as the matter in question), еще не разрешил is 'have still not resolved' — еще (yet/still) + negation не + past tense разрешил (I resolved), so the whole means 'I have not yet resolved this question.'
segments.p2-1-g311: The sentence is built around the impersonal past form 'Хотелось' (from хотеться) which expresses a felt desire or inclination and governs the infinitive 'заглушить' — literally 'it was desired to drown out' but naturally rendered 'I wanted to drown out / I felt like drowning out.' 'Заглушить' is an infinitive taking the phrase 'внешними ощущениями' in the instrumental as the means or instrument. 'Все' (everything) is the direct object of 'заглушить' and is modified by the past participle 'накипавшее' ('that had accumulated'), with the adverb 'беспрерывно' and the locative phrase 'внутри меня' qualifying that participle. Word order places the instrument before the object for emphasis; the participle 'накипавшее' is used nominally ('all that had accumulated').
segments.p2-1-g312: 'A' is a connective particle introducing a mild contrast with the previous clause. 'Из внешних ощущений' is a genitive phrase 'out of/from external sensations' specifying the domain. The core phrase 'было ... в возможности' is slightly formal/idiomatic: 'было в возможности' = 'was within the realm of possibility'; 'для меня' (dative) personalizes that possibility ('for me'). 'Только одно чтение' is the nominal predicate ('only one — reading'), where 'чтение' (neuter) is the thing that was possible. Word order (verb before phrase 'в возможности') is typical in literary Russian and emphasizes the existential/impersonal sense 'there was (available)'.
segments.p2-1-g313: 'Чтение' is a nominative noun (neuter) functioning as the subject/topic of the sentence; the comma marks it off as the item being described by the following predicates.
segments.p2-1-g314: 'конечно' is an adverbial parenthetical ('of course') qualifying the speaker's assurance about the next claim.
segments.p2-1-g315: 'много' here is an adverb meaning 'a great deal' and modifies the verb 'помогало'; 'помогало' is past tense, neuter singular to agree with the neuter subject 'чтение', expressing continued or habitual help ('helped a lot').
segments.p2-1-g316: The dash introduces a series of further predicates describing what reading did. 'Волновало' is past tense, neuter singular (agreeing with 'чтение') meaning 'it agitated/stirred [me]' or 'it excited me emotionally.'
segments.p2-1-g317: 'Услаждало' and 'мучило' are two more past-tense neuter singular predicates (agreeing with 'чтение') joined by 'и' — 'it pleased/delighted and tormented [me].' The sequence shows mixed, contradictory effects of reading.
segments.p2-1-g318: 'Но' is a coordinating conjunction ('but') introducing a contrast. 'По временам' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'at times' or 'from time to time' (по + dative plural of время used idiomatically). 'Наскучало' is a past neuter form meaning 'became/grew boring' (impersonal experiential form matching the neuter subject 'чтение' from the previous sentence or used in a general sense), and 'ужасно' is an adverbial intensifier 'terribly/very.'
segments.p2-1-g255: Наш романтик — subject + noun phrase 'our romantic'. Скорей is an adverbial modifier meaning 'rather/sooner' that qualifies the verb; сойдет is the perfective verb сойти used here in the idiom сойти с ума 'go mad'. The preposition с governs the genitive ума. The clause is the main assertion and the parenthesis follows immediately after.
segments.p2-1-g256: Впрочем is a parenthetical/concessive particle 'however' used to soften or qualify the previous claim; it stands alone here and links the parenthetical remark to the main clause.
segments.p2-1-g257: Очень редко бывает is a parenthetical present-tense comment 'very rarely happens'. Бывает is impersonal/habitual here meaning 'it happens/occurs'. The whole parenthesis (что, впрочем, очень редко бывает) qualifies the preceding statement.
segments.p2-1-g258: А introduces a contrast 'but'. Плеваться (infinitive, reflexive) 'to spit' is the action being negated; не станет uses стать in a future sense 'will begin / will (not) do' so не станет + infinitive = 'will not start to/ will not (do)'.
segments.p2-1-g259: Если opens a conditional clause 'if'. Другой карьеры is a genitive noun phrase 'another career' and у него is the possessive construction ('he has' / 'in his view'); в виду is an idiomatic two-word phrase 'in view' / 'in mind', and имеется (impersonal) means 'exists / is present'. The clause thus says 'if he does not have another career in mind.'
segments.p2-1-g260: И continues the list/contrast 'and'. В + толчки here expresses motion 'into the tolchki' (colloquial for public latrines); его is the accusative object 'him'; никогда не выгонят is future/plural, negated 'they will never drive/expel (him)'.
segments.p2-1-g261: А разве introduces a rhetorical question/possibility 'or perhaps' (разве softens or questions the improbability). Свезут is perfective future 'they will cart/bring (by vehicle)' and в сумасшедший дом is the destination 'to the insane asylum'. В виде + noun (испанского короля, genitive) means 'in the guise/form of.'
segments.p2-1-g262: Да и то is an idiom 'and even that' / 'and that only'. Если уж introduces a concessive conditional 'if indeed / if really'; уж intensifies. Он очень с ума сойдет repeats the idiom сойти с ума in future-perfective 'he really goes mad.' The whole clause limits the preceding hypothetical: even taking him to the asylum would occur only if he truly went insane.
segments.p2-1-g263: Но ведь combines contrastive but (но) with the particle ведь 'after all/you see' to introduce an emphatic statement. Сходят (pl.) together with с ума forms the idiom 'go mad'; у нас here means 'among us' or 'in our society'. Только limits the statement 'only'. The adjectives жиденькие and белокуренькие are informal diminutive descriptors 'scrawny/weak' and 'fair‑haired' and serve as the subjects' description.
segments.p2-1-g264: Неисчетное число is an idiomatic nominative subject 'an innumerable number'. Же is an emphatic particle reinforcing the statement. Романтиков is a genitive complement to число ('a number of romantics'). Значительные чины is the outcome/predicate 'significant ranks/offices', and впоследствии is the adverb 'subsequently/afterwards'. Происходят is a verb meaning 'occur/turn out/come to be' here used to mean 'come to hold / attain' such ranks.
segments.p2-1-g265: A terse exclamation made of the noun многосторонность 'multifacetedness/versatility' followed by the adjective необыкновенная 'extraordinary'. There is no verb; the phrase serves as an emphatic comment on the preceding statements.
segments.p2-1-g344: This is a concise past-tense clause: the subject Я (I) is stated, with именно giving emphasis ('precisely' / 'exactly'). Себя is the reflexive accusative marking that the action опра́вдать (to justify) is directed at the speaker, and хотел is the past masculine form of хоте́ть, indicating a past desire ('wanted to'). Word order (subject — adverb — object — verb) is natural and slightly emphatic because именно precedes себя.
segments.p2-1-g345: Это here functions as a demonstrative 'this' or 'it' pointing to what follows; the sequence Это я is colloquial and slightly emphatic ('this I' / 'I am doing this'). Для себя is a prepositional phrase meaning 'for myself' or 'to myself', marking the beneficiary of the action that follows.
segments.p2-1-g346: Господа is a vocative/addresses form (plural of господин) used to address the audience: 'gentlemen' or 'sirs'. It is set off by commas as a direct address and does not change the sentence's syntax.
segments.p2-1-g347: Заметочку is the diminutive/accusative form of заметка ('a little note'), giving a sense of smallness or casualness; делаю is the 1st person present of дела́ть ('I make / I am making'), an imperfective verb showing the act as current or habitual. Together they mean 'I am making a little note.'
segments.p2-1-g348: Не negates the verb phrase. Хочу is 1st person present of хоте́ть ('I want'), and лгать is the infinitive complement ('to lie'); the construction не хочу + infinitive means 'I do not want to [do X]'.
segments.p2-1-g349: This is an elliptical past-tense clause with the subject (я) omitted as common in Russian; словo is the direct object of дать in the idiom дать слово ('to give one's word' = to promise). Дал is the past masculine singular of дать ('gave'), so together they mean 'I gave my word' or 'I promised.'
segments.p2-1-g300: The preposition с governs the instrumental plural товарищами (with my comrades); моими is the instrumental plural possessive agreeing with товарищами; the subject pronoun я is placed after the prepositional phrase for emphasis.
segments.p2-1-g301: Разумеется is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally', used to qualify the statement that follows.
segments.p2-1-g302: This clause strings together three past-imperfective actions describing habitual or repeated behavior: дружества не выдерживал uses the genitive дружества after не выдерживал to mean 'could not tolerate/endure companionship'; расплевывался is a reflexive imperfective verb meaning literally 'spat (repeatedly)' or figuratively 'showed contempt'; вследствие + noun in genitive (еще юной тогдашней неопытности) gives the cause 'owing to my then still youthful inexperience'; переставал + infinitive (кланяться) means 'ceased (to do something)', and им is the dative 'to them'.
segments.p2-1-g303: Точно here functions as a simile particle 'as if' (rather than strictly 'exactly'); отрезывал is imperfective past of отрезывать 'to cut off' — used figuratively to mean he severed relations or treated them as cut off.
segments.p2-1-g304: Это is the neuter demonstrative pronoun 'this/that' functioning as the grammatical subject of случилось; it is often used to point to the event just described.
segments.p2-1-g305: Впрочем is a parenthetical particle meaning 'however' or 'though', softening or qualifying the statement.
segments.p2-1-g306: Со мной is the prepositional phrase 'with me' used idiomatically with случилось to mean 'happened to me'; всего один раз is an expression 'only one time' (всего = 'only/merely', один раз = 'once'); and случилось is the perfective past 'happened' agreeing in gender/number with the neuter demonstrative это.
segments.p2-1-g307: Вообще is an adverb meaning 'in general' or 'generally'; же is an emphatic particle adding contrast or insistence; я is the subject; всегда 'always' modifies the predicate; был is past tense of быть ('was'); один here is a predicative adjective meaning 'alone' (not the numeral).
segments.p2-1-g308: Дома is an adverb meaning 'at home' (location); the pronoun я follows for a neutral subject position: 'At home I...'.
segments.p2-1-g309: Во-первых is a fixed discourse marker meaning 'firstly' or 'for one thing'; it is formed from во (variant of в) + первых but functions here as a single adverbial connective.
segments.p2-1-g310: Всего больше is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'mostly' or 'more than anything else' (всего intensifies больше); читал is past imperfective 'read' indicating habitual or repeated action.
