segments.p2-1-g332: Закипала is a past-tense, feminine-singular verb form (from закипать) agreeing with the feminine noun тоска that follows; placed before the noun for stylistic inversion. Here it is used metaphorically — "to boil/seethe up."
segments.p2-1-g333: сверх того is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'besides'/'in addition'; сверх is a preposition/adverb and того is the genitive/demonstrative element that completes the idiom. It links the clause just said to an additional point.
segments.p2-1-g334: тоска is a feminine noun placed after the verb (inverted order); it names the feeling that "boiled up." In this context it means a melancholic anguish or melancholy rather than mere longing.
segments.p2-1-g335: являлась is the past-tense, feminine-singular form of являться, used here as a simple intransitive 'appeared/manifested' and agreeing with the feminine noun жажда; истерическая is an adjective modifying жажда, and жажда (in genitive construction following являться) means a strong craving. Противоречий is genitive plural, the object of that craving ('for contradictions').
segments.p2-1-g336: контрастов is another noun in genitive plural coordinated with противоречий, continuing the list of objects of the preceding 'craving for ...'. The genitive plural follows the noun жажда introduced earlier.
segments.p2-1-g337: и вот is a discourse sequence meaning 'and then/so'; the double и (first a simple connector, second an emphatic/colloquial particle) is stylistic. пускался is the past-tense, masculine-singular reflexive form of пускаться and with an infinitive indicates 'would let oneself go / set about doing' something; развратничать is the infinitive 'to behave licentiously/debauchedly.' Altogether: 'and so I would give myself up to debauchery.'
segments.p2-1-g338: This whole clause is a single negated statement. Я is the subject; ведь is a particle emphasizing 'after all'; вовсе не is a strong negator 'by no means/at all'; для + правдание (оправдания) uses the genitive to mark purpose ('for the sake of justification' = 'to justify'); моего is a possessive in genitive ('my'); сейчас is a temporal adverb ('now/just now'); столько modifies наговорил ('said so much'); наговорил is past tense, perfective, masculine-singular (the narrator's 'I said/uttered so much'). The phrase means the narrator did not speak those many words now in order to excuse himself.
segments.p2-1-g341: А впрочем combines the conjunction а ('but') with впрочем ('however/on the other hand') to signal an abrupt revision or qualification of what was just said; it's a discourse marker meaning roughly 'but then' or 'on second thought.'
segments.p2-1-g342: нет is an interjection here ('no') used to reject or reverse the previous statement; emphatic and standalone.
segments.p2-1-g343: соврал is the past-tense, masculine-singular perfective form of соврать, used as an abrupt exclamation; the narrator confesses that what he just said was a lie. It is a simple past assertion used emphatically.
segments.p2-1-g238: То есть is a set phrase meaning 'that is' / 'I mean' (то + copular есть). Что ж combines the interrogative что ('what') with the particle ж for emphasis — together forming an exclamatory/questioning phrase. The final это я is a colloquial, slightly inverted expletive construction (это + pronoun) making the speaker's self the focus of the exclamation; the copula is not expressed because Russian typically omits 'to be' in present-tense predicates.
segments.p2-1-g239: Nominal predication with a short-form adjective: романтик (nominative) is the subject and умен is the short-form adjective functioning as the predicate ('is clever'). The particle и before всегда adds a slight emphatic/colloquial force ('indeed/after all' or 'even'), and the present copula is omitted.
segments.p2-1-g240: A simple past-tense clause: я хотел (past masculine of хотеть) + infinitive заметить expresses 'I wanted to remark.' Только is an adverb limiting the intention ('only/merely').
segments.p2-1-g241: This begins a subordinate clause introduced by что ('that'). Хоть и is a concessive formula meaning 'although/even though.' Бывали is past plural of бывать used impersonally/habitually to mean 'there were' or 'occurred' (we have had); у нас ('among us') is a locative/possessive expression. Дураки-романтики is a compound noun: 'foolish romantics.'
segments.p2-1-g242: Но introduces contrast ('but'). Это не в счет is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'this does not count/doesn't matter' (не + noun phrase в счет). И единственно потому links on to give the sole reason: и ('and/also') + единственно ('only/solely') + потому ('for that reason').
segments.p2-1-g243: The clause gives the reason: что ('that') + они ('they') + еще ('still') + в цвете сил ('in the prime of their powers' — set phrase: в + prepositional 'цвете' + genitive 'сил') + окончательно ('completely') + в немцев перерождались (перерождаться + into-accusative indicates 'were transformed/turned into Germans'). И links to the following purpose clause.
segments.p2-1-g244: Чтоб is a colloquial subordinator meaning 'in order that' and governs the infinitive сохранить ('to preserve/keep'). Удобнее is a comparative adverb 'more conveniently'; свою ювелирскую вещицу is the accusative object — a possessive + adjective + diminutive noun meaning 'their little jewelled trinket.'
segments.p2-1-g245: Поселялись is past-imperfective plural of поселиться/поселяться, here indicating 'they settled' or 'took up residence' (habitual/past). Там где-нибудь is an adverbial phrase meaning 'somewhere there' (там + indefinite где-нибудь).
segments.p2-1-g246: Больше here functions adverbially to mean 'more' or 'mostly' (i.e., 'more often in Weimar'). В Веймаре is the locative expression 'in Weimar.'
segments.p2-1-g247: Simple alternative: или ('or') + locative phrase в Шварцвальде ('in the Black Forest').
segments.p2-1-g248: Simple nominative pronoun 'Я' ('I') used as the sentence subject; the comma marks a pause before the parenthetical следующая вставка ('for example').
segments.p2-1-g249: Например is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'for example' or 'for instance,' modifying the whole utterance that follows.
segments.p2-1-g250: Two coordinated predicates: искренно презирал (past tense imperfective: 'sincerely despised') + direct object свою служебную деятельность ('my official activity/duties'). И connects the second predicate не плевался ('did not spit' / 'refrained from spitting'); только по необходимости ('only out of necessity') restricts that second action — i.e. he refrained only when necessity required it.
segments.p2-1-g251: Потому что introduces the causal clause 'because.' Сам is emphatic 'myself' (I personally). Там сидел is literal 'sat there' but here idiomatically means 'occupied that post/position' (сидел can mean 'held a position'); и деньги за то получал states he 'received/was paid money for it' — за то refers back to the work/duties.
segments.p2-1-g252: В результате is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning “as a result” or “in consequence”; the particle же adds emphasis/contrast and is commonly attached to such adverbials (here punctuated with a comma).
segments.p2-1-g253: Заметьте is the 2nd-person plural (or polite) imperative of заметить meaning “note” or “mind you / observe.” It is parenthetical here, drawing the reader’s attention to the following point.
segments.p2-1-g254: Все-таки is an adverb meaning “after all” / “still.” Не is the negation, and плевался is the past tense, masculine singular form of плеваться (“to spit”); together they mean “(I) did not spit,” with the subject omitted but understood from context (first person).
segments.p2-1-g287: Впрочем is a sentence‑initial discourse particle meaning 'however' or 'anyway' that links this sentence to what preceded; here it functions as a contrastive connector.
segments.p2-1-g288: я (nominative) is the subject; уверен is the short form adjective (masculine, nominative) used predicatively to mean 'I am sure/assured'. Together they form the main clause 'I am sure'.
segments.p2-1-g289: вы is the 2nd person plural/formal pronoun (subject of the subordinate clause); опять is an adverb 'again' placed before the verb for emphasis; думаете is 2nd person plural present indicative of думать 'to think', so 'you think again'.
segments.p2-1-g290: что is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing the complement clause; я is the clause subject; смеюсь is 1st person singular present of смеяться (imperfective) meaning 'I am laughing' (literal here), completing the thought 'that I am laughing.'
segments.p2-1-g291: А is a coordinating particle (here 'and' / 'well') that introduces a new, slightly adversative remark; кто знает is an idiomatic interrogative phrase 'who knows' (rhetorical).
segments.p2-1-g292: может быть is a parenthetical modal phrase meaning 'perhaps' or 'it may be'; грамматически это сочетание модального может + инфинитив/инфинитивное быть, here frozen as an adverbial expression of possibility.
segments.p2-1-g293: и is a coordinating particle adding 'even/also' and обратно is an adverb meaning 'conversely' or 'the other way round' — together they signal 'perhaps even the opposite (is true)'.
segments.p2-1-g294: то есть is a parenthetical clarification 'that is' (то = demonstrative 'that', есть = 'is'); уверены is the short adjective plural (nominative) 'you are certain', with the subject (вы) carried over from earlier.
segments.p2-1-g295: что is the complementizer 'that'; я is the subject; и в самом деле is a fixed phrase (и + в самом деле) meaning 'indeed/actually' (intensifier); так modifies думаю and here means 'so' or 'in that way'; думаю is 1st person present 'I think'. The clause reads 'that I really do think so.'
segments.p2-1-g296: Во всяком случае is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'in any case' or 'in any event' (во is the preposition variant of в used before certain forms, всяком is the prepositional singular of всякий, случае is prepositional of случай).
segments.p2-1-g297: господа is the vocative/address form 'gentlemen' (plural), used here to address the listeners directly; it's placed as a direct address set off by commas.
segments.p2-1-g298: оба мнения ваши puts the numeral оба ('both') before the noun мнения (nominative plural of мнение) with the possessive вашɪ placed after the noun (permissible word order for emphasis): 'both your opinions'. я буду считать is the periphrastic future (я + буду + infinitive считать) meaning 'I will consider'; себе is a dative reflexive meaning 'for myself' or 'to myself'; за честь is the idiom 'as an honor' (считать за + accusative); и особенное удовольствие adds 'and a particular/special pleasure' as a further complement.
segments.p2-1-g299: А is a coordinating particle introducing the brief polite request; отступление мое is a noun phrase 'my digression' (отступление 'digression', мое 'my'); мне is the dative 'to me' functioning idiomatically with the imperative to soften the request; простите is the 2nd person plural (or polite) imperative of простить 'forgive' — word order places the dative before the verb for emphasis/politeness: 'forgive me my digression.'
segments.p2-1-g277: Повторяю is the 1st-person singular present indicative of повторять, used here as an emphatic discourse marker: “I repeat” or “I reiterate.” The comma signals continuation into an explanatory clause.
segments.p2-1-g278: This whole clause contains an idiom and a simple clause: 'ведь сплошь да рядом' is a colloquial idiom meaning 'as a rule / more often than not'; 'из наших романтиков' = 'from among our romantics' (из + genitive); 'выходят' is 3rd-person plural present indicative meaning 'emerge/turn out'; 'иногда' is an adverb 'sometimes'; 'такие деловые шельмы' is the noun phrase 'such practical scoundrels' as the complement; the parenthesis '(слово "шельмы" я употребляю любя)' is a parenthetical clause where 'я употребляю' is present tense 'I use' and 'любя' is an adverbial participle (деепричастие) meaning 'lovingly / affectionately' and modifies the manner of 'употребляю.'
segments.p2-1-g279: 'такое' here (= 'such') modifies both following nouns; 'чутьё действительности' = 'a feel/instinct for reality' (чутьё + genitive); 'и знание положительного' = 'and knowledge of the positive' (положительного used substantively); these nouns function as the subject for the verb 'оказывают' (3rd-person plural present), and 'вдруг' (= 'suddenly') is an adverb qualifying that verb: 'suddenly (these qualities) show themselves / assert themselves.'
segments.p2-1-g280: 'что' introduces the result clause: 'that ...'; 'изумленное' is a short/attributive participle 'astonished' agreeing with the compound subject 'начальство и публика'; 'только языком на них в остолбенении пощелкивают' literally 'only with the tongue at them in stupefaction they click' — idiomatically 'can only click their tongues at them in stupefaction.' 'пощелкивают' is present plural imperfective, indicating repeated/habitual action.
segments.p2-1-g281: Многосторонность is a feminine noun in the nominative (the subject). 'поистине' is an adverb meaning 'truly' modifying the predicate adjective 'изумительная'; together they form the clause 'The multifacetedness is truly astonishing.'
segments.p2-1-g282: This is a compound continuation: 'и бог знает' is an idiomatic parenthetical ('and God knows') used as a hedge; 'во что обратится она' = 'into what it will turn' where 'обратится' is future reflexive/intransitive 'turn into' (3rd sing.) and 'она' refers to 'многосторонность'; 'и выработается при последующих обстоятельствах' uses 'выработается' (future passive/reflexive 'will be worked out/developed') with 'при' + locative plural 'последующих обстоятельствах' = 'under/en suing circumstances'; final 'и что сулит нам в нашем дальнейшем?' is a rhetorical question 'and what does it bode/promise us in our future?' with 'сулит' present used in a prophetic/rhetorical sense.
segments.p2-1-g283: А here functions as an interjection/contrastive particle ('ah' / 'and'); 'недурен' is a short-form adjective meaning 'not bad' (masculine nominative), predicative referring to the implied noun; 'матерьял' is a colloquial spelling of 'материал' meaning 'material' or 'subject matter'; the final '-с' is an enclitic colloquial/folksy particle (stylistic, emphatic) attached to the noun — not a preposition here.
segments.p2-1-g284: 'Не' negates the reason expressed by the following prepositional phrase; 'из патриотизма' (из + genitive) means 'out of patriotism'; 'какого-нибудь' is an indefinite postpositive modifier meaning 'some kind of / any sort of' and softens the noun to 'any kind of patriotism.'
segments.p2-1-g285: Adjectives in the genitive ('смешного или квасного') give examples of the sort of patriotism the speaker excludes: 'смешного' = 'ridiculous' and 'квасного' is an ironic adjective (literally 'kvass-like') meaning petty, narrow-minded nationalism. They are presented as alternatives by 'или.'
segments.p2-1-g286: 'я' is the subject pronoun 'I'; 'так' is an adverb meaning 'thus / in that sense' referring back to what was just said; 'говорю' is 1st-person-singular present 'I say' — a simple declarative clause stating the speaker's motive is not patriotic.
segments.p2-1-g213: Свойства (plural) is the grammatical subject meaning 'qualities' and is followed by a dash plus это used as a linking demonstrative ('these are' / 'are'). нашего романтика is a genitive phrase 'of our romantic' modifying свойства. The final infinitive phrase все понимать functions as a predicate nominal expressing the characteristic action 'to understand everything.'
segments.p2-1-g214: This continues the catalogue with two parallel infinitives: все видеть ('to see everything') and (repeated) видеть modified by adverbs. часто 'often' modifies the second видеть; несравненно is an intensifying adverb 'incomparably' and яснее is the comparative adjective 'more clear(ly)' used adverbially with видеть.
segments.p2-1-g215: чем introduces the comparison ('than') attaching to яснее. видят is 3rd-person plural present 'they see.' самые плюс superlative phrase (самые положительнейшие) intensifies 'our minds' (наши умы) — an ironical superlative construction meaning 'the most positively-minded of our intellects.'
segments.p2-1-g216: A parallel infinitival clause with a double negative pattern: ни с кем и ни с чем (literally 'with neither anyone nor anything') followed by не примиряться (infinitive negative 'to not reconcile' / 'to make peace with'). The ni...ni... construction emphasizes total refusal to reconcile.
segments.p2-1-g217: но в то же время ('but at the same time') introduces a contrasting infinitive clause: ничем и не брезгать. This uses a negative pronoun (ничем) + negation (не) with the infinitive брезгать to mean 'to disdain nothing' / 'to recoil from nothing' — another paradoxical trait.
segments.p2-1-g218: A short infinitival item in the list: все (everything) + обойти. Here обойти is an infinitive meaning 'to get/ go around' or 'to bypass/evade' — i.e. to avoid direct confrontation and work around obstacles.
segments.p2-1-g219: всему is dative ('to/everything'); уступить is the infinitive 'to yield' or 'to concede.' The dative + уступить expresses the habit of giving way to everything.
segments.p2-1-g220: со всеми is instrumental/plural 'with everyone'; поступить is a perfective infinitive meaning 'to act' or 'to behave,' and политично is an adverb meaning 'politically' or better here 'tactically / tactfully.' Together: 'to act tactfully with everyone.'
segments.p2-1-g221: постоянно ('constantly') modifies the following negative infinitive не терять ('not to lose'). из виду is an idiomatic complement ('from sight' → 'lose sight of'), and полезную is an adjective in the accusative feminine agreeing with an understood noun цель carried into the next segment: 'to constantly not lose sight of a useful...'.
segments.p2-1-g222: This completes the object begun in the previous segment: практическую цель ('a practical goal'). The parenthetical examples start with какие-нибудь ('some/any') plus там (colloquial filler 'there' / 'you know') and казенные квартирки ('state-owned little flats'). The adjectives precede their nouns normally and цель is the head noun.
segments.p2-1-g223: A single parenthetical noun in diminutive: пенсиончики — a pejorative/diminutive plural meaning 'little pensions' or 'small boarding-houses' as another practical aim/example.
segments.p2-1-g224: This long clause: звездочки closes the parenthesis; усматривать эту цель is an infinitive phrase 'to discern/perceive this goal.' через introduces the means ('through' / 'by means of') and governs все энтузиазмы и томики лирических стишков ('all enthusiasms and little volumes of lyrical verses'). и в то же время ('and at the same time') introduces a coordinated infinitive-clause headed by сохранить (perfective infinitive 'to preserve'), with the quoted phrase "и прекрасное и высокое" functioning as its direct object. по гроб своей жизни is an idiom meaning 'to one's coffin' = 'to the grave / for the whole of one's life,' в себе 'in oneself,' and нерушимо is a predicative/adverbial describing the manner 'inviolably.' Note the alternation of imperfective (усматривать) and perfective (сохранить) infinitives to mark different aspectual nuances.
segments.p2-1-g225: да и (conjunctive particle 'and moreover') introduces an additional coordinated infinitive phrase: себя сохранить — 'to preserve oneself.' уже кстати вполне are adverbial modifiers ('already', 'incidentally', 'completely') stacked for emphasis. так-таки is a colloquial emphatic repetition ('just so' / 'after all') and в хлопочках ('in cotton-wads' — idiomatic small protective wrappings / little fusses) is a locative/instrumental phrase describing the manner of preservation.
segments.p2-1-g226: как introduces a simile 'like/as'; ювелирскую вещицу — accusative phrase 'a jeweler's little thing' (diminutive, 'trinket'); какую-нибудь is a post-nominal indefinite 'some sort of' used colloquially after the noun to soften/indefinite the example.
segments.p2-1-g227: хотя бы is a fixed colloquial particle meaning 'at least' or 'if only' — formed from хотя ('though / at least') plus the modal particle бы; here it serves as a softening 'at least' after the simile.
segments.p2-1-g228: примерно is not present; the actual word is например — a single adverb meaning 'for example,' introducing a concrete illustration of the preceding simile.
segments.p2-1-g229: для пользы is a genitive construction meaning 'for the benefit / for the sake of.' того же uses the demonstrative in genitive plus же as an emphatic 'that very/same' referring back to the quoted 'прекрасного и высокого' — abstract nouns in genitive meaning 'the beautiful and the lofty.' The whole phrase: 'for the benefit of that same "beautiful and the lofty."'
segments.p2-1-g230: Широкий человек is an adjectival phrase 'a broad (large‑minded/generous) man'; наш романтик is an apposition identifying that man as 'our romantic.' The coordinated phrase и первейший плут из всех наших плутов adds 'and the foremost rogue of all our rogues' — из takes the genitive plural (всех наших плутов) to mark 'of/out of all.'
segments.p2-1-g231: Уверяю is 1st person singular present 'I assure' and takes вас (accusative 'you') as its direct object. В том is a fixed prepositional phrase (в + demonstrative pronoun in the prepositional case) meaning 'of that'/'about that' — here functioning like English 'of that' in 'I assure you of that.'
segments.p2-1-g234: Даже is an adverb 'even' adding emphasis. По with the dative опыту forms a short adverbial phrase meaning 'by/according to/from experience' (по + dative often expresses 'on the basis of' or 'in the matter of').
segments.p2-1-g235: Разумеется is an introductory adverb meaning “of course” or “naturally.” It functions parenthetically here to qualify the whole sentence and is followed by a comma.
segments.p2-1-g236: Все это is a noun phrase meaning “all this” or “all of that”; все (here a pronoun/quantifier) precedes the demonstrative это. The comma after it sets off the following conditional clause.
segments.p2-1-g237: Если is the subordinating conjunction “if.” Романтик (nominative singular) is the subject of the clause, and умен is a short-form predicate adjective (masculine singular) meaning “is clever/smart”; Russian omits the copula 'is' in the present, so the clause reads literally “if the romantic (is) clever.”
segments.p2-1-g319: Все-таки is a compound particle meaning 'still / after all' (here split into two words in the segmentation). Хотелось is the impersonal past of хотеться and expresses a felt desire — 'I wanted / I felt like'; it takes an infinitive (двигаться) as its complement. Двигаться is an infinitive (imperfective) meaning 'to move' and completes the impersonal construction.
segments.p2-1-g320: И is the simple conjunction 'and'. Я is the nominative personal pronoun 'I'. Вдруг is an adverb 'suddenly'. Погружался is the imperfective past (reflexive) verb 'погружаться', here describing a sudden inward movement/process 'I plunged/was sinking into' (imperfective focuses on the process). В + adjective (темный) expresses direction into a state/place (в + accusative).
segments.p2-1-g321: Подземный is an adjective 'underground' that coordinates with the previous adjective (темный); grammatically it agrees in gender/number/case with the implied noun (here the idea of a place/state into which he plunges).
segments.p2-1-g322: Гадкий is an adjective 'nasty/vile' describing that subterranean state. The dash and the negation не before разврат mark a contrast: he denies the high-sounding noun разврат ('depravity') as the right label for what he experienced.
segments.p2-1-g323: А is the contrastive conjunction 'but'. Развратишко is a colloquial/diminutive pejorative noun (formed from разврат) meaning a petty or nasty little vice; the diminutive here downplays and belittles the thing compared with 'разврат'.
segments.p2-1-g324: Страстишки is a diminutive plural noun (little passions/ardors) serving as the subject in the nominative. Во мне ('in me') is a prepositional phrase indicating location/possession. Были is the past plural copula 'were' linking subject and predicate. Острые is a plural predicate adjective 'sharp/acute' agreeing with страстишки.
segments.p2-1-g325: Жгучие is another predicate adjective 'burning/fiery' (here continuing to describe the same plural noun). От + genitive (от всегдашней болезненной моей раздражительности) expresses cause — 'from/because of' his constant painful irritability. The word order болезненной моей раздражительности (adjective + possessive + noun) is slightly marked/stylistic but grammatically standard: both adjectives modify раздражительности and моя is a possessive pronoun agreeing with the noun.
segments.p2-1-g326: Порывы is a plural nominative noun 'impulses/outbursts'. Бывали is the imperfective past used idiomatically to indicate habitual or recurring occurrence ('would be / used to be'). Истерические is the predicate adjective 'hysterical' modifying порывы.
segments.p2-1-g327: Со is the variant of 'с' used before certain consonant clusters and means 'with'. Слезами и конвульсиями are both in the instrumental plural and denote accompaniment — 'with tears and convulsions', i.e. the impulses occurred accompanied by those symptoms.
segments.p2-1-g328: Кроме is a preposition meaning 'apart from / besides' and governs the genitive (чтения). Чтения is a noun in the genitive singular 'reading' (the activity), so the whole phrase means 'aside from reading'.
segments.p2-1-g329: Идти is an infinitive 'to go', used in the impersonal existential construction. Было is the impersonal past 'there was', and некуда is an adverbial negative ('nowhere (to)') — together 'идти было некуда' is an idiom meaning 'there was nowhere to go'.
segments.p2-1-g330: То есть is a parenthetical phrase meaning 'that is' or 'in other words' (то + есть). Не было ничего is an existential negation ('there was nothing'), where не + было + ничего produce the meaning 'nothing existed' (Russian uses negative pronoun with negative verb here).
segments.p2-1-g331: Чтобы introduces the subordinate relative/purpose clause here ('that / such that'). Мог я тогда уважать is a past-tense modal construction ('that I could then respect'), with мог (past of мочь) indicating ability/possibility and the pronoun я following the verb (a common, slightly emphatic order). В моем окружающем is a locative phrase meaning 'in my surroundings' (окружающем = the surrounding environment); и connects the next coordinated clause. К чему бы потянуло меня uses к + relative pronoun чему ('toward what') plus the conditional particle бы and the perfective past потянуло (impersonal in form) with object меня — together 'to which I would have been drawn' or 'toward which I would have felt pulled', the бы making it hypothetical/conditional.
segments.p2-1-g266: И is a linking/exclamatory particle here ('and'/'and what'), introducing an emphatic exclamation. Какая is the exclamatory pronoun 'what a...' modifying способность, which is a noun meaning 'capacity/ability.' К + dative (ощущениям) marks the object or domain of that capacity ('for feelings/sensations'). Самым is the superlative/emphatic form used with the dative plural that follows, and противоречивейшим is a superlative/adjectival form (‘most contradictory’); ощущениям is dative plural of ощущение, governed by к. The whole phrase is an inverted exclamatory construction emphasizing astonishment.
segments.p2-1-g267: Я is the subject 'I.' И here functions as 'even/also' (emphatic), so 'I even then...'. Тогда is 'then' (at that time). Был утешен is a compound predicate: был (past auxiliary) + утешен (short passive participle) = 'was comforted' (passive past). Этим is instrumental singular of это and indicates the cause/means ('by that' / 'by this').
segments.p2-1-g268: Да и теперь is a colloquial linking phrase meaning 'and even now' or 'besides, now.' The clause is elliptical: the personal subject and verb are omitted but understood from the previous clause. Тех же мыслей is a genitive-phrase 'of the same thoughts' (тех ... же = those same), used idiomatically to mean 'I am of the same mind / have the same thoughts.' Же is an emphatic particle marking identity ('the same').
segments.p2-1-g269: Оттого-то is a reasonative pair (оттого + emphatic то) meaning 'for that reason/that's why' with added emphasis. У нас means 'among us' / 'we have' (locative/possessive sense). Так и много is an emphatic way to say 'so many' (так ... и). Широких натур is genitive plural governed by много: 'of broad natures' (i.e. 'broad souls/characters').
segments.p2-1-g270: Которые introduces a relative clause referring back to 'натур' ('who'). Даже при самом последнем паденьи is a prepositional/concessive phrase 'even at the very last downfall' (при + noun phrase with самый + последнем for 'the very last'). Никогда не теряют is present-tense habitual/general statement ('never lose'); своего идеала is a possessive phrase ('their own ideal'), with свой referring back to the antecedent 'натур.'
segments.p2-1-g271: И хоть и ... is a concessive construction 'and though/even if ...' (хоть и = 'even if/although'). Пальцем не пошевелят is an idiom 'they will not move a finger' (пальцем — instrumental; пошевелят is perfective future 3rd-person-plural used here as a fixed negative expression meaning 'won't lift a finger'). Для идеала-то has для marking purpose/benefit ('for the ideal') and the enclitic -то on идеала is an emphatic particle highlighting that ideal.
segments.p2-1-g272: Хоть again signals concession ('even if'). Разбойники и воры отъявленные is a nominal enumeration 'notorious brigands and thieves' with отъявленные as a postpositive adjective meaning 'notorious/incorrigible.' The phrase intensifies how extreme these people might be while still preserving the following point.
segments.p2-1-g273: А все-таки (а + всё-таки) = 'and yet / nevertheless.' До слез is an adverbial expression 'to tears' (indicating degree). Свой первоначальный идеал is the object 'their original ideal' (свой reflexive possessive refers back to the subjects). Уважают is present-tense plural 'they respect.' И необыкновенно в душе честны coordinates a second predication: 'and are extraordinarily honest in their souls' (в душе = 'at heart'; честны is the short-form adjective predicate agreeing with a plural subject).
segments.p2-1-g274: Да‑с is a colloquial interjection (да with a following sibilant) equivalent to 'yes' or 'indeed' with a drawn or ironical tone; it's an exclamatory particle rather than a full clause.
segments.p2-1-g275: Только между нами is a parenthetical 'only between ourselves' / 'just between us' (только = 'only', между нами = 'between us'). Самый отъявленный подлец is a superlative noun phrase 'the most notorious scoundrel.' Может быть is a modal periphrasis here ('can/may be'), where может expresses possibility. Совершенно и даже возвышенно modify честен (short-form adjective) to mean 'completely and even loftily/sublimely honest'; в душе ('in the soul') marks the sphere 'at heart.'
segments.p2-1-g276: В то же время = 'at the same time'; нисколько не переставая is a negative gerundial construction 'not in the least ceasing' (нисколько + не + деепричастие переставая), indicating that the previous quality continues without stopping. Быть подлецом is the infinitive construction 'to be a scoundrel' (подлецом in the instrumental marks role/identity). The clause as a whole means 'while in no way ceasing to be a scoundrel.'
segments.p2-1-g350: Развратничал is the past tense, masculine singular of развратничать (an imperfective verb used here to describe habitual or repeated past behaviour: 'indulged in debauchery' or 'behaved lewdly'). The verb precedes the subject (я) for a slightly emphatic or confessional word order; уединенно is an adverb modifying the verb meaning 'in solitude' or 'privately'.
segments.p2-1-g351: По ночам is an adverbial time phrase using по + dative plural to mean 'during the nights' or 'at night[s]' and indicates that the action happened habitually at night.
segments.p2-1-g352: Потаенно is an adverb meaning 'secretly' or 'inwardly'; it further characterizes the manner of the preceding verb (how he acted).
segments.p2-1-g353: Боязливо is an adverb 'timidly' or 'fearfully' modifying the verb phrase; it carries the nuance that his lewd behaviour was accompanied by fear or timidity.
segments.p2-1-g354: Грязно is an adverb here meaning 'dirty' or 'sordidly' (moral or physical filth); it continues the string of manner adverbs describing his conduct.
segments.p2-1-g355: Со + стыдом: со is a variant of the preposition с used before certain consonant clusters and means 'with'; стыдом is the noun 'стыд' in the instrumental case ('with shame'), specifying a companion state accompanying his actions.
segments.p2-1-g356: This is a participial clause modifying стыдом. Не оставлявшим is the past active participle (imperfective) in the instrumental singular agreeing with стыдом and preceded by negation не: 'not leaving.' Меня is the object ('me'). В самые омерзительные минуты is a time expression ('in the very/really revolting moments') with самые as an intensifier. И даже доходившим continues in the same participial instrument, 'and even reaching,' доходившим agreeing with стыдом; в такие минуты 'in such moments' repeats the time frame; до проклятия uses до + genitive to mean 'to the point of a curse' or 'up to a curse.' Together: 'with a shame that did not leave me in the most revolting moments and which even in such moments reached as far as a curse.'
segments.p2-1-g357: Sentence structure: Я (subject) + уж и тогда (temporal/emphatic adverbial cluster: уж 'already', и 'even/also' used here as an intensifier, тогда 'then') + носил (past imperfective носить used as 'carried' or 'harboured' — habitual or ongoing past) + в душе моей (prepositional phrase 'in my soul', with мой agreeing in case/number) + подполье (direct object: neuter noun meaning 'underground' metaphorically). Word order puts the internal location (в душе моей) before the object for emphasis.
segments.p2-1-g358: Боялся is the past tense of бояться ('to be afraid'); the subject я follows the verb for a confessional tone. Ужасно is an adverb intensifying the fear ('terribly').
segments.p2-1-g359: Чтоб introduces a subordinate clause expressing the feared outcome (in colloquial/classical Russian, чтоб + past tense verbs expresses a wish/fear about a possible event). Меня is the object in accusative ('me'). Как-нибудь is a compound adverb ('somehow'/'anyhow') formed from как + нибудь (как here 'how', нибудь an indefinite particle). Не + увидали is the negated past plural form of увидеть ('to see'); after чтоб the past tense plural is used to express possibility or fear ('that I might be seen').
segments.p2-1-g360: Не встретили continues the same subordinate clause (elliptical repetition of чтоб), using the past plural of встретить ('meet/encounter') with negation to list another feared outcome.
segments.p2-1-g361: Не узнали is the final verb in the series, past plural of узнать ('recognize/find out'), again negated and paralleling the previous verbs — all are possibilities he feared might happen to him.
segments.p2-1-g362: Ходил is the past habitual or iterative form of ходить ('used to go' / 'went about'). Же is an emphatic/contrastive particle (here adding a 'after all / though' flavor). Я is the subject. По + dative plural (по разным местам) indicates movement 'to/around various places'; разным agrees in case/number and means 'different/various'; весьма is an adverb 'very' modifying темным; темным is dative plural of тёмный agreeing with местам ('dark'); together this phrase describes the kinds of places he frequented.
segments.p2-1-g495: "несколько лет" is a noun phrase meaning "several years" (несколько + genitive of год). The trailing "-с" is a colloquial/emphatic particle attached after the phrase (an idiosyncratic, expressive marker here) and does not change the grammatical role of the phrase; the whole is an exclamation indicating duration. Word order is the normal quantifier + noun.
segments.p2-1-g496: Subject phrase "Злоба моя" places the noun before the possessive pronoun for slight emphasis (literally "malice my"). The two verbs "укреплялась и разрасталась" are imperfective past forms with the reflexive ending -ся, describing ongoing processes in the past ("became stronger" / "grew and spread"). The temporal phrase "с годами" uses the instrumental plural to mean "as the years went by" or "over the years." "даже" is an adverb of emphasis modifying the verbs.
segments.p2-1-g497: "Сначала" is an adverbial phrase meaning "at first" placed before the subject for temporal framing; the comma marks it off as an introductory phrase followed by the subject "я."
segments.p2-1-g498: "потихоньку" is a colloquial adverb meaning "slowly/gradually, little by little"; set off by commas it modifies the manner of the following action and conveys that the action was done quietly or gradually.
segments.p2-1-g499: "начал разузнавать" is a periphrastic past construction: past tense of "начать" (начал) + imperfective infinitive "разузнавать," which indicates he started an ongoing process of inquiring (not a single completed act). "об этом офицере" uses the preposition "об" + prepositional to mean "about that officer" ("этом" points back to the person mentioned before).
segments.p2-1-g500: Impersonal/subject-predicative order: "Трудно" (predicative adverb/short adjective) + dative experiencer "мне" + demonstrative "это" + past copula "было." Literal word order is "Difficult to me this was," idiomatically "It was difficult for me."
segments.p2-1-g501: "потому что" is the subordinating conjunction "because." The clause "я ни с кем не был знаком" uses the negative construction "ни с кем ... не" (literally "with nobody ... not") to mean "I knew nobody / I wasn't acquainted with anyone." "был знаком" is past tense with the short adjective "знаком" meaning "acquainted/familiar."
segments.p2-1-g450: Струсил is a colloquial past-tense verb meaning 'chickened out' (verb before subject gives a slightly brusque or emphatic tone). Я is the subject; тут is an adverb 'here/at this moment' placed after the subject for emphasis. Не из + genitive (трусости) negates the causal phrase 'from cowardice' (literally 'not out of cowardice').
segments.p2-1-g451: А introduces the contrast 'but/rather' and links this clause to the previous negation. Из takes the genitive (безграничнейшего тщеславия) to express cause 'out of/from...'. Безграничнейшего is a superlative-like form in the genitive singular modifying тщеславия ('the most boundless vanity').
segments.p2-1-g452: Я испугался is a past reflexive verb meaning 'I became frightened' (subject follows verb for a colloquial rhythm). Не ... и не того negates two possible causes: 'not (because of) ten vershoks of height and not that (reason)'; вершков росту is a measure phrase ('ten vershoks of height') using genitive after a numeral.
segments.p2-1-g453: Что introduces the subordinate clause describing what he feared. Меня is the object 'me' (accusative). Больно is an adverb here meaning 'painfully/very badly' modifying прибьют (3rd-person plural future 'they will beat/kill' in context 'beat'); и coordinates a second verb спустят ('they will lower'), with в окно indicating direction ('through/out the window').
segments.p2-1-g454: Физической храбрости is a noun phrase in the genitive used with хватило to indicate the kind/amount that would suffice: 'physical (bodily) courage.' The phrase is an object/measure phrase rather than a nominative subject.
segments.p2-1-g455: Право here is a parenthetical/exclamatory particle meaning 'indeed' or 'really' (used to emphasize the following statement), not the noun 'right.'
segments.p2-1-g456: Хватило is the past tense impersonal form of хватить meaning 'would have been enough' when combined with the conditional particle бы (placed after the verb). This expresses a counterfactual or hypothetical: 'there would have been sufficient [physical courage].'
segments.p2-1-g457: Но introduces a contrast 'but.' Нравственной храбрости is in the genitive (as required by недостало) and means 'moral (ethical) courage.' Недостало is an impersonal past form meaning 'was lacking' or 'there was not enough,' so the clause reads 'but moral courage was lacking.'
segments.p2-1-g458: 'Я испугался того' uses 'испугаться' (to become afraid) with the genitive demonstrative 'того' as an anticipatory object; it prepares for the following 'что' clause ('that...'). The verb is past tense, first person singular.
segments.p2-1-g459: The subordinate clause begins with 'что' ('that'). 'Меня' is the accusative object meaning 'me', and 'все присутствующие' ('all those present') is the nominative plural subject; word order 'что меня все присутствующие' stresses 'me' as the object of their understanding/ridicule.
segments.p2-1-g460: 'Начиная с ... до ...' is a range expression 'starting from X up to Y.' 'С' takes genitive ('нахала маркера') and 'до' also governs the genitive ('последнего ... чиновничишки'). Adjectives agree in case, and the phrase enumerates the spectrum of people present.
segments.p2-1-g461: A participial (adjectival) phrase modifying the preceding noun: 'тут же увивавшегося' — 'the one who was immediately fawning.' 'Увивавшегося' is a past imperfective reflexive participle agreeing in case/gender with the noun it describes; 'тут же' here functions adverbially ('then/at once/immediately').
segments.p2-1-g462: 'С воротником из сала' is an instrumental prepositional phrase 'with a collar of lard.' 'С' + instrumental 'воротником' indicates accompaniment/feature, and 'из' + genitive ('сала') specifies material ('made of lard').
segments.p2-1-g463: Two coordinated future verbs with negation: 'не поймут и осмеют' = 'will not understand and will ridicule.' 'Поймут' and 'осмеют' are future tense, 3rd person plural; 'не' negates the first verb and pragmatically applies to the expectation of both actions.
segments.p2-1-g464: Temporal clause introduced by 'когда' ('when'). The first verb is periphrastic future 'буду протестовать' (future of imperfective 'протестовать' = 'I will be protesting' / 'I will protest') and the second is perfective future 'заговорю' ('I will begin to speak'); the two different future forms indicate ongoing action vs. the initiation of speech. 'С ними языком литературным' uses the instrumental 'языком' to indicate 'in the language/style of' and 'литературным' characterizes that style.
segments.p2-1-g465: "Потому что" introduces a subordinate clause giving the reason. "о пункте чести" is a prepositional phrase (о + noun in prepositional case) meaning "about a point of honour," with чести in the genitive modifying пункте.
segments.p2-1-g466: "то есть" is an explanatory formula meaning "that is/namely"; the rest "не о чести" negates the following notion, i.e. "not about honour." Note that есть here is the copular element inside the idiom "то есть."
segments.p2-1-g467: The conjunction "а" signals contrast: "but rather about a point of honour." The parenthetical French "point d'honneur" repeats the same idea as a loanword. "пункте чести" again is prepositional + genitive.
segments.p2-1-g468: "у нас до сих пор" is a fixed phrase meaning "among us up to now." The construction "иначе ... нельзя" is impersonal: "it is impossible to do it otherwise." "ведь" adds emphasis/argumentative colouring and "и" here reinforces the impossibility ("even/indeed").
segments.p2-1-g469: "как" introduces the comparison/limit "other than," and "языком литературным" is instrumental case marking the manner/medium "in literary language/style." Note the adjective follows the noun (языком литературным) — a slightly marked word order for emphasis.
segments.p2-1-g396: Черт знает что is an idiomatic exclamation ('who the devil knows', i.e. 'anything'); grammatically, что introduces a fused exclamatory/indefinite object ("what/whatever") that combines with дал бы я (conditional past meaning 'I would have given'). Бы is the conditional particle attached to дал, and тогда ('then') gives the time; за governs the object given in exchange ('for'). Настоящую is the feminine accusative adjective modifying the implied noun 'ссору' that follows.
segments.p2-1-g397: Более ('more') is a comparative adverb modifying the following adjective; правильную is feminine accusative singular of правильный ('proper/correct') and directly modifies the understood noun ссору (explicit next segment). Word order places multiple adjectives before the noun for emphasis.
segments.p2-1-g398: Another comparative phrase: более modifies приличную (feminine accusative of приличный 'decent/respectable'), continuing the list of qualities the speaker desires. The trailing comma shows these are stacked attributes of the same (feminine) noun.
segments.p2-1-g399: A single emphatic comparative 'более' ('more'), used here to continue the enumerative pattern and heighten the speaker's longing; it stands alone for rhetorical effect before the following parenthetical.
segments.p2-1-g400: Так сказать is a parenthetical idiom ('so to speak'); так ('so/thus') + the infinitive сказать ('to say') softens or qualifies the following adjective, marking it as figurative or tentative.
segments.p2-1-g401: Литературную is the feminine accusative of литературный ('literary' or 'bookish/affected') and completes the series of adjectives describing the implied noun 'ссору' (quarrel). The exclamation mark shows strong feeling; the adjective here carries a slightly ironic or stylistic shade.
segments.p2-1-g402: Со мной is the prepositional/instrumental phrase ('with me' / 'toward me') marking the patient of поступили; поступили is the past plural of поступить meaning 'acted' or 'treated' (here intransitive with an implied agent 'they'). Как с мухой is a simile ('as/like with a fly') showing how they treated the speaker.
segments.p2-1-g403: Был is the simple past of быть ('was') and introduces the description. Этот офицер ('this/that officer') is the subject; вершков десяти is a genitive-quantity phrase ('of ten vershoks') modifying росту, an archaic unit of length used here to indicate his height. Росту is used in a colloquial measure construction meaning 'of a height' (literally 'of height').
segments.p2-1-g404: Я же uses же as a contrastive particle ('I, however') setting the speaker against the officer; человек низенький и истощенный is a nominal predicate where человек ('person/man') is qualified by two adjectives: низенький ('short/diminutive', colloquial diminutive form) and истощенный (past participle used adjectivally, 'emaciated').
segments.p2-1-g405: Ссора is the noun 'quarrel' in the nominative and stands alone here as the topic or subject of the sentence; the comma signals the following concessive/parenthetical comment.
segments.p2-1-g406: Впрочем is a discourse particle meaning 'however' or 'nevertheless' that qualifies the statement that follows; it often softens or limits the preceding idea.
segments.p2-1-g407: Была в моих руках is a figurative phrase: была (past of 'to be') + в моих руках (literally 'in my hands') meaning 'was under my control' or 'in my power.' В governs the plural locative/instrumental руках (hands), and моих is the genitive/possessive form 'my'.
segments.p2-1-g408: Стоило + infinitive (попротестовать) is an impersonal construction meaning 'it would have been enough to...' or 'all it would have taken was to object a bit.' Here стоило conveys sufficiency rather than monetary cost.
segments.p2-1-g409: И is the coordinating conjunction 'and,' linking the clause about what it would have taken to the consequent clause.
segments.p2-1-g410: Конечно is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally,' marking that what follows is expected or obvious.
segments.p2-1-g411: Меня бы спустили в окно is a conditional passive/impersonal construction: меня (accusative 'me') is the patient, бы is the conditional particle, and спустили is past plural indicating 'they would have lowered/let down.' В окно ('through/out the window') gives the direction/place of lowering; together the phrase means 'they would have lowered me out the window.'
segments.p2-1-g412: A short, elliptical past-tense clause. 'Но' is the adversative conjunction 'but'; 'я' is the explicit subject placed before the verb for emphasis; 'раздумал' is past masculine singular of 'раздумать' meaning 'changed one's mind' (perfective, completed action); 'и' links a second verb; 'предпочел' is past masculine singular of the perfective verb 'предпочесть' ('preferred/decided [to do otherwise]') with its complement omitted, so the preference is understood from context (i.e. he preferred not to continue).
segments.p2-1-g415: 'Озлобленно' is an adverb formed from the adjective 'озлобленный' meaning 'resentfully' and here modifies the following infinitive; 'стушеваться' is a reflexive infinitive (verb + -ся) meaning 'to hang back / to back down / to become abashed'. Together they form the infinitival complement of 'предпочел' in the previous sentence ('he preferred to ...').
segments.p2-1-g416: 'Вышел' is past masculine singular of 'выйти' ('went out/left'); the pronoun 'я' follows the verb (a normal word-order variant used for tone/emphasis). The participial adjectives 'смущенный и взволнованный' are predicative modifiers in the nominative that describe the subject's state on leaving (they agree with the subject and follow it syntactically).
segments.p2-1-g417: 'Прямо домой' is an adverbial phrase modifying the motion verb 'вышел' (implied 'I went straight home'). 'Прямо' intensifies direction ('straight/directly') and 'домой' is the directional adverb 'towards home'. The verb is not repeated because this phrase attaches to the previous clause.
segments.p2-1-g418: Inverted order: the past verb 'продолжал' (masculine singular past of 'продолжать' 'continued') precedes its subject 'мой развратик' for stylistic effect; 'на другой день' sets the time ('on the next day'). 'Ещё робче' is a comparative adverbial phrase ('even timider') modifying how the 'развратик' continued.
segments.p2-1-g419: A string of comparative adjectives/adverbs ('забитее и грустнее') that further describe the state of that 'развратик' — both are comparative forms (more downtrodden / more sad) coordinated by 'и'. They agree in sense with the subject and continue the comparative series begun by 'еще робче'.
segments.p2-1-g420: 'Чем прежде' is the standard comparative particle plus adverb meaning 'than before'; it completes the comparison begun by the comparative adjectives in the previous segment.
segments.p2-1-g421: 'Как будто' is a two-word conjunction meaning 'as if' / 'as though'; 'со слезой на глазах' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'with a tear in (his) eyes' — 'со' is the variant of 'с' used for euphony before the following phonetic environment, 'слезой' is instrumental singular of 'слеза' ('with a tear'), and 'на глазах' uses the preposition 'на' with the plural 'глазах' to indicate location ('in his eyes').
segments.p2-1-g422: The dash marks a slight pause; 'а' resumes the clause with contrast. 'Все-таки' (here written as 'все — таки' split by punctuation) is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning 'nevertheless / still / after all', and 'продолжал' repeats the past-tense verb 'continued' (the subject is again understood to be the 'развратик').
segments.p2-1-g423: 'Не думайте' is a negative imperative (plural/formal) formed with 'не' + imperative 'думайте', meaning 'don't suppose' or 'don't think (that)'. It's an instruction to the reader not to form a particular opinion that will be given in the following subordinate clause.
segments.p2-1-g424: 'впрочем' is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'however' / 'mind you', used to soften or qualify the previous imperative; it does not change the clause structure and signals a qualification.
segments.p2-1-g425: This is a subordinate 'that'-clause introduced by 'что' reporting the content of 'Не думайте'. 'Я' is the subject; 'струсил' is past tense, imperfective/colloquial perfective sense here meaning 'chickened out' or 'backed down'; it takes 'офицера' (accusative) as the person he backed away from. 'От' + genitive ('трусости') expresses cause: 'because of cowardice.'
segments.p2-1-g426: 'Я никогда не был трусом в душе' is a negative past statement: 'I was never a coward at heart.' 'Никогда' (never) precedes the negated auxiliary 'не был' (was not). 'В душе' is a frozen locative phrase meaning 'in my soul / at heart' (inner disposition).
segments.p2-1-g427: Concessive clause introduced by 'хотя' ('although'). 'Беспрерывно' (continuously) modifies the imperfective past verb 'трусил' indicating repeated/ongoing behavior: 'I continuously chickened out.' 'На деле' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'in practice' / 'in deed' and qualifies how he behaved.
segments.p2-1-g428: 'Но - подождите смеяться' is an interruption marked by a dash. 'Подождите' is the plural/formal imperative 'wait' or 'hold on', and the infinitive 'смеяться' names the action the audience might take ('to laugh'). Together: 'but—wait before you laugh.'
segments.p2-1-g429: 'На это есть объяснение' is an existential/presentational clause: 'there is an explanation for this.' 'На это' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'for this' (purpose/target), 'есть' is the existential 'there is', and 'объяснение' is the noun 'explanation.'
segments.p2-1-g430: 'У меня на все есть объяснение' uses the possessive construction 'у меня есть' = 'I have.' 'На все' indicates scope ('for everything'). The structure literally reads 'At me for everything there is an explanation' → 'I have an explanation for everything.'
segments.p2-1-g431: 'Будьте уверены' is a plural/formal imperative: 'be sure' / 'rest assured.' 'Будьте' is the imperative of 'быть' and 'уверены' is the short-form adjective 'certain/sure' used predicatively.
segments.p2-1-g483: Full sentence: Subject 'Я' (I) + two adverbs 'часто потом' (often, later) set temporal frequency and sequence before the verb. 'Встречал' is the imperfective past tense, first person singular, indicating repeated or habitual encounters in the past. 'Этого офицера' is the animate accusative (demonstrative 'этого' + noun) meaning 'that officer.' 'На улице' is a locative prepositional phrase 'on the street.' The coordinate clause after 'и' uses the perfective past 'заприметил' (I noticed/took notice), with 'хорошо' as an intensifier and 'его' as the object; the imperfective (встречал) followed by a perfective (заприметил) shows habitual meeting followed by a completed act of noticing.
segments.p2-1-g484: 'Не знаю' is the present tense, first person singular with negation: 'I don't know.' The adverb/particle 'только' here narrows the scope (''the only thing' / 'only') and heads an elliptical construction (i.e., 'The only thing I don't know is ...').
segments.p2-1-g485: 'Узнавал' is the imperfective past tense of узнавать ('recognize/come to know'), used here in an indirect question introduced by the enclitic 'ли' ('whether'). The word order is Subject 'он' (he) + verb + object 'меня' (me, accusative). The imperfective implies the question of recognition as an ongoing/indefinite action rather than a single perfective event.
segments.p2-1-g486: 'Должно быть' is an impersonal modal phrase meaning 'probably' or 'it must be,' formed from the short/neuter modal adjective 'должно' plus the infinitive 'быть.' It expresses conjecture rather than obligation in this usage.
segments.p2-1-g487: A one-word negative response 'нет' meaning 'no' or 'there was not' here, standing alone as the answer to the preceding question (i.e., 'probably not').
segments.p2-1-g488: 'Заключаю' is first person singular present of заключать in the sense 'I infer / I conclude.' The preposition 'по' governs the dative, so 'по некоторым признакам' means 'from/according to certain signs' with 'некоторым признакам' in the dative plural, indicating the evidence on which the speaker bases his inference.
segments.p2-1-g489: 'Но' is the conjunction 'but.' The combination 'я-то' attaches the contrastive particle 'то' to 'я' to emphasize or contrast the speaker ('as for me' / 'I, at least'), a colloquial emphatic construction.
segments.p2-1-g490: The repeated 'я,' set off by commas and a dash, is a rhetorical repetition for emphasis (the speaker doubling the pronoun to stress his personal involvement). Grammatically it remains the first-person singular subject.
segments.p2-1-g491: 'Смотрел' is the imperfective past of смотреть ('looked/was looking' or 'regarded'); 'на него' uses the preposition 'на' + accusative 'него' meaning 'at him.' 'Со' is the variant of 'с' used before certain consonant clusters and here means 'with,' governing the instrumental nouns 'злобою' and 'ненавистью' ('with malice and hatred'). Note the old-fashioned instrumental ending 'злобою.'
segments.p2-1-g492: 'И' is 'and' linking to the next clause; 'так' is an adverb 'thus/that way'; 'продолжалось' is the past, third-person neuter of продолжаться ('it continued/it went on'), describing that the described behavior/state persisted. The reflexive verb expresses that the situation continued by itself.
segments.p2-1-g363: Раз here is a narrative introductory adverb meaning “once” (one time). It stands alone with a comma to begin the anecdote and sets the temporal frame for the action to follow.
segments.p2-1-g364: Проходя is the adverbial participle (деепричастие) ‘passing/while passing,’ indicating a concurrent action. Ночью is an adverbial temporal modifier (‘at night’). Мимо + genitive (мимо одного трактиришка) is a prepositional phrase meaning ‘past/by’ a small/diminutive tavern (трактиришка is colloquial/diminutive), the whole phrase giving the circumstance of motion.
segments.p2-1-g365: Я увидел is the main past-tense clause ‘I saw/caught sight of.’ В + accusative (в освещенное окно) is used here with увидеть to mean seeing into/through the lit window; освещенное is a past participle/adjective ‘lit/illuminated’ modifying окно.
segments.p2-1-g366: Как introduces reported content of what was seen (“how/that”); господа is the plural noun ‘gentlemen’ (used ironically/contextually as ‘men’). Киями is the instrumental plural of кий — ‘with cues,’ indicating the weapon/instrument of the fight. Подрались is past plural of подраться ‘fought.’ У + genitive (у биллиарда) means ‘at/by the billiard (table).’ The second как begins a coordinated subordinate clause ‘and how…’ Один из них with из (here “of/out of”) identifies one person of that group; в окно + accusative indicates direction ‘out through the window’; спустили is past plural (they let/lowered [him] down), here impersonal/agentless in the narration.
segments.p2-1-g367: В другое время is a temporal phrase ‘at another time’ (в here indicates time). Мне бы ... стало is the conditional/impersonal construction meaning ‘I would have felt’; бы is the conditional particle, and мерзко стало is an impersonal predicate ‘became/was unpleasant/disgusting to me.’
segments.p2-1-g368: Но тогда такая вдруг минута нашла is an idiomatic sequence: но тогда ‘but then,’ такая вдруг минута ‘such a sudden moment,’ and нашла (from найти) used impersonally = ‘befell/overtook (me)’ or ‘came upon [me].’ The word order emphasizes the suddenness and quality of the moment.
segments.p2-1-g369: Что introduces the result clause ‘that ...’ Я позавидовал takes a dative object (позавидовал чему/кому). Этому спущенному господину is a dative phrase ‘to that gentleman who had been let down’ — спущенному is a past passive participle modifying господину, and the demonstrative этому points to that very person.
segments.p2-1-g370: И до того позавидовал intensifies the previous verb: и ‘and’ plus до того ‘to such a degree/extent’ expresses that the envy was very strong; позавидовал repeats the past action of envying.
segments.p2-1-g371: Что even introduces the continuing result: что даже в трактир вошел = ‘that I even entered the tavern.’ Даже ‘even’ emphasizes the unexpectedness; в + accusative (в трактир) marks motion into the tavern; вошел is past tense ‘entered’ (I entered).
segments.p2-1-g372: В биллиардную is a terse locative/motion phrase ‘into the billiard-room’ (биллиардную is an elliptical adjective/noun for the billiard-room in accusative, with в marking motion into).
segments.p2-1-g373: Авось is a colloquial particle meaning ‘perhaps/maybe’ or expressing reliance on chance; here it introduces the narrator’s hopeful/hedging thought.
segments.p2-1-g374: Дескать is a parenthetical/discourse particle often used to report one’s thought or reported speech ('as if to say' / ‘he thought’); here it softens or frames the following interior remark.
segments.p2-1-g375: И я подерусь is the first-person singular future of the perfective подраться ‘I will fight’ (подерусь). И я adds the narrator as an additional actor; the whole phrase expresses his fanciful hope/expectation that he might fight too.
segments.p2-1-g376: И меня тоже из окна спустят completes the quoted thought: и ‘and’ + меня (accusative) ‘me’ + тоже ‘also’ + из окна ‘out of the window’ (из + genitive) + спустят is 3rd person plural future ‘they will let down’ (agent omitted). It expresses the narrator’s imagined outcome that he, too, would be lowered out the window.
segments.p2-1-g377: Я is the subject pronoun “I.” Не is the negation. Был is the past-tense copula of быть. Пьян is the short-form adjective “drunk” (masculine) used as a predicate: “I was not drunk.”
segments.p2-1-g378: Но is the conjunction “but.” Что here is an interrogative/exclamatory “what” introducing a rhetorical question. Прикажете is the 2nd-person plural future form of приказать (“you will order/command”); in the idiom что прикажете делать it functions as a polite/formal way to say “what are you to do / what can you do.” Делать is the infinitive complement “to do.”
segments.p2-1-g379: До такой is a prepositional phrase meaning “to such (an extent).” Ведь is a particle adding the sense “after all” / “you know.” Истерики is the genitive of истерика (“hysteria”), here used with до такой to indicate degree (“to such a hysteria/state”). Может is a modal verb “may/can” indicating possibility. Тоска is the nominative subject “melancholy/longing,” and заесть (infinitive) literally means “to gnaw at / to eat away (at)” — together: “melancholy may gnaw one into such hysteria.”
segments.p2-1-g380: A short declarative sentence. 'Но' is a contrastive conjunction ('but'). 'Ничем' is the instrumental form of 'ничто' used idiomatically here with 'обошлось' to mean 'without anything' / 'it turned out to be nothing.' 'Обошлось' is past tense, perfective, meaning 'to manage/get by' or 'to turn out (without something).' The whole phrase means that whatever trouble was feared did not happen.
segments.p2-1-g381: Оказалось is the impersonal past form (neuter) of оказаться used as a sentence-opening connective meaning 'it turned out' or 'it proved to be'; it introduces the following subordinate clause.
segments.p2-1-g382: This is a subordinate clause introduced by the conjunction что ('that'). я is the nominative subject. The particle и here has an emphatic sense 'even/also' (emphasizing the impossibility). в + accusative окно (в окно) marks the direction/target of прыгнуть (the infinitive), and the clitic particle то after окно (в окно-то) gives contrastive emphasis ('even the window' / 'of all things, the window'). прыгнуть is the infinitive governed by the predicative adjective способен ('able'); the finite predicate is the short-form adjective способен with negation не before it: не способен = 'not able'.
segments.p2-1-g383: и connects this clause to what follows ('and'), я is the subject and ушел is the perfective past of уйти meaning 'left' (completed action). не + деепричастие подравшись (perfective gerund of подраться) functions as an adverbial participle meaning 'without having fought' (negated gerund indicates absence of that action).
segments.p2-1-g384: The verb Осадил is a past, perfective, transitive form meaning roughly 'checked', 'put down' or 'rebuked' (here physical/social check). It comes first, followed by the object меня ('me'), then locative там ('there') and the temporal/extent phrase с первого же шагу ('from the very first step')—же is an emphatic particle ('very/right'), and шагу is an older/stylistic form of the genitive 'шага' used after с. The subject один офицер appears at the end for stylistic emphasis (inverted word order).
segments.p2-1-g385: Я стоял is past imperfective 'I was standing'; у + биллиарда indicates location 'by/at the billiard (table)' (биллиарда here means the billiard table). по неведению is an adverbial phrase 'through/out of ignorance' (по + noun expresses cause/reason here). заслонял дорогу is imperfective past 'was blocking/obstructing the way' with дорогу as the direct object.
segments.p2-1-g386: а introduces contrast ('but/and'), тому is the dative 'to that one' meaning 'for him' or 'he' in effect, and надо было + infinitive (пройти) is an impersonal past-tense necessity construction meaning 'he had to pass' or 'it was necessary for him to pass'.
segments.p2-1-g387: он взял меня за плечи is literal: он (he) + взял (perfective past 'grabbed/took') + меня (direct object) + за плечи ('by the shoulders'—за with the accusative here marks the manner/place of the grip). и молча adds the adverb молча 'silently/without words'.
segments.p2-1-g388: This parenthetical fragment contains two negated perfective gerunds: не предуведомив ('not having given prior notice') and не объяснившись ('not having explained himself'). As perfective gerunds they indicate actions that were not performed before the main action; the commas/dashes mark them as an incidental remark explaining how he acted.
segments.p2-1-g389: переставил (perfective past of переставить) is the main verb here meaning 'moved' or 'shifted' (physically relocated) and takes меня as its object. с того места uses с + genitive (того места) to mark the source 'from that place/spot'.
segments.p2-1-g390: This is a relative clause introduced by где 'where' describing the place: где я стоял = 'where I was standing.' It modifies того места in the preceding phrase.
segments.p2-1-g391: на другое uses the preposition на plus an accusative/neuter adjective другое to mark the destination 'onto another (place/spot)'; together with переставил ... с того места на другое it forms a full 'moved me from that place to another' construction.
segments.p2-1-g392: а сам ('but he himself' or 'and he himself') emphasizes the agent; прошел is the perfective past of пройти 'passed by'. как будто introduces a concessive 'as if' clause; и не заметив contains the particle и (here 'even'/'also' giving additional emphasis) plus the perfective gerund не заметив 'not having noticed' (i.e. 'as if he hadn't even noticed').
segments.p2-1-g393: Я бы даже побои простил uses бы to mark the conditional ('I would'), даже for emphasis 'even', and побои (plural) 'beatings' as the object; простил is the masculine past of простить 'forgive'. The construction expresses a hypothetical willingness to forgive being beaten.
segments.p2-1-g394: но никак не мог простить того: но introduces contrast 'but'; никак ... не мог is an emphatic negative past 'could in no way' or 'simply could not'; простить is the infinitive 'forgive'; того is a genitive demonstrative 'that' referring forward to the clause that follows ('the fact that …').
segments.p2-1-g395: что introduces the content clause 'that he moved me and so completely did not notice (it/me)'. он (he) is the subject; меня (me) is the object of переставил (perfective past 'moved'); и так окончательно не заметил combines и так ('and so' / 'in such a way') + окончательно ('completely, definitively') + не заметил (past perfective 'did not notice'), stressing that he moved him and totally failed to notice him.
segments.p2-1-g432: 'О' here is an interjection (an exclamatory Oh); it expresses emotion and stands alone before the conditional clause. This is not the preposition 'о/about' despite the identical spelling.
segments.p2-1-g433: This is the protasis of a counterfactual wish. 'Если б' is a colloquial contraction of 'если бы' (if only / if), with 'б' marking the conditional/subjunctive. 'Этот офицер был' is past tense (быть → был) used in the subjunctive sense ('had been'); 'из тех' means 'one of those (kinds)'. Word order is standard: condition particle + demonstrative + noun + past of 'to be' + prepositional phrase indicating membership in a group.
segments.p2-1-g434: 'Которые' introduces a relative clause modifying 'тех'. 'Соглашались' is the imperfect past (they used to agree / would agree) describing habitual willingness; it's imperfective. 'Выходить на дуэль' is a fixed verbal phrase meaning 'to go out for a duel / to accept a duel' — here infinitive 'выходить' expresses the action they would consent to. 'На' marks purpose/target ('for' a duel).
segments.p2-1-g435: 'Но нет' is a short disconfirming phrase: 'but no' or 'not so.' 'Но' is the adversative conjunction 'but', and 'нет' here functions as 'no' / 'not so' to reject the preceding wish or possibility.
segments.p2-1-g436: 'Это был' = 'he/it was' (past of 'быть'). 'Именно' is an emphatic particle meaning 'exactly / precisely.' 'Из тех господ' repeats the 'one of those gentlemen' construction: 'из' + genitive plural 'тех господ' (the genitive after 'из'). The parenthetical 'увы' ('alas') is an interjection expressing regret and interrupts the clause.
segments.p2-1-g437: 'Давно исчезнувших' is a past active participle phrase in genitive plural modifying 'господ' (it agrees with 'господ' in case and number because of the preceding 'из'). 'Давно' (adv) intensifies the participle: 'long-vanished' or 'long since disappeared.'
segments.p2-1-g438: 'Которые' resumes the relative clause. 'Предпочитали' is the imperfect past 'preferred / would rather' describing habitual preference. 'Действовать' is infinitive 'to act'; here it takes an instrument in the next word. 'Киями' is the instrumental plural form (instrumental case) of 'кий(?)' and means 'by/with cudgels or clubs' — an older or colloquial word; the instrumental marks the means by which they acted. The particle 'или' ('or') connects the alternatives.
segments.p2-1-g439: 'Как поручик Пирогов у Гоголя' is a comparative example 'like Lieutenant Pirogov in Gogol.' 'Как' = 'as / like'; 'поручик' is a military rank 'lieutenant'; 'Пирогов' here is a proper name (Gogol character). 'У Гоголя' means 'in the works of Gogol' or 'according to Gogol.'
segments.p2-1-g440: 'По начальству' means 'by/through the authorities' or 'according to / via one's superiors.' 'По' with dative (here 'начальству') often marks manner or channel ('by means of / through') when referring to officials or procedure.
segments.p2-1-g441: 'На дуэль не выходили' is a negated habitual past: 'they did not go out to duel' or 'they did not take part in duels.' The particle 'же' adds contrast/emphasis ('however' / 'on the other hand'). 'Выходили' is imperfect past plural 'would go / used to go', here negated by 'не'.
segments.p2-1-g442: 'А' contrasts the previous clause ('but/and'). 'С нашим братом' literally 'with our brother' — here idiomatic for 'with our sort/fellow' (i.e. men like him). 'С' is the comitative 'with', and 'нашим братом' is in instrumental case as a standard expression meaning 'our fellow / our sort.'
segments.p2-1-g443: 'С штафиркой' uses 'с' + instrumental to indicate accompaniment or with something; 'штафиркой' is a diminutive/colloquial noun (instrumental) referring to a small fine or petty penalty — i.e., 'with his little fine' (an identifying trait of that 'brother').
segments.p2-1-g444: 'Считали бы' is the conditional construction: 'they would have considered.' The particle 'бы' marks the conditional. 'Дуэль во всяком случае неприличною' is the object clause: 'a duel indecent in any case' — 'во всяком случае' = 'in any case' and 'неприличною' is the predicative adjective in the (archaic) instrumental/old adjective ending, meaning 'indecent / improper.'
segments.p2-1-g445: 'Да и вообще' is a colloquial connective: 'and indeed, generally.' 'Считали дуэль чем-то немыслимым' repeats the idea with 'чем-то' ('something' — instrumental with indefinite particle) meaning 'considered dueling to be something unthinkable.' The phrase 'чем-то' is the instrumental indefinite form meaning 'something,' and 'немыслимым' is a predicative adjective 'unthinkable.'
segments.p2-1-g446: 'Вольнодумным' is an additional predicative adjective (instrumental) describing how they judged dueling: 'freethinking' or 'liberal-minded' (used pejoratively here). It's part of a series of adjectives linked by commas.
segments.p2-1-g447: 'Французским' is another adjective in the series (instrumental): 'French' — i.e., they considered dueling to be something French (and thus foreign/undesirable).
segments.p2-1-g448: 'А сами обижали довольно' turns to a contrast: 'yet they themselves offended/wronged [people] sufficiently.' 'А' = 'but/yet'; 'сами' emphasizes 'they themselves'; 'обижали' is imperfect past 'they offended / treated unfairly'; 'довольно' = 'quite / enough.'
segments.p2-1-g449: 'Особенно в случае десяти вершков росту' specifies a circumstance: 'especially in the case of ten vershoks of height.' 'В случае' = 'in the case of'; 'десяти вершков' is a genitive-quantity phrase (old Russian unit 'vershok'); 'росту' is genitive of 'рост' (height) — altogether 'especially when the person was ten vershoks tall.'
segments.p2-1-g536: "Письмо было так сочинено," is a past passive construction: "было" (past auxiliary) + short passive participle "сочинено" = "was composed." "так" modifies the manner ("in such a way / so"); the whole phrase sets up a manner/result clause that the following "что..." will specify.
segments.p2-1-g537: "что если б офицер чуть-чуть понимал "прекрасное и высокое"," is a subordinate conditional introduced by "что" + "если б" ("if" + conditional particle "б"). "чуть-чуть" is a doubled adverb meaning "a little/slightly." "понимал" is imperfective past (here in a counterfactual conditional: "had understood/even understood a little"). The quoted neuter adjectives "прекрасное и высокое" are abstract nouns meaning "the beautiful and the lofty."
segments.p2-1-g538: "то непременно бы прибежал ко мне," contains the correlative result-marker "то" (as in "если... то...") + adverb "непременно" (certainly). "бы" is the conditional particle; together with the past form "прибежал" it yields the English conditional "would have rushed/ran." "ко мне" is the preposition "ко" (a variant of «к» used before certain pronouns) + dative pronoun "мне" = "to me."
segments.p2-1-g539: "чтоб броситься мне на шею и предложить свою дружбу." uses "чтоб" (colloquial/slightly contracted form of "чтобы") to introduce an infinitival purpose/result clause: "to/so as to..." "броситься" is a perfective reflexive infinitive ("to throw oneself / to rush toward"); with dative "мне" + "на шею" it means "to throw himself on my neck" (i.e. embrace). The second infinitive "предложить" coordinates the action: "and offer his friendship;" "свою" agrees with the (implied) subject "he."
segments.p2-1-g540: This exclamatory clause "И как бы это было хорошо!" uses "как" as an exclamative "how," and "бы" with the past form "было" produces the counterfactual/optative sense "would be." The phrase expresses a wish or imagined pleasure: "how good that would be!" The initial "И" simply links or heightens the feeling.
segments.p2-1-g541: "Мы бы так зажили!" is a short conditional/exclamatory sentence: "Мы" (we) + conditional particle "бы" with the past perfective "зажили" (here meaning "to begin to live / to start living/ to prosper"). "так" is an adverb "so/that way/like that." Together it expresses a wished-for result: "We would have begun to live like that!"
segments.p2-1-g542: This terse exclamation "так зажили!" repeats the adverb "так" + past perfective "зажили" to emphasize the imagined result. The verb lacks the conditional particle here but the elliptical, exclamatory context carries the same counterfactual sense as the previous sentence: "(We) would have lived like that!"
segments.p2-1-g559: Мороз is the grammatical subject ('frost' or 'a chill'). по коже is an adverbial phrase meaning 'along/over the skin' (по + dative 'коже'), placed after the subject. пробирает is a 3rd-person singular imperfective present (пробирать) used impersonally here: literally 'penetrates' or idiomatically 'a chill runs through (one)' — together the clause means 'a chill runs over my skin.'
segments.p2-1-g560: как is used as a temporal/subordinating particle 'when/whenever' (colloquial 'как вспомню' = 'whenever I remember'). вспомню is 1st-person singular of the perfective вспоmнить and here functions like a present/future-general 'I remember' or 'I recall' to introduce the subordinate clause.
segments.p2-1-g561: что acts as the interrogative/relative 'what' here ('what would/might have come of it'). бы is the conditional particle marking the potential/contrary-to-fact meaning. могло is the neuter past form of мочь used modally ('could/might'), and выйти is used idiomatically 'to turn out / to come of it.' Together they form a potential subordinate clause: 'what might have turned out.'
segments.p2-1-g562: если + б (here contracted 'если б') introduces the unreal conditional 'if (had)'. я is the explicit subject 'I' (pronoun often included for emphasis), and послал is the past tense masculine of послать ('sent'). The whole clause expresses an unreal/hypothetical event: 'if I had sent (it).' The direct object (the letter) is omitted but understood from context.
segments.p2-1-g563: И is a simple coordinating particle 'and' used here as a discourse connector; вдруг is an adverb 'suddenly.' The two together form a short exclamatory/transition fragment signaling an abrupt change.
segments.p2-1-g566: и вдруг repeats the suddenness ('and suddenly'). я is the subject 'I.' отомстил is the past tense masculine singular of the perfective отомстить 'to avenge oneself' (i.e., 'I took revenge'). самым простейшим is a superlative/emphatic phrase in the instrumental case ('in the very simplest [manner]') that grammatically modifies the coming noun образ(ом).
segments.p2-1-g567: самым гениальнейшим образом continues the instrumental phrase: две степени усиления ('the very most ingenious') plus образом (instrumental of образ) meaning 'in the manner/way.' The double superlative (самым ... гениальнейшим) is emphatic: 'in the simplest, most brilliant fashion.'
segments.p2-1-g568: Меня is the accusative object 'me' placed before the verb for emphasis/inverted word order. вдруг is the adverb 'suddenly.' осенила is the past tense feminine singular of the perfective осенить ('to strike' as of a thought) and agrees with the delayed subject мысль (feminine); literally 'a thought struck me.' пресветлая is an intensified adjective ('most radiant' or 'very bright') modifying мысль, the nominative subject that follows the verb.
segments.p2-1-g514: Раз (an adverb/particle) here means “once” or “one time” and combines with the time expression поутру (“in the morning”) to mean “one morning.” The phrase is fronted and set off by a comma as a temporal adverbial.
segments.p2-1-g515: This is a concessive clause: хоть ... и ... says “although/even though.” Я is the subject; никогда не литературствовал is a past-tense, imperfective phrase meaning he had never dabbled in literature. The particle и after я is an idiomatic intensifier in this construction.
