segments.p2-1-g516: Мне (dative) + пришла мысль is an idiom meaning “a thought came to me.” Пришла agrees in gender/number with мысль (feminine). Вдруг modifies пришла (“suddenly”). Описать is an infinitive expressing the content/purpose of the thought; этого офицера is the accusative direct object (этого = “that/this”); в абличительном виде is a prepositional phrase (в + вид) meaning “in an accusatory form,” where абличительном is the descriptive adjective in the required case.
segments.p2-1-g517: В карикатуре is another prepositional phrase repeating the idea “in a caricature.” The preposition в takes the prepositional (or set) form here; the phrase functions as a parallel specification of the manner/form of the description.
segments.p2-1-g518: В виде повести is the idiom “in the form/kind of a story.” В виде uses вид in the appropriate case to mean “in the form/kind of,” and повести here denotes the type (a повесть — a novella/tale).
segments.p2-1-g519: Я is the subject; с наслаждением is an instrumental adverbial phrase meaning “with pleasure/with enjoyment”; писал is past imperfective (“wrote/was writing”) indicating the action in the past; эту повесть is the accusative object (“this story/novella”).
segments.p2-1-g520: Я is the subject pronoun 'I'. Абличил is a past-tense, masculine singular verb (archaic/literary) meaning 'exposed' or 'denounced'; together they form a simple past clause 'I exposed (him).' The verb is perfective and complete.
segments.p2-1-g521: Даже is an adverbial intensifier 'even' modifying the following verb; поклеветал is past tense 'slandered' (a completed action). The semicolon links this admission as an extension of the previous clause.
segments.p2-1-g522: Фамилию is the accusative object 'the surname' (placed before the subject for emphasis). Я repeats the subject. Так modifies подделал, introducing a result clause (так ... что). Подделал is past tense 'forged/altered' (perfective). Сначала is the adverb 'at first' placing the action temporally.
segments.p2-1-g523: Что introduces the result clause 'so that...'. Можно было is an impersonal past-possibility construction ('it was possible/could') with the infinitive узнать 'to recognize'; тотчас 'immediately' modifies the verb. Literally: 'so that it was possible to recognize [it] immediately.'
segments.p2-1-g524: Но is the adversative conjunction 'but'; потом is the adverb 'later/afterwards' signaling a change of plan or timing.
segments.p2-1-g525: По + prepositional (по зрелом рассуждении) is an idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning 'after mature reflection' or 'upon sober consideration'; зрелом is the adjective in the prepositional modifying рассуждении (reasoning/reflection).
segments.p2-1-g526: Изменил is past tense 'changed/altered' (the earlier accusative фамилию is the implicit object carried over), и links the next action, and отослал is past 'sent (off/by post)'. В + title indicates destination ('to Otechestvennye zapiski', the journal's name).
segments.p2-1-g527: 'Но' is the conjunction 'but'. 'Тогда ещё' are temporal adverbs meaning 'then' / 'as yet' and set the time frame. The phrase 'не было абличений' is an impersonal existential construction: past neuter 'было' + genitive plural 'абличений' indicates the absence of denunciations (i.e. 'there were no denunciations').
segments.p2-1-g528: 'И' links this clause to the previous one. 'Мою повесть' is the direct object in accusative feminine singular ('my story'), and 'не напечатали' is a past plural verb with negation, an impersonal/indefinite past meaning 'they did not print/publish it' (the agent is unspecified).
segments.p2-1-g502: Но is the coordinating conjunction 'but' linking to the previous clause; однажды is an adverb meaning 'once/one day' marking time; кто + то forms the indefinite 'кто-то' ('someone') with то as the enclitic particle; окликнул is a past-tense, perfective, transitive verb ('called out to'); его is the accusative object 'him'; по + dative (по фамилии) is a prepositional expression meaning 'by/using his surname'; на улице is a locative phrase 'in/on the street'.
segments.p2-1-g503: Когда is a subordinate conjunction 'when'; я is the subject 'I'; издали here functions as an adverb 'from afar / from a distance' modifying the verb; шел is the imperfective past 'was walking/following'; за ним is a prepositional phrase with за + instrumental meaning 'behind him' / 'after him' = 'following him'.
segments.p2-1-g504: Точно here means 'as if' / 'exactly as' introducing a simile; привязанный is a past passive participle (adjectival form) 'tied' or 'fastened' describing the narrator; к нему is the complement with the preposition к + dative 'to him', so the participial phrase means 'as if tied to him'.
segments.p2-1-g505: И вот is a discourse connective ('and so' / 'and then') indicating result; я is the subject 'I'; фамилию (accusative) precedes the verb for emphasis; узнал is perfective past 'learned/found out' — together 'and so I learned his surname.'
segments.p2-1-g506: Другой раз is an adverbial phrase 'another time' (lit. 'another occasion'); я — subject 'I'; проследил is perfective past 'followed' and takes его (accusative) as its direct object 'him'; до самой его квартиры is a prepositional phrase meaning 'as far as/up to his very apartment' with самой emphasizing 'very'; и links to the next action; за + noun (за гривенник) here indicates 'in exchange for / for (the price of)' — i.e. the narrator paid a гривенник (a ten-kopeck coin); узнал is perfective 'found out'; у + genitive (у дворника) marks the source/agent 'from the doorman.'
segments.p2-1-g507: Где introduces a subordinate interrogative/relative clause 'where'; он is the subject 'he'; живет is present-tense indicative 'lives' or 'resides' describing his habitual place of residence.
segments.p2-1-g508: В каком этаже is an interrogative prepositional phrase 'on which floor' (в + locative/prepositional case), with каком agreeing in case and number with этаже.
segments.p2-1-g509: Односложная enumeration: один (implied earlier) 'alone' contrasted by или с кем-нибудь 'or with somebody' — с + instrumental кем-нибудь is the indefinite instrumental 'with anyone/someone'; и т. is the start of the abbreviation 'и т. д.' meaning 'etc.'
segments.p2-1-g510: This single-letter segment 'д.' is the final piece of the common abbreviation 'и т. д.' (и так далее), meaning 'and so on' / 'etc.' — it completes the abbreviation begun in the previous segment.
segments.p2-1-g511: Одним словом is an idiomatic instrumental phrase meaning 'in a word' or 'in short' (одним + словом, instrumental case), used to introduce a brief summary.
segments.p2-1-g512: Все is an indefinite pronoun 'everything' here, standing as the topic of the summary.
segments.p2-1-g513: Что is a relative pronoun 'that/what' introducing the clause; можно + infinitive (можно узнать) is an impersonal modal construction meaning 'can be learned' / 'it is possible to find out'; от + genitive (от дворника) indicates the source 'from the doorman.'
segments.p2-1-g529: "Мне" is a dative experiencer ('to me'), used here instead of a nominative subject — Russian often marks the person affected in the dative in impersonal constructions. "это" is the demonstrative pronoun acting as the grammatical subject ('this/that/it'). "было" is the past tense linking/auxiliary verb (neuter past form used with a predicate adjective/adverb), and "очень досадно" is a predicative short adjective/adverb phrase meaning 'very annoying' — together the construction reads literally 'To me this was very annoying.'
segments.p2-1-g530: "Иногда" is an adverb ('sometimes') setting frequency. "злоба" is the nominative subject ('spite/anger'); Russian uses it as an animate cause of the action. "меня" is the accusative object ('me') receiving the action. "просто" is an adverb modifying the verb ('simply/merely'), and "душила" is the past imperfective verb (feminine form agreeing with the subject 'злоба') meaning 'was choking/stifling' — indicating a repeated or ongoing past feeling.
segments.p2-1-g531: "Наконец" is an adverb ('finally'). "я" is the nominative subject. "решился" is the past reflexive form of решиться (here meaning 'resolved/decided (to)'); with an infinitive it expresses deciding to undertake an action. "вызвать" is the perfective infinitive 'to call/summon/challenge' and complements "решился." "противника моего" is the direct object in accusative plus the possessive modifier after it ('my opponent' — noun + possessive adjective), and "на дуэль" is a prepositional phrase with "на" indicating purpose ('to/for a duel').
segments.p2-1-g532: "Я" is the subject 'I.' "сочинил" is the past perfective verb 'composed/wrote' indicating a completed action. "к нему" ('к' + dative 'нему') means 'to him' — the recipient. "прекрасное" is a neuter adjective standing before the noun (the noun follows in the next segment); it agrees with the (omitted here) noun in gender/number/case and functions as part of the direct object phrase ('a splendid/fine ...').
segments.p2-1-g533: "привлекательное" is an adjective modifying the noun that follows ('attractive/tempting'), forming together with "прекрасное" the full adjectival description. "письмо" is the accusative neuter noun 'letter' serving as the direct object of "сочинил." The two adjectives + noun form a single object phrase: 'a fine, attractive letter.'
segments.p2-1-g534: "умоляя" is a verbal adverb (деепричастие) from the imperfective verb 'умолять' and expresses a concurrent circumstance ('imploring/begging'), linking the manner of composing the letter to the main clause. Grammatically it governs an accusative object plus an infinitive: "его" is the accusative 'him' (object of the pleading), "передо мной" ('перед' + instrumental "мной" with the archaic/euphonic form 'передо') means 'in my presence/to my face,' and "извиниться" is the perfective infinitive 'to apologize' — together: 'imploring him to apologize to me.'
segments.p2-1-g535: "в случае отказа" is a conditional noun phrase ('in the case of refusal'), where "отказа" is genitive governed by "в случае." The particle "же" adds emphasis/contrast ('then/indeed'); "довольно" is an adverb 'quite/rather' modifying "твердо" ('firmly'), and "намекал" is the past imperfective verb 'hinted/insinuated' (agreeing implicitly with the subject 'I'). "на дуэль" ("на" + accusative) indicates the object/target of the hint ('at/about a duel'). The whole phrase says he would, in case of refusal, hint fairly firmly at a duel.
segments.p2-1-g606: Он (he) is the subject. тоже (also/too) adds that he shares the same behavior. ходил is the past imperfective of ходить used here to indicate habitual or repeated action ('used to go' / 'went regularly'). туда is a directional/time adverb 'there'. более here modifies frequency/degree ('more' / 'rather'), and в праздники is a temporal phrase ('on holidays'); together более в праздники = 'mostly on holidays' or 'more often at holidays'. Word order places the frequency modifier before the time phrase for emphasis.
segments.p2-1-g607: Он (he) is the subject; хоть here means 'though/even if' introducing a concessive clause. тоже repeats 'also'. сворачивал is the imperfective past of сворачивать meaning 'turned aside' or 'would turn off' (habitual/repeated past action). с дороги literally 'from the road' (с + noun). перед генералами / перед особами сановитыми uses перед + instrumental to mean 'in front of / in the presence of generals and of stately personages'; сановитыми is an adjective describing особами.
segments.p2-1-g608: как introduces a simile 'like/as'. вьюн here is a small wriggling fish (weatherfish); the comparison likens his movement to the fish's wriggle.
segments.p2-1-g609: между ними is a prepositional phrase 'between them'; между governs the pronoun ними (instrumental/oblique form after the preposition).
segments.p2-1-g610: но is a simple adversative 'but/however'. таких is a demonstrative plural 'such (ones)' referring forward to a class of people.
segments.p2-1-g611: как наш брат literally 'like our brother' — как introduces the comparison, наш marks possession, and брат here means 'fellow'/'our kind of man' (not necessarily literal sibling).
segments.p2-1-g612: или даже 'or even' adds emphasis; почище is a colloquial comparative of 'пoчистый' meaning 'neater/cleaner' or 'more respectable' (used comparatively). нашего брата repeats the reference to 'our fellow'.
segments.p2-1-g613: он (he) is resumed; просто here means 'simply/just' (intensifier). давил is the past imperfective of давить meaning 'pressed/crushed/oppressed' — used here as a habitual past action meaning he overwhelmingly pushed them aside.
segments.p2-1-g614: шел (he went/walked) is past imperfective of идти indicating movement; прямо 'straight/directly' modifies the verb; на них (на + them) = 'toward them/at them' — here implying he walked directly at those people.
segments.p2-1-g615: как будто ('as if') introduces a comparison/hypothesis. перед ним было пустое пространство: перед + ним = 'before/in front of him'; было is past neuter of быть used impersonally 'there was'; пустое пространство = 'empty space' (пустое agrees with пространство).
segments.p2-1-g616: и 'and' connects clauses. ни в каком случае is an idiomatic negation 'in no case / under no circumstances' (ни intensifies the negation). дороги here stands for 'on the road' (accusative/nominal usage), не уступал is the past imperfective negative 'did not yield' or 'would not give way'.
segments.p2-1-g617: Я = I. упивался is a reflexive imperfective past verb meaning 'got drunk on' or 'revelled in' (figurative: to take intoxicating pleasure in something). моей злобой is an instrumental phrase 'in my malice' that indicates the state/content in which the subject is indulging.
segments.p2-1-g618: на него глядя is a gerund clause (деепричастие) meaning 'looking at him' — на + него is the prepositional phrase 'at him' (на here = toward/at), and глядя is the present gerund of глядеть indicating simultaneous action.
segments.p2-1-g619: и is the coordinating conjunction 'and' that here ends the sentence abruptly, signaling an unfinished continuation or a pause in the narrator's thought.
segments.p2-1-g622: озлобленно is an adverb 'resentfully' describing the manner. перед ним ('in front of/before him') uses перед + pronoun in the oblique form to indicate location/position relative to 'him'. каждый раз = 'every time' and svорачивать in the past imperfective сворачивал indicates a repeated/past habitual action 'would turn aside' or 'turned aside each time.' The clause overall describes a habitual, resentful action performed whenever in his presence.
segments.p2-1-g598: Для чего is a fixed question phrase meaning “for what reason/why.” набирал is imperfective past, here in the sense “took on/collected” (набирать на себя — to gather/raise onto oneself, i.e. to inflict or bring upon oneself). на себя marks the action as directed onto the speaker; эту муку is accusative ‘this torment’ (мука in the abstract sense of suffering).
segments.p2-1-g599: The repeated Для чего introduces a second rhetorical ‘why.’ ходил is the imperfective past with habitual/iterative nuance (“I used to go / I went”), на + accusative Невский indicates motion toward Nevsky (the street/avenue). The dash before не знаю makes the final clause an abrupt, rhetorical admission: “I don’t know.”
segments.p2-1-g600: но is a contrastive ‘but.’ Меня тянуло is an impersonal past construction where the experiencer is in the accusative (меня) and тянуло (imperfective past) means ‘I felt drawn / it was pulling me’; просто weakens/emphasizes (‘simply/just’). при + prepositional каждой возможности = ‘at every opportunity’ (при here = on the occasion of).
segments.p2-1-g601: Тогда уже (by that time, already) gives temporal emphasis. начинал испытывать is a past progressive/incipient construction (“began to experience”): начинал + infinitive indicates the start of a repeated or developing action. приливы is plural ‘surges/rushes’ and тех наслаждений is genitive plural (‘of those pleasures’), showing the surges belong to those previously mentioned pleasures.
segments.p2-1-g602: о которых is the relative pronoun ‘about which/about whom’ referring back to тех наслаждений; я уже говорил is past simple (‘I already spoke/said’) and в первой главе is a locative temporal/place phrase (‘in the first chapter’). The clause is a relative clause modifying наслаждений.
segments.p2-1-g603: После истории (после + genitive) = ‘after the incident/affair.’ же is an emphatic particle adding contrast or insistence. история с офицером uses с + instrumental to mean ‘the affair involving the officer.’ The impersonal phrase меня ... стало тянуть uses стало (it began) + infinitive тянуть to mean ‘I began to be drawn’ with еще сильнее indicating an increase in intensity.
segments.p2-1-g604: на Невском is a locative phrase (‘on Nevsky [Prospekt]’); the clitic particle -то after Невском adds emphasis/contrast (‘on Nevsky, in particular’). я его встречал наиболее uses the imperfective встречал to indicate habitual or repeated encounters (‘I met/ran into him most often’), and наиболее = ‘most/especially.’ The fronted location emphasizes where the meetings occurred.
segments.p2-1-g605: там-то (‘there’ with -то for emphasis) sets the place again. любовался is the imperfective past of любоваться, which takes the instrumental (им) and means ‘to gaze at/admire’; the particle и adds emphatic ‘even/indeed’ before the verb. Note that любоваться governs the instrumental case (им).
segments.p2-1-g543: This is a hypothetical clause formed with the particle 'бы' plus the past-tense verb 'защищал', giving a conditional meaning 'would defend'. 'Своей сановитостью' is in the instrumental case and names the means ('by/with his sanovity'); 'своей' agrees with the subject and indicates possession. The clause ends with a semicolon linking it to the next contrasting hypothetical.
segments.p2-1-g544: 'Я бы облагораживал' is another conditional (бы + past imperfective) expressing a repeated or potential action 'I would ennoble'. 'Его' here is the direct object ('him'), and 'своей развитостью' is instrumental ('with my refinement/development'), where 'своей' refers back to the subject 'я'.
segments.p2-1-g545: 'Ну и' is a colloquial, elliptical filler: 'well, and...' or 'and so on' — here it trails off and signals the speaker's casual dismissal or that the list continues implicitly. There is no full clause following it.
segments.p2-1-g548: 'Идеями' is the instrumental plural of 'идея' and continues the list of means introduced earlier (e.g. 'with ideas'). As an instrumental it expresses manner or means.
segments.p2-1-g549: The phrase 'и много кой-чего' is a colloquial indefinite meaning 'many sorts of things' or 'a great deal of other things'. The construction 'много ... бы могло быть' uses the impersonal conditional 'бы могло быть' (neuter singular) to mean 'could/might have been'.
segments.p2-1-g550: 'Вообразите' is an imperative (second person plural or polite singular) meaning 'Imagine' or 'Picture'. It introduces the listener into a hypothetical situation.
segments.p2-1-g551: 'Что тогда прошло уже два года' is a subordinate clause introduced by 'что' = 'that'. 'Прошло' is an impersonal past neuter verb used with a time expression ('два года') to mean 'two years had passed' (elapsed time); 'уже' = 'already' and 'тогда' = 'by that time/then'.
segments.p2-1-g552: Here 'как' introduces a temporal clause meaning 'since/when' (i.e. 'two years had passed since he offended me'). 'Обидел' is past tense, perfective, a punctual action 'offended/wronged'.
segments.p2-1-g553: This clause states the result: 'и вызов мой был безобразнейшим анахронизмом.' 'Вызов мой' (my challenge) is the subject, 'был' is the past-tense copula 'was', and the predicate is an instrumental noun phrase 'безобразнейшим анахронизмом' (superlative adjective + noun in instrumental), a common Russian way to form 'was an X'.
segments.p2-1-g554: 'Несмотря на' is a compound preposition meaning 'despite'. It takes the accusative: 'всю ловкость' (all the dexterity/cleverness) with 'письма моего' in genitive ('of my letter') forming 'despite all the cleverness of my letter.'
segments.p2-1-g555: These are past active participles ('объяснявшего' and 'прикрывавшего') modifying 'письма моего' and agreeing with it in case (genitive). They describe what the letter did: 'which explained and which covered/concealed the anachronism.'
segments.p2-1-g556: 'Но' is the coordinating conjunction 'but' introducing contrast with the previous thought.
segments.p2-1-g557: 'Слава богу' is an idiomatic exclamation meaning 'thank God' or 'glory to God.' The parenthetical 'до сих пор благодарю всевышнего со слезами' is a present-tense first-person remark 'I still thank the Almighty with tears' (до сих пор = 'up to now/still'), added as an emotional aside.
segments.p2-1-g558: A straightforward negated past-tense clause: 'я ... не послал' = 'I did not send.' The direct object 'письма моего' appears in the genitive because Russian often uses the genitive object after negation (partitive/negation genitive).
segments.p2-1-g623: 'Меня мучило' is an impersonal past construction: 'мучило' is the past tense, neuter form of мучить used impersonally, with the experiencer in the accusative 'меня' placed before the verb for emphasis. Literal structure: 'It tormented me' (or 'I was tormented').
segments.p2-1-g624: The clause is introduced by the conjunction 'что' ('that'). 'Я' is the subject and 'могу быть' is the modal + infinitive ('can be'); 'никак не могу' is a fixed negative intensifier meaning 'simply cannot / in no way can'. The cluster 'даже и' is emphatic ('even'), and 'на улице' is a locative prepositional phrase ('out on the street'). The idiom 'быть с ним на равной ноге' means 'to be on equal footing / on equal terms with him.'
segments.p2-1-g625: 'Отчего' is a question word 'why / for what reason'. 'Ты' is the 2nd-person subject. 'Непременно' is an adverb meaning 'necessarily / always / without fail' and modifies the verb. 'Первый' here is used as a predicative/attribute meaning 'the first (one to ...)', and 'сворачиваешь' is present-tense, 2nd-person singular of the imperfective 'сворачивать' meaning 'to turn aside / turn off' (habitual or repeated action). The sentence asks why the addressee habitually is the first to turn away/leave.
segments.p2-1-g626: The verb 'приставал' is past-imperfective and here implies repeated action ('I would go on pestering / accosting'). The subject 'я' is emphasized by 'сам' ('I myself'). 'К себе' ('to myself') shows the action is directed inward—he is confronting or tormenting himself. The dash marks reported or quoted inner speech.
segments.p2-1-g627: 'В бешеной истерике' is an adverbial phrase of manner: 'в' + prepositional 'истерике' with the agreeing adjective 'бешеной' (feminine prepositional). It describes the mental state in which the action occurred: 'in a furious / frantic hysteria.'
segments.p2-1-g628: 'Проснувшись' is a verbal adverb (деепричастие) from perfective 'проснуться' meaning 'having woken up' and links temporally to the main clause ('I would pester myself, having woken up...'). 'Иногда' ('sometimes') modifies frequency. The time expression 'часу в третьем ночи' is idiomatic and roughly means 'at about the third hour of the night' — i.e. 'around three o'clock in the morning'; 'часу' here marks approximation, 'в третьем' agrees with 'ночи'.
segments.p2-1-g629: 'Отчего' again is 'why / for what reason'. 'Именно' is an emphatic particle 'exactly / precisely', narrowing the question. 'Ты' is the emphatic subject 'you' — the speaker stresses the contrastive choice of 'you.'
segments.p2-1-g630: 'А не он?' is a short contrasting tag: 'а' introduces the contrast ('and / but'), 'не' negates, and 'он' is the nominative 'he' — together forming the brief question 'and not him?' contrasting the earlier 'you'.
segments.p2-1-g631: Ведь is a discourse particle (rhetorical 'after all/you know') introducing a reminder; для governs the following noun phrase in the genitive (этого закона). Закон appears in genitive because нет requires a genitive object; нет here is the impersonal predicate 'there is no'. The whole clause literally means 'For this there is no law' = 'There is no law for this.'
segments.p2-1-g632: Ведь repeats the reminding particle; это is the demonstrative 'this/it'. Нигде (nowhere) combines with the negator не to form 'nowhere is it written'. Написано is the short neuter past participle used as an impersonal predicate meaning 'is written' (passive), so нигде не написано = 'it is not written anywhere.'
segments.p2-1-g633: Ну is a pragmatic particle ('well'); пусть here is the jussive particle forming a concessive/let-it-be clause ('let it be'); будет is third person singular of быть (here as part of the jussive construction), and поровну is an adverb meaning 'equally/half-and-half'. Together: 'Well, let it be equal.'
segments.p2-1-g634: как introduces a comparative clause 'as'; обыкновенно is an adverb 'usually'; бывает is present tense used idiomatically to mean 'happens' or 'is the case' (habitual occurrence): 'as usually happens.'
segments.p2-1-g635: когда introduces the temporal clause 'when'; деликатные is an adjective 'tactful/delicate' modifying люди ('people'); встречаются is the reflexive present plural meaning 'meet (each other)' — reflexive voice indicates reciprocal action here.
segments.p2-1-g636: он is the third-person subject 'he'; уступит is the perfective future (third singular) of уступить meaning 'will yield/give way'; половину is the direct object in the accusative meaning 'half' (i.e. 'half of the way').
segments.p2-1-g637: и simply coordinates with the previous clause ('and'); ты is the addressed 'you' (singular/informal); половину repeats the noun 'half' — the verb уступишь is omitted by ellipsis but implied from the previous clause ('and you [will yield] half').
segments.p2-1-g638: вы here addresses the two people together ('you' plural/formal) as the subject of the next verb; и is a connective/emphatic 'and'; пройдете is future tense second person plural of пройти 'to pass by / walk past' — 'and you'll pass by.'
segments.p2-1-g639: взаимно is an adverb 'mutually'; уважая is a present active adverbial participle (деепричастие) from уважать, here meaning 'respecting' and describing how you will pass; друг друга is the reciprocal expression 'one another' (друг in the nominative + друга in the genitive used idiomatically to mean 'each other').
segments.p2-1-g640: "Но" is the coordinating conjunction 'but' introducing contrast; "так" here means 'so/thus'; together with the past tense verb "было" (from быть) the phrase "так не было" is an impersonal past statement 'that was not so' (negation with не before the verb).
segments.p2-1-g641: The particles "и все-таки" form the adverbial phrase 'and yet/after all' ("все" + "таки"); "сворачивал" is imperfective past tense of сворачивать meaning 'to turn aside/step aside' and here expresses the speaker's repeated or continuous bodily action; the subject pronoun "я" follows the verb for emphasis (verb-subject word order is normal in narrative).
segments.p2-1-g642: "А" contrasts with the previous clause ('while/and'); "он" is the subject 'he'; the doubled particles "даже и" add emphasis ('not even'); "не" negates the verb; "замечал" is imperfective past 'noticed' and implies he did not notice repeatedly or at any moment — a background/state description.
segments.p2-1-g643: "Что" introduces the content clause 'that ...'; "я" is the clause subject; "ему" is the dative pronoun 'to him' indicating the person receiving the gesture; "уступаю" is present imperfective 'I yield/give way' used to describe the action as ongoing or habitual at that time (Russian often uses present in subordinate clauses to convey a contemporaneous or habitual action even when the main clause is past).
segments.p2-1-g644: "И вот" are discourse particles ('and then' / 'and so'); "удивительнейшая" is a superlative form (very emphatic) of 'удивительный' meaning 'most astonishing' or 'the most amazing'; "мысль" is the noun 'thought'; "вдруг осенила меня" is an idiomatic perfective past — "вдруг" 'suddenly' + "осенила" (осенить) 'struck/seized (with an idea)' with the object in accusative and the experiencer in accusative/oblique form "меня' — together: 'a thought suddenly struck me.'
segments.p2-1-g569: This whole clause is a simple past-habitual statement. 'Иногда по праздникам' is a temporal adverbial ('sometimes, on holidays'); 'я хаживал' is the imperfective past tense indicating habitual or repeated action ('I used to go'); 'в четвертом часу' is a time expression meaning 'at the fourth hour' (i.e. about four o'clock); 'на Невский' uses the preposition + proper name to indicate destination ('to Nevsky [Prospect]'); 'и гулял по солнечной стороне' adds a parallel habitual action ('and walked along the sunny side').
segments.p2-1-g570: 'То есть' is a fixed phrase meaning 'that is (to say)'; grammatically it introduces a clarification. 'я там вовсе не гулял' is a negated past imperfective clause: 'I did not stroll there at all' — 'вовсе' strengthens the negation.
segments.p2-1-g571: The conjunction 'а' contrasts the previous clause. 'испытывал' is past imperfective meaning 'I experienced' (habitual/ongoing in past); 'бесчисленные мучения' is the direct object in the accusative: 'countless torments'.
segments.p2-1-g572: 'унижения и разлития желчи' lists further direct objects. 'разлития' is a noun in genitive/accusative construction meaning 'the outpourings/spillings'; 'желчи' (genitive) literally 'of bile' and here carries a figurative meaning.
segments.p2-1-g573: 'но' introduces a contrastive clause. 'того-то мне' uses the demonstrative/genitive 'того' with the emphatic particle 'то' and the dative 'мне' — a colloquial way to mean 'that (very thing) to me' or 'that, for me...'.
segments.p2-1-g574: A single-word parenthetical 'верно' functions adverbially: here 'surely' or 'I suppose', adding the narrator's confirmation or uncertainty.
segments.p2-1-g575: 'и надобно было' is a short past impersonal construction: 'надобно было' = 'it was necessary' (here: 'I needed it' in sense 'it was necessary for me'); the conjunction 'и' links this conclusion to the previous comment.
segments.p2-1-g576: 'Я шмыгал' is a past imperfective clause meaning 'I darted/snapped about' — 'шмыгать' is colloquial and describes quick, darting movements; the verb is in the masculine singular past form.
segments.p2-1-g577: 'как вьюн' is a simile introduced by 'как' ('like/as'); 'вьюн' is a small eel-like fish (weatherfish), so the narrator compares his motion to the quick writhing of that fish.
segments.p2-1-g578: 'самым некрасивым образом' is an adverbial phrase: 'самым' is the superlative/emphatic form ('the very/most'), 'некрасивым образом' literally 'in an un-beautiful manner' = 'in the ugliest way'; 'образом' here is instrumental noun meaning 'manner/form'.
segments.p2-1-g579: 'между прохожими' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'among passersby'; 'между' governs the instrumental plural 'прохожими' to indicate being amid them.
segments.p2-1-g580: 'уступая беспрерывно дорогу' is a participial phrase (present active participle 'уступая' = 'yielding/giving way') describing continuous action; 'дорогу' is the direct object in accusative in the idiom 'уступать дорогу' ('to give way'), and 'беспрерывно' modifies the participle.
segments.p2-1-g581: The repeated particle 'то ... то ...' sets up alternation ('now ... now ...'); here it coordinates different groups to whom he yields: 'кавалергардским и гусарским офицерам' (adjectives in dative plural modifying 'офицерам').
segments.p2-1-g582: Another alternate item with 'то' followed by 'барыням' (dative plural of 'барыня' = 'ladies'), completing the list of people he yields to.
segments.p2-1-g583: Main clause: 'я чувствовал в эти минуты' ('I felt in those moments') — 'чувствовал' is past imperfective; 'конвульсивные боли в сердце и жар в спине' are the felt symptoms (accusatives); 'при одном представлении о мизере моего костюма' is a prepositional phrase with 'при' meaning 'at/with the mere' and 'представлении' (noun of perception) governing 'о' + 'мизере' (about the meagerness) as the cause of the sensation.
segments.p2-1-g584: 'о мизере и пошлости моей шмыгающей фигурки' continues the object of 'представлении': 'о' + nouns in prepositional case describe what the imagination presents; 'шмыгающей' is a present participle modifying 'фигурки' ('my darting little figure').
segments.p2-1-g585: 'Это была мука-мученская' is a nominal predication in the past: 'It was a martyr's torment.' The compound noun 'мука-мученская' is emphatic/poetic (repetition/derivation) and 'была' is the past copula.
segments.p2-1-g586: 'беспрерывное невыносимое унижение от мысли' is a noun phrase in nominative describing the nature of the torment: two adjectives 'беспрерывное невыносимое' modify 'унижение' (humiliation), and 'от мысли' ('from the thought') expresses the source ('caused by the thought').
segments.p2-1-g587: A participial (relative) phrase 'переходившей в ... ощущение того' modifies 'мысли': 'переходившей' is the feminine genitive singular past participle agreeing with 'мысли' and means 'which passed/turned into'; 'в' introduces the resulting state 'в беспрерывное и непосредственное ощущение того' = 'into an uninterrupted and immediate sensation of that'.
segments.p2-1-g588: 'что я муха' is a content clause introduced by 'что' (that): direct reported content 'that I was a fly' — simple subject-predicate with 'я' and nominal predicate 'муха'.
segments.p2-1-g589: 'перед всем этим светом' is a prepositional phrase: 'перед' + instrumental expresses 'in the presence of/compared with all this world/society'; 'светом' here is used figuratively for 'high society' or 'the world'.
segments.p2-1-g590: Single adjective 'гадкая' modifies the omitted noun (муха) as an emphatic adjective inserted into the sentence: 'nasty' or 'foul'.
segments.p2-1-g591: 'непотребная муха' is a noun phrase: 'непотребная' (indecent/unseemly/worthless) modifies 'муха' (fly); the phrase underscores worthlessness.
segments.p2-1-g592: Comparative clause fragments separated by commas: 'всех умнее' uses genitive 'всех' with comparative adjective 'умнее' to mean 'smarter than everyone.'
segments.p2-1-g593: Same pattern: 'всех развитее' is a comparative 'more developed than all' — 'всех' again marks the standard of comparison.
segments.p2-1-g594: Again comparative: 'всех благороднее' = 'nobler than everyone'; these clauses express the narrator's ironic self-assessment before the contradicted reality that follows.
segments.p2-1-g595: '- это уж само собою' is a parenthetical: 'это' = 'this', 'уж' adds emphasis ('indeed'), and 'само собою' = 'by itself' or 'goes without saying' — a dismissive formula that the superiority is obvious (ironic here).
segments.p2-1-g596: '- но беспрерывно всем уступающая муха' returns to the main ironic point: 'но' (but), 'беспрерывно' (continuously), and the present participle 'уступающая' (yielding) modifies 'муха' — 'a fly that continuously yields to everyone.'
segments.p2-1-g597: 'всеми униженная и всеми оскорбленная' is a passive-described result: two short passive past participles in instrumental/agentive sense ('униженная' and 'оскорбленная') accompanied by 'всеми' in instrumental meaning 'by all' — 'humiliated by everyone and insulted by everyone.'
segments.p2-1-g645: 'А' here is an interjection meaning 'and'/'well' used to introduce a speculative remark; 'что' is the interrogative 'what' (part of the formula 'А что...') initiating the thought. Together they function as an introductory 'Well, what...' that leads into the inner question.
segments.p2-1-g646: 'вздумал' is the past tense, perfective form meaning 'it occurred to (me)' or 'I thought of'; placed before the pronoun 'я' for emphasis inside an inserted authorial comment (the dashes mark reported thought). This is a simple past narrative comment: 'I thought'.
segments.p2-1-g647: This repeat 'что' resumes the interrogative content ('what...') and heads the subordinate hypothetical clause that follows; here it functions as 'what' rather than the conjunction 'that'.
segments.p2-1-g648: 'если' is the conditional 'if'; 'встретиться' is the perfective infinitive 'to meet' used elliptically to form a hypothetical (the clause is intentionally incomplete); 'с ним' = 'with him' (preposition + pronoun in the instrumental); the final 'и' is the coordinating conjunction 'and' trailing off (ellipsis) to indicate the thought continues. The whole segment gives the beginning of a conditional 'if (I) were to meet him and...'.
segments.p2-1-g651: This is a short elliptical rhetorical question: the negative particle 'не' plus the infinitive 'посторониться' ('to step aside') expresses 'would (I) not step aside?' or 'not step aside?' The infinitive stands in for a full clause in colloquial/hypothetical speech.
segments.p2-1-g652: 'Нарочно' is an adverb meaning 'deliberately/on purpose' modifying the negated infinitive phrase 'не посторониться' ('not to step aside'); together this expresses the idea 'to deliberately refuse to step aside.'
segments.p2-1-g653: 'хоть бы даже' is a concessive phrase meaning 'even if' or 'even should it be the case that'; 'бы' is the conditional/modal particle; 'пришлось' is the past-tense impersonal form of 'прийтись' meaning 'it would be necessary/I would have to'; 'толкнуть' is the perfective infinitive 'to push'; 'его' is the accusative pronoun 'him.' The clause expresses a concession: 'even if I had to push him.'
segments.p2-1-g654: The particle 'а' here is a connective/contrastive 'and' (or 'but/and so') that introduces the final interrogative follow-up to the hypothetical action.
segments.p2-1-g655: 'каково' is the interrogative adjective 'what/how (is it) like'; 'это' = 'this/it'; 'будет' is the future tense of 'быть' ('will be'). Together they form 'what will that be like?' (literally 'how will this be?').
segments.p2-1-g656: The noun phrase 'Дерзкая мысль эта' ('this bold thought') places the demonstrative after the noun for emphasis. 'Мало-помалу' is an adverb meaning 'little by little/gradually.' The phrase 'до того' is an intensifier meaning 'to such an extent.' 'Овладела' is the past feminine singular of 'овладеть' ('to seize, to take possession of') and governs the instrumental 'мною' ('by me'), so the whole means 'this bold thought gradually took such hold of me.'
segments.p2-1-g657: 'что' here introduces the result clause ('that'); 'не' negates the imperfective past 'давала' ('gave/allowed'); 'мне' is the dative 'to me'; 'покоя' is the genitive of 'покой' ('peace/rest'). The idiom 'не давала мне покоя' means 'would not let me rest' or 'gave me no peace.'
segments.p2-1-g680: This is a simple past-tense statement: решился is the past of the perfective reflexive verb решиться, meaning “made up (my) mind / decided (to do something).” Наконец is an adverb meaning “finally,” and совершенно is an adverbial intensifier “completely/absolutely.” Word order (subject Я + verb + adverb(s)) is normal for Russian emphasis.
segments.p2-1-g681: Взяли is the past plural of взять and here means “took” (i.e., consumed). Приготовления is the plural noun “preparations” (subject), and времени is in the genitive singular after много to express quantity (очень много времени = “very much time”). Но at the start signals contrast: “but/however.”
segments.p2-1-g682: Первое is the nominalized ordinal “the first (thing).” То is a demonstrative pronoun used here to introduce the explanatory clause that follows (roughly: “the first was that …”). The comma marks the pause before the explanatory что-clause.
segments.p2-1-g683: Что begins a content clause (“that …”). Во время is the fixed prepositional phrase “during,” followed by исполнения in the genitive (“during the execution/performing” of the act). Нужно было быть is an impersonal past construction meaning “it was necessary to be” (нужно + past было + infinitive быть). В более приличнейшем виде is a descriptive prepositional phrase “in a more respectable appearance” — приличнейшем is a colloquial/heightened comparative/superlative form and with более becomes an emphatic layered comparative. И позаботиться о костюме uses the perfective infinitive позаботиться “to take care of / attend to” with о + prepositional (костюме) “about the outfit/suit.”
segments.p2-1-g684: На всякий случай is a fixed idiom meaning “just in case / for any eventuality.” На here has the purposive sense (“for”), и всякий случай literally “any case/occasion.”
segments.p2-1-g685: Если is the conditional conjunction “if,” marking the subordinate conditional clause that follows.
segments.p2-1-g686: Например is a parenthetical adverb “for example / for instance,” used to introduce an illustrative possibility.
segments.p2-1-g687: Завяжется is a reflexive future (or imperfective colloquial) form meaning “will begin / should get tied up / should arise” — here “a public incident should begin/ensue.” Публичная история literally “public story/incident/affair.” А introduces a contrast and публика-то uses the particle -то for emphasis (“and as for the public…”). Тут means “here/at this place,” and суперфлю is a clipped colloquialism (from ‘superfluity’) meaning ostentatious surplus/extravagance about the public.
segments.p2-1-g688: Графиня is the nominative subject “the countess.” Ходит is an imperfective present used here to mean she habitually appears/attends (literally “walks/goes about”), implying she is part of that ostentatious public.
segments.p2-1-g689: Князь is the noun “prince” (a title). Д. is an abbreviated initial — here it stands for the noble’s name; it is an initial/abbreviation rather than a lexical word in the sense list (see note).
segments.p2-1-g730: The verb мучился is the past tense, masculine singular form of the reflexive verb мучиться ('to suffer/agonize'); the reflexive ending -ся emphasizes a state experienced by the subject. Word order places the verb before the pronoun (Мучился я) for stylistic/emphatic effect; я is the nominative subject. Ужасно is an adverb modifying мучился (‘terribly’). The sentence is a compact past-state statement emphasizing the speaker's distress.
segments.p2-1-g731: Попросить is a perfective infinitive ('to ask (for)') functioning as the grammatical subject/nominal clause of казалось (an impersonal construction). Денег is the genitive plural object of попросить ('money' — genitive used after the perfective verb). У + name (у Антона Антоныча) indicates 'from' someone (possession/source). Мне is the dative experiencer required by казалось; казалось is past tense neuter impersonal 'it seemed' and takes the infinitive-clause as the logical subject. Чудовищным и постыдным are predicative adjectives in the instrumental used after казалось to describe the perceived quality of the action ('seemed monstrous and shameful').
segments.p2-1-g732: Я is the nominative subject. Даже ('even') emphasizes the following quantity. Две-три ночи is a duration expression ('two or three nights') indicating how long the action extended. Не спал is the past-tense verb phrase with negation ('did not sleep'); спал is past masculine singular. The clause states a negated past-duration of sleep.
segments.p2-1-g733: Да и вообще is a colloquial linking phrase meaning 'and besides / in fact, generally'. Я тогда мало спал continues with the same subject and past tense verb: тогда ('then' — at that time), мало ('little'/'insufficient') modifies спал ('slept'). The clause generalizes the speaker's poor sleeping pattern.
segments.p2-1-g734: Был is the past-tense form of быть ('was'); в + prepositional лихорадке expresses state ('in a fever' / 'feverish'). This short clause states a medical/physical condition in the past.
segments.p2-1-g735: Сердце у меня is a possessive construction literally 'the heart at me' = 'my heart' (у + genitive shows possession). Как-то смутно is an adverbial phrase ('somehow vaguely') modifying the verb. Замирало is the imperfective past tense ('would stop / would fail'), describing recurring or habitual sensations. Или вдруг начинало прыгать switches to an alternative reaction: или ('or'), вдруг ('suddenly'), начинало (imperfective past 'would begin') + infinitive прыгать ('to jump') — together 'or would suddenly begin to jump'. The clause contrasts two kinds of sensations in the speaker's chest.
segments.p2-1-g736: A bare infinitive прыгать repeated here after a comma functions as an enumerative/emphatic continuation of the prior verb phrase — the repetition evokes the repeated, frantic action of the heart.
segments.p2-1-g737: The final повторение прыгать with an exclamation mark is a stronger emphatic repetition: repeating the infinitive and ending with an exclamation heightens the speaker's emotional intensity and mimics the racing, staccato quality described.
segments.p2-1-g740: Proper name plus patronymic in the nominative (subject). 'сначала' is an adverb meaning 'at first' that front-modifies the following verb. 'удивился' is the past tense, masculine singular, perfective form of 'удивиться' (reflexive), here meaning 'was surprised' and completes this small clause as the first item in a sequence of actions.
segments.p2-1-g741: 'потом' is an adverb 'then' that continues the temporal sequence. 'поморщился' is the past tense, masculine singular of the reflexive verb 'поморщиться' meaning literally 'wrinkled (his) face' or 'made a grimace/wince'; it stands as a second short clause parallel to the first.
segments.p2-1-g742: 'потом' again introduces the next action in the sequence. 'рассудил' is past tense 'decided' or 'judged' (perfective). The conjunction 'и' links to the following clause. 'все-таки' (here split into 'все' + 'таки') is an adverbial modifier meaning 'nevertheless' or 'after all' and qualifies 'дал'. 'дал' is past tense 'gave', and with the adverbial 'взаймы' (an adverbial phrase) it means 'lent (as a loan)'.
segments.p2-1-g743: 'взяв' is a деепричастие (verbal adverb / gerund) from 'взять' meaning 'having taken' and forms a circumstantial clause that modifies the main action ('дал'). 'с меня' uses preposition 'с' + genitive to mean 'from me'. 'расписку' is the accusative object ('an IOU / signed pledge'). 'на право' = 'for the right (to...)', where 'на' introduces the purpose or subject matter. 'получения' is a verbal noun (gerundial noun) in genitive 'of receiving/obtaining'; 'данных' is a genitive plural adjective/participle meaning 'the given/aforementioned' (i.e., the sum in question). 'заимообразно' is an adverbial/archaic formation meaning roughly 'in a loan-like/loan-related manner' and qualifies the relationship to 'денег' (genitive 'of money'). 'через две недели' is a time expression 'in two weeks' with 'через' indicating an interval, and 'из жалованья' specifies the source 'out of (one's) salary'. Altogether this participial clause states that he took an IOU from me giving him the right to receive the specified loan-money from my pay in two weeks.
segments.p2-1-g702: Хорошую рубашку is an accusative phrase (feminine singular) placed at the start of the clause for emphasis: хорошую (adjective) agrees with рубашку (noun) and functions as the direct object of приготовил later in the sentence.
segments.p2-1-g703: с белыми костяными запонками is an instrumental accompaniment phrase introduced by с 'with' (meaning possession/accompaniment). Белыми and костяными are instrumental plural adjectives modifying запонками; костяными here means 'made of bone' rather than just 'bone-colored'.
segments.p2-1-g704: я уж давно приготовил is a simple past (perfective) clause: я (subject), уж давно (adverbial phrase 'long ago' with уж as an intensifier), приготовил is past masculine singular perfective 'prepared / had prepared' indicating the action was completed before the moment of speaking.
segments.p2-1-g705: но задержала очень шинель is an inversion where the verb задержала (past feminine) precedes its feminine subject шинель; но 'but' contrasts the clause with the preceding one, очень intensifies the verb (i.e. 'delayed/held up very much'). The coat is the grammatical subject performing the action of delaying.
segments.p2-1-g706: Сама-то по себе (emphatic phrase) places stress on the coat itself: сама is a feminine emphatic pronoun 'itself/as for itself', -то is a contrastive particle, and по себе 'by itself' means 'taken alone'. Шинель моя is the noun plus possessive 'my'. Очень была недурна uses the short-form adjective недурна (past-linking construction) meaning 'was not bad/was pretty good' with очень as an intensifier.
segments.p2-1-g707: грела is past tense (feminine) of греть and here means 'kept (me) warm'—the object (me) is omitted but understood. It's an imperfective past describing the coat's warming function.
segments.p2-1-g708: но она была на вате is contrastive (но 'but'); она (it) + была (was) + на вате literally 'on wadding' — an idiomatic way to say the coat was padded/insulated with wadding (вата). Here на + prepositional indicates the material/lining.
segments.p2-1-g709: а воротник был енотовый uses а for contrast/shift ('and/but'); воротник (subject) был (was) + енотовый (adjective) meaning the collar was made of raccoon fur or raccoon-like — a stylistic/material description.
segments.p2-1-g710: что составляло уже верх лакейства is a relative clause introduced by что 'which'; составляло is past neuter of составлять 'amounted to/constituted'; уже 'already' emphasizes that this detail reached an extreme; верх лакейства is a nominal phrase 'the height/peak of servility' (лакейства in genitive). The verb is neuter/impersonal to agree with the abstract clause 'что'.
segments.p2-1-g711: Надо было is an impersonal modal construction 'it was necessary' (often rendered 'I had to' in context). Переменить is the perfective infinitive 'to change/replace' and воротник is its direct object. Во что бы ни стало is a fixed concessive idiom meaning 'at any cost / whatever happens' — grammatically it contains the particle бы + ни for a concessive sense and стенло (стало) of стать; и завести бобрик continues with и 'and' + завести (infinitive 'to acquire/get') + бобрик (diminutive used for a beaver pelt).
segments.p2-1-g712: вроде как у офицеров is a colloquial simile: вроде как (sort of / like) introduces the comparison, and у офицеров uses у + genitive to express possession ('as the officers have'). The whole phrase means 'like the officers' or 'as the officers do'.
segments.p2-1-g713: "Для этого" is 'for this/for that purpose' with для + genitive; "я стал ходить" uses "стал" + infinitive to mean 'I began to' and the imperfective "ходить" implies habitual or repeated going (frequenting); "по Гостиному двору" uses "по" with the dative to mean 'around/through the Gostiny Dvor' (a commercial arcade); "и после нескольких попыток" means 'and after several attempts' (после + genitive); "нацелился на" is perfective past 'took aim at / set my sights on' (нацелился) with preposition "на" + accusative to indicate the target; "один" here functions like 'a certain/one' (selecting a single item); "дешевый немецкий бобрик" is 'a cheap German bобрик' where "бобрик" is a diminutive referring to a beaver pelt/young beaver — contextually a beaver-skin garment or pelt being offered for sale.
segments.p2-1-g690: ходит is 3rd person singular present of ходить used here as a brief, parenthetical verb — part of a list of people who 'go' or 'attend' (an event). It carries the habitual/attending meaning ('goes/attends') rather than a one-time motion.
segments.p2-1-g691: вся литература is a noun phrase with вся (feminine form of весь) agreeing with литература; it means 'the whole literary world' or 'all literature.' The following ходит repeats the same 3rd-person habitual meaning as a continuation of the list.
segments.p2-1-g692: нужно is an impersonal modal predicate meaning 'it is necessary'; it takes the infinitive быть + adjective/participle одетым to express 'to be well dressed.' хорошо is an adverb modifying одетым ('well'/'neatly').
segments.p2-1-g693: This clause combines two coordinated predicates: внушает (3rd sing. present 'makes an impression/instills') and поставит (perfective future 'will put/place'). прямо ('directly/straightaway') modifies поставит. нас is the object 'us'; некоторым образом is an adverbial phrase meaning 'in some way' ('некоторым' in instrumental + образ(ом) 'form/manner'). на равную ногу is an idiomatic prepositional phrase 'on an equal footing' and в глазах высшего общества means 'in the eyes of high society' (глазах + genitive).
segments.p2-1-g694: С этою целью is a colloquial/old-fashioned instrumental phrase 'with this purpose' (этою is archaic instrumental of 'это'). я (I) is the subject; выпросил is past masculine of выпросить 'obtained by asking/begging' and takes вперед жалованья ('in advance of my pay' with жалованья in the genitive). и купил continues with a coordinated verb 'and bought'; черные перчатки and порядочную шляпу are accusative objects ('black gloves' and 'a respectable/ proper hat'). у Чуркина indicates source/place ('at Churkin’s' or 'from Churkin').
segments.p2-1-g695: Черные перчатки is the noun phrase 'black gloves' (plural). казались is past plural of казаться 'seemed/appeared'; because it is followed by мне (dative 'to me') it means 'seemed to me.' и солиднее uses и as a connective ('both/and') and солиднее is the comparative form 'more solid/respectable.'
segments.p2-1-g696: и continues the paired comparatives ('and also'); бонтоннее is a comparative formed from бонтонный meaning 'more fashionable/well-bred' (style/etiquette sense). The pair 'солиднее и бонтоннее' intensifies the judgment.
segments.p2-1-g697: чем introduces the standard of comparison 'than.' лимонные is an adjective used as a substantive plural (the lemon-colored ones) referring back to перчатки.
segments.p2-1-g698: на которые is a relative pronoun phrase ('which' with preposition на because посягать governs на + acc.). я is the subject; посягал (past imperfective) from посягать means 'reached for/tried to take' or 'made for' in this context; сначала 'at first' places the action earlier in the sequence.
segments.p2-1-g699: Цвет is the nominative subject 'the color'; слишком is an adverb 'too/excessively' modifying the adjective резкий, which here means 'sharp/striking' (i.e., gaudy or too bright).
segments.p2-1-g700: This clause contains a colloquial simile: слишком как будто (repeating 'too' then 'as if') + хочет (3rd sing. present 'wants') + выставиться (reflexive infinitive 'to display oneself/show off'). The word человек appears after the verb for stylistic inversion ('as if a person wants to show off'), giving a slightly brusque tone.
segments.p2-1-g701: и is a simple conjunction; я is the subject; не взял is the past negative (perfective взять) 'did not take' — a completed-action negation; лимонных is the plural adjective used substantively 'the lemon-colored ones' (i.e., the gloves).
segments.p2-1-g658: Мечтал is the past tense, masculine singular, imperfective of мечтать and here means 'I dreamed/was fantasizing'; the verb precedes the pronoun (Мечтал я) for emphasis. Об этом is a prepositional phrase ('about this') modifying the verb; беспрерывно is an adverb qualifying the manner ('incessantly').
segments.p2-1-g659: Ужасно and нарочно are adverbs stacked as intensifier + manner ('extremely' / 'deliberately'); чаще is a comparative adverb 'more often' modifying ходил. Ходил is the imperfective past habitual 'used to go' (habitual action). На + Невский indicates direction/place ('to Nevsky' — the street).
segments.p2-1-g660: Чтоб introduces a purpose clause ('in order that / so as to') and is followed by the infinitive представить. Ещё яснее is a comparative phrase ('even more clearly'); себе is the dative/reflexive 'to myself' that often accompanies verbs like представить (to picture to oneself). Представить is the perfective infinitive meaning 'to picture/represent' (viewed as a completed mental act).
segments.p2-1-g661: Как introduces an interrogative-style subordinate clause of manner ('how'). Сделаю is the perfective future (1st person singular) of сделать and expresses a completed-action view of 'do/make' — here: 'how I shall do it.' Это is the direct object placed between subject and verb for clarity.
segments.p2-1-g662: Когда begins a temporal clause ('when'). Буду делать is the periphrastic future using быть + imperfective infinitive, giving an ongoing/frequentative nuance 'will be doing' (i.e. 'when I am doing it' rather than 'after I have done it'). Делать is the imperfective infinitive indicating an ongoing action.
segments.p2-1-g663: Я был is simple past of быть ('I was'); в + восторге is a fixed prepositional phrase meaning 'in rapture' or 'ecstatic' (восторге is the prepositional case of восторг).
segments.p2-1-g664: Все более и более is a fixed comparative phrase meaning 'more and more' (всё functions as an intensifier). Мне казалось is an impersonal past construction with казаться ('it seemed to me'); казалось is 3rd singular past and takes the thing that seems (это намерение) as subject. Вероятным и возможным are predicate adjectives in the instrumental after казалось, meaning 'probable and possible.'
segments.p2-1-g665: Разумеется is a fixed adverb/parenthetical meaning 'of course' or 'naturally' and opens the remark as a qualification.
segments.p2-1-g666: Не совсем negates and softens the infinitive толкнуть ('to push/force'); толкнуть is perfective infinitive ('to push once/forcefully'). Together: 'not to push him entirely' (i.e. not to shove him harshly).
segments.p2-1-g667: Думал я is past tense 'I thought', with the pronoun after the verb for emphasis or narrative voice.
segments.p2-1-g668: Уже заранее combines 'already' and 'in advance' ('already beforehand'); добрея is an archaic/colloquial gerund meaning 'growing kinder' or 'softening (in disposition)'; от радости is an ablative/cause phrase 'from/because of joy.'
segments.p2-1-g669: А так is a colloquial pivot 'but rather like this' or 'instead, so' introducing the contrasted manner.
segments.p2-1-g670: Просто 'simply' modifies the negated infinitive посторониться ('to step aside / to get out of the way'); the whole phrase means 'simply not to step aside' (i.e. to remain in the line).
segments.p2-1-g671: Состукнуться с ним is a perfective reflexive verb meaning 'to ruffle/bump/knock together with him' or 'to collide lightly with him'; с + ним is the instrumental construction 'with him.'
segments.p2-1-g672: Не так is a short contrastive phrase 'not in such a way,' preparing the qualification in the next subordinate clause.
segments.p2-1-g673: Чтобы here is used in a negative comparative construction 'не так, чтобы...' meaning 'not so that ...' (i.e. not to the extent that); очень 'very' modifies больно, which here means 'painfully' — 'not so as to make it very painful.'
segments.p2-1-g674: А так resumes the contrastive 'but rather like this' formula to introduce the preferred manner of contact.
segments.p2-1-g675: Плечо о плечо is an idiomatic expression 'shoulder to shoulder' meaning side by side; о in this idiom expresses contact ('shoulder against shoulder').
segments.p2-1-g676: Ровно is an adverb 'exactly / precisely'; на here begins a measure expression 'на столько' ('by/for that much'). The phrase sets up a quantitative limitation.
segments.p2-1-g677: Сколько is a relative word 'as much as / how much'; определено is a short passive/impersonal form meaning 'is determined/defined'; приличием is instrumental ('by propriety') — together: 'as much as is determined by propriety.'
segments.p2-1-g678: Так что introduces a result/consequence clause ('so that'); на сколько is a measure expression 'by how much / as much as'; он меня стукнет uses стукнет (perfective future 3rd sing.) 'he will strike/knock me' as the protasis to the promised reciprocity.
segments.p2-1-g679: На столько и я его стукну is the apodosis: на столько ('to that extent') + и я (emphatic 'I too') + стукну (perfective future 1st sing.) 'I will strike him.' Его is the accusative object 'him.' The construction promises equal retaliation 'as much as he strikes me, so will I strike him.'
segments.p2-1-g798: Воротился is a past-tense verb (masculine singular) with the reflexive ending -ся, meaning 'returned' and placed before the pronoun for a slightly emphatic or colloquial word order. домой is a directional adverb 'home' and follows the verb. совершенно is an adverb modifying the participle отмщенный, which is a past passive participle used predicatively to describe the subject 'я' ('avenged'); за все is a prepositional complement to отмщенный meaning 'for everything'.
segments.p2-1-g799: Я был is a simple past copula (быть in past, masculine singular) meaning 'I was'. в восторге is a fixed prepositional phrase (в + noun in prepositional/locative case) meaning 'in rapture' or 'ecstatic', functioning as the predicate complement.
segments.p2-1-g800: This is a compound past predicate with the subject Я understood: торжествовал and пел are both past-tense verbs describing consecutive or simultaneous actions. и simply links the two verbs. итальянские арии is the direct object in accusative plural ('Italian arias'), placed after the verb пел.
segments.p2-1-g801: Разумеется is a discourse particle meaning 'of course' or 'naturally', placed sentence-initially and set off by a comma to mark the comment.
segments.p2-1-g802: я вам не буду описывать того is a negated future construction: буду (future of быть) + infinitive описывать forms the future imperfective 'I will (be) describing / will not describe'. вам is dative 'to you', and того is a genitive demonstrative pronoun referring forward to the subordinate clause ('that which' / 'what').
segments.p2-1-g803: что произошло со мной через три дня is the subordinate clause introduced by что ('what/that'). произошло is a past neuter verb form because что is an impersonal neuter pronoun; со мной ('with me') means 'happened to me', and через три дня is a temporal phrase 'after three days' / 'three days later'.
segments.p2-1-g804: если читали мою первую главу "Подполье", is a conditional clause with an omitted subject (вы is implied): читали is past tense plural used here like English present perfect/simple past ('if you read/have read'). мою первую главу is the accusative object ('my first chapter'), and Подполье is the title (here meaning the figurative 'Underground').
segments.p2-1-g805: то можете сами догадаться is the main clause: то ('then/so') links to the condition, можете is present tense 'you can / are able to', сами adds emphasis 'yourselves', and догадаться is the perfective infinitive 'to guess' meaning 'you can figure it out yourselves'.
segments.p2-1-g806: Офицера is accusative singular (the officer as the direct object), placed first for emphasis; потом is an adverb 'later'; куда + то form the indefinite 'куда-то' meaning 'somewhere' (то is the enclitic particle). Перевели is past tense, 3rd person plural (perfective) with an implicit agent ('they transferred'), so the clause is effectively passive in meaning though formed actively in Russian.
segments.p2-1-g807: This line uses an inverted time expression: лет (genitive plural of год) + уже + четырнадцать = 'already fourteen years' (more usual order would be 'уже четырнадцать лет'). Я is the subject; его is the accusative object 'him'; теперь here means 'by now' or 'since then'; не видал is a colloquial/older past form of видеть ('видеть') meaning 'have not seen' (standard modern would be не видел).
segments.p2-1-g808: Что-то is an indefinite/exclamatory combination (что + enclitic то) often meaning 'what' or 'something' in colloquial questions; the word order Что-то он теперь places the question word before the subject он ('he') and the adverb теперь ('now'), producing 'what of him now' or 'what is he like now'.
segments.p2-1-g809: мой голубчик is a vocative-like direct address using the possessive мой + diminutive голубчик ('my little dove' = 'my dear'), here likely ironic or familiar; the comma marks the pause before the address.
segments.p2-1-g810: Кого is the accusative of кто ('whom'), used in a short, elliptical question; давит is present tense, 3rd person singular of давить ('to press, to oppress'), and the subject ('he') is understood from the previous sentence (i.e. Кого (он) давит?). The question asks who is receiving his pressure/oppression.
segments.p2-1-g776: "И" is the simple coordinating conjunction 'and' beginning the sentence; "вдруг" is an adverb of suddenness ('suddenly') placed early for emphasis. "все" is the neuter pronoun 'everything' and is the grammatical subject; "закончилось" is a past‑tense, perfective, neuter singular reflexive verb (закончиться) meaning 'ended' or 'came to an end'. The phrase "как нельзя лучше" is an idiomatic comparative: "как" introduces the comparison, "нельзя" here functions idiomatically ('cannot' in the sense 'could not be') and combines with the comparative "лучше" to mean 'as well as possible' or 'couldn't have been better'.
segments.p2-1-g777: "Накануне ночью" is an adverbial time phrase 'on the previous night' (накануне + ночью). "я окончательно положил" uses положил in a slightly archaic/colloquial sense 'laid it down/decided' (i.e. resolved); "окончательно" is an adverb 'finally/definitively'. The two infinitives that follow, "не исполнять моего пагубного намерения" and "оставить втуне," are coordinated objects of that resolution: "не исполнять" is the negated infinitive 'not to carry out/perform' governing "моего пагубного намерения" (genitive possessive phrase 'my destructive intention'), and "оставить" is the second infinitive 'to leave' with "втуне" (an older adverb meaning 'in vain/abandoned' or 'let be'). The final part of the segment, "и с этою целью в последний раз я вышел на Невский," contains the purpose phrase "с этою целью" (instrumental: 'with this aim'), a time phrase "в последний раз" ('for the last time'), and the main past action "я вышел на Невский" ('I went out onto Nevsky' — на + name of the avenue used as a destination). Note "этою" is an archaic/demonstrative instrumental form.
segments.p2-1-g778: "чтобы" introduces a subordinate purpose clause ('in order that/in order to'); it governs the infinitive that follows. "только" is a limiter 'just/merely', and "так" 'so/that way' narrows the manner; together with the infinitive "посмотреть" (perfective infinitive 'to take a look' or 'to have a look') the clause means 'just to look that way' or 'simply to take a look' and expresses the stated motive for going out.
segments.p2-1-g779: This is a rhetorical question introduced by the colloquial "- как это" meaning roughly '— how on earth' or 'how could I'. "я оставлю" is the future tense (perfective оставлю) 'I will leave' used rhetorically to mean 'could I leave'; "все это втуне" is the direct object 'all this' plus the adverb "втуне" ('in vain' / 'let be'). The dash marks the speaker's astonished afterthought; the whole clause questions the possibility of abandoning everything.
segments.p2-1-g780: "Вдруг" is an adverb meaning 'suddenly' used as a sentence adverb to announce an abrupt action; the comma emphasizes the sudden interruption of the narrative.
segments.p2-1-g781: "в трех шагах" is a locative/measure expression meaning 'at a distance of three steps' (в + numeral + plural noun in prepositional/locative case here); "от врага моего" uses the preposition "от" with the genitive to mean 'from' or 'away from' and places the possessive "моего" after the noun in a slightly elevated/poetic word order ('my enemy').
segments.p2-1-g782: "я неожиданно решился" contains the subject and a past reflexive verb "решился" which here means 'summoned the courage' / 'decided to do it' (решиться + infinitive often means 'to dare/resolve to do something'); "неожиданно" is an adverb 'unexpectedly' modifying the verb. The verb is past tense, indicating a sudden inward decision.
segments.p2-1-g783: "зажмурил глаза" is a perfective past action 'I screwed/shut my eyes tight' (зажмурить глаза). The coordinating "и" plus the dash link this preparatory action to the immediate result: "мы плотно стукнулись плечо о плечо" — "мы" (we) + past plural reflexive verb "стукнулись" 'bumped/collided', "плотно" 'closely/tightly', and the idiomatic repetition "плечо о плечо" ('shoulder against shoulder' or 'shoulder to shoulder') uses the preposition "о" to indicate contact.
segments.p2-1-g784: "Я не уступил ни вершка" is an idiomatic negation meaning 'I didn't give up even a tiny bit' — "ни" intensifies the negation and "вершка" is an old small unit of length used figuratively. "и прошел мимо" is the coordinated past action 'and I passed by', with "мимо" indicating movement past someone. "совершенно" is an adverb 'completely/entirely', and "на равной ноге" is an idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning 'on equal footing' or 'as an equal' ("на" + adjective + noun in prepositional case describing manner or stance).
segments.p2-1-g785: Subject + adverbial phrase + coordinated verb phrase. «Он» is the 3rd-person subject. «даже и не оглянулся» is a negative past action with double emphatic particles «даже и» meaning 'didn't even', and «оглянулся» is perfective past 'looked back'. The second «и сделал вид» is a coordinated past action ('and made a show / pretended') using the set phrase «сделал вид (что)».
segments.p2-1-g786: A subordinate clause introduced by «что» ('that'). «не заметил» is a negative past tense (3rd person sing.) meaning 'did not notice' and functions as the content of the pretence signalled by «сделал вид».
segments.p2-1-g787: Contrastive clause started by «но» ('but'). Word order here («он только вид сделал») inverts the more neutral «он только сделал вид» to stress the noun «вид» ('a show, pretense'); «только» means 'only/merely'. Past tense «сделал» repeats the idea that the earlier 'not noticing' was just affectation.
segments.p2-1-g788: Short declarative clause: «я» subject + predicative adjective «уверен» (no copula in present) with the prepositional phrase «в этом» ('of that') marking the thing one is sure about; literal: 'I am sure in this.'
segments.p2-1-g789: Single clause with the fixed adverbial phrase «до сих пор» ('until now / to this day') followed by the predicative adjective «уверен» governed by the prepositional «в этом» ('of it'). The parts literally read 'I up to this time in this am sure.'
segments.p2-1-g790: A parenthetical/discourse particle «Разумеется» meaning 'of course' or 'naturally'; it comments on the following statement and is set off by a comma.
segments.p2-1-g791: Impersonal-experiential construction: dative «мне» + past neuter «досталось» expresses that something 'fell to me' or 'I got/received' (here colloquially 'I got the worse of it'); «больше» is a comparative adverb 'more' indicating greater extent.
segments.p2-1-g792: Simple past clause: subject «он» + copula «был» (past 'was') + comparative adjective «сильнее» ('stronger'); straightforward statement of contrast.
segments.p2-1-g793: Fixed idiomatic negation: «но не в том было дело» literally 'but the matter was not in that' — i.e. 'that wasn't the point.' «в том» is the prepositional pronoun 'in that', and «было дело» is the impersonal construction 'the matter was.'
segments.p2-1-g794: Topic-setting clause «Дело было в том» — «дело» ('the matter/point') is the subject, «было» is the past copula, and «в том» ('in that') prepares the content clause introduced next. This is a common formula meaning 'the point was that...'.
segments.p2-1-g795: Subordinate clause introduced by «что» ('that'). «я достиг цели» uses perfective past «достиг» to indicate a completed achievement ('I attained/reached the goal'), with «цели» as the direct object (goal/aim).
segments.p2-1-g796: Short coordinated phrase: past verb «поддержал» takes direct object «достоинство» — here 'поддержал достоинство' means 'maintained/preserved (his) dignity.' The verb is perfective past, indicating a completed action.
segments.p2-1-g797: Compound clause: «не уступил ни на шаг» is a negated past verb + intensifier «ни» meaning 'not even' and the phrase literally 'didn't yield an inch/step.' Then «и публично поставил себя с ним на равной социальной ноге» means 'and publicly put himself with him on an equal social footing' — «поставил себя» ('set/placed himself'), «с ним» uses the instrumental 'with him', and «на равной социальной ноге» is a figurative locative/prepositional expression 'on equal social foot/footing.'
segments.p2-1-g714: Эти (demonstrative) modifies the plural noun бобрики and is nominative plural; немецкие is an agreeing adjective (nominative plural) meaning 'German'. хоть и is a concessive connective 'although/even though' introducing the clause. очень скоро is an adverbial phrase 'very soon/very quickly'. занашиваются is 3rd person plural present (reflexive/inchoative) 'become worn'; и links the coordinated verb принимать (принимают) which is also 3rd person plural present and takes the direct object вид in the accusative (here form = nominative). мизернейший is a superlative/pejorative adjective modifying вид, meaning 'most paltry' or 'very meager'.
segments.p2-1-g715: но is the adversative particle 'but/however'. сначала is an adverb meaning 'at first' or 'initially' and modifies what follows.
segments.p2-1-g716: с here means 'from'/'when (worn) as' in the fixed phrase с обновки — 'when new' (обновки is genitive of обновка, 'new purchase/clothing'); the whole phrase is adverbial, specifying the initial condition.
segments.p2-1-g717: смотрят is 3rd person plural present of смотреть used here in the sense 'look/appear' (they present an appearance). даже и is an emphatic combination meaning 'even', and очень прилично is an adverbial predicate 'quite decently' or 'rather nicely' describing their appearance when new.
segments.p2-1-g718: а ведь combines contrastive a ('and/but') with ведь ('after all') to stress the point. мне is the dative 'to/for me' (indirect experiencer). только is the particle 'only/just'. для одного разу is a prepositional phrase 'for one time/for a single occasion' (разу is genitive of раз here meaning 'time/occasion'). и надо было is the impersonal past construction meaning 'was necessary' — together: 'I only needed it for one occasion.'
segments.p2-1-g719: Спросил is the perfective past singular of спросить 'asked'; the verb is fronted (verb‑subject order) for narrative effect. я is the nominative pronoun 'I'. цену is the accusative singular of цена, the direct object 'the price'.
segments.p2-1-g720: все-таки is a particle meaning 'after all / nevertheless' modifying the clause. было дорого is an impersonal past tense construction: было (past of быть) + predicate adverb дорого ('expensive'), equivalent to 'it was expensive.'
segments.p2-1-g721: По основательном рассуждении is an adverbial phrase 'after/according to thorough reasoning'; note the older/stylistic case form (основательном ... рассуждении) instead of the modern dative (основательному рассуждению). я решился is past reflexive 'I decided / I resolved' (решиться + infinitive), and продать is the infinitive 'to sell' that completes решился. мой енотовый воротник is the direct object in accusative (воротник is inanimate masculine so form equals nominative) meaning 'my raccoon (fur) collar.'
segments.p2-1-g722: Недостающую is the adjective/participle 'lacking/missing' in accusative feminine singular agreeing with сумму. же is an emphatic particle adding contrast. и весьма для меня значительную is a nested modifier: и (linking 'and/also'), весьма ('very'), для меня ('for me' — dative expressing to whom it was significant) and значительную ('considerable') — all agree with сумму (accusative). решился выпросить is 'decided to beg/obtain by entreaty' (решился + infinitive выпросить). взаймы is an adverb/phrase 'on loan'. у + name marks the source ('from Anton Antonovich Setochkin').
segments.p2-1-g723: моего is the possessive pronoun in genitive masculine singular (agreeing with столоначальника) because it's in apposition to the preceding genitive name (у Антона ...). столоначальника is genitive singular of столоначальник 'head clerk/chief steward', describing Setochkin.
segments.p2-1-g724: человека is genitive singular (continuing the appositive phrase) 'a person / man'. смиренного is an adjective/participle in genitive singular agreeing with человека, meaning 'meek' or 'humble'.
segments.p2-1-g725: но is 'but/however' introducing contrast. серьезного и положительного are adjectives in genitive singular modifying человека: серьезного 'serious/earnest' and положительного 'positive/decisive/definite' (here 'положительного' means 'decisive/definite' rather than 'positive' in the modern colloquial sense).
segments.p2-1-g726: никому (dative) 'to no one' + не дававшего (past active participle, genitive) forms a relative participle phrase modifying человека: 'who had not given (did not lend)'. взаймы is 'on loan' and денег is genitive plural 'money' (the thing not given). Together: 'who lent money to no one.'
segments.p2-1-g727: но 'but'; которому is the dative relative pronoun 'to whom' referring back to Setochkin. я был is past 'I was'; когда-то (kогда + то) is the adverbial 'once / at one time' (когда-то = once). The clause begins the explanation 'but to whom I had once been ...'.
segments.p2-1-g728: при + noun in prepositional (при вступлении) is a temporal/ circumstantial preposition meaning 'upon' or 'at the time of'. вступлении is the noun 'entry/assumption' (here 'assuming a post'), в is the preposition 'into/to', and должность is the noun 'post/position'. Together: 'upon taking up the post.'
segments.p2-1-g729: особенно is an adverb 'especially'. рекомендован is a short passive participle/past passive form 'recommended' — here meaning 'I had been recommended'. определившим is an active past participle in instrumental (literally 'by the one who had appointed/assigned'), and меня is its object ('who had assigned me'); на службу is the complement 'to the service' (на + accusative meaning 'to' the service/office). значительным лицом is instrumental 'by a person of importance' (agent of the recommendation). The whole phrase means 'had been especially recommended by a person of importance who had assigned me to the service.'
segments.p2-1-g762: Subject pronoun Я (I) with the adverb даже (even) modifying the verb; молитвы is the direct object (accusative plural) of читать here, and читал is the past tense, masculine, imperfective verb ‘read/recite,’ indicating a repeated or habitual/ongoing act (I was saying/reciting prayers).
segments.p2-1-g763: Подходя is a present adverbial participle (деепричастие) from подходить meaning ‘approaching,’ expressing an action simultaneous with the main clause; к нему is the preposition + dative phrase ‘toward him’ indicating direction/target of that approach.
segments.p2-1-g764: Чтоб (colloquial for чтобы) introduces a purpose/wish clause (‘so that’); бог is the nominative subject; вселил is the past form used after чтоб to express an optative/subjunctive sense (‘would instill’), from a perfective verb вселить ‘instill/move into’; в меня решимость literally orders the constituents with the preposition в + accusative (into me) followed by the thing instilled (решимость, accusative) — ‘instill determination into me.’
segments.p2-1-g765: Один раз = 'once' (one occasion).
